## TOPIC 00001: ABC to Alpha-Beta-Gamma Transform VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`

You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. Details Example Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI. ABC to

### ABC to Alpha-Beta-Gamma Transform VI

**Owning Palette:** Transform VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Transforms signals from the ABC reference frame to the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

ABC to Alpha-Beta-Gamma Transform (Magnitude Invariant)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/ABC_to_Alpha-Beta-Gamma_Transform__Magnitude_Invariant.gif']

|  | A specifies the signal of A. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | B specifies the signal of B. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | C specifies the signal of C. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | alpha returns the signal of alpha. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |

|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | gamma returns the signal of gamma. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |

ABC to Alpha-Beta-Gamma Transform Details

The equations for transforming voltage also apply to current. This section takes the voltage equations for an example.

This VI uses the following equations to transform signals from the ABC reference frame to the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame:
Magnitude Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_abc_to_alpha_beta_magnitude_invariant.gif']

where

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>α</sub>, v<sub>β</sub>, v<sub>γ</sub>] represents signals in the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame
Power Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_abc_to_alpha_beta_power_invariant.gif']

where

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>α</sub>, v<sub>β</sub>, v<sub>γ</sub>] represents signals in the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame


Example

Refer to the Transforms VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\EV Power Test\Transforms directory for an example of using the ABC to Alpha-Beta-Gamma Transform VI.

### ABC to DQZ Transform VI

**Owning Palette:** Transform VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Transforms signals from the ABC reference frame to the DQZ reference frame. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

ABC to DQZ Transform (Magnitude Invariant)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/ABC_to_DQZ_Transform__Magnitude_Invariant.gif']

|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

ABC to DQZ Transform Details

The equations for transforming voltage also apply to current. This section takes the voltage equations for an example.

This VI uses the following equations to transform signals from the ABC reference frame to the DQZ reference frame:
Magnitude Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_abc_to_dqz_magnitude_invariant.gif']

where

θ represents the instantaneous angle between d-axis and a-axis in radians

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents the signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>d</sub>, v<sub>q</sub>, v<sub>z</sub>] represents the signals in the DQZ reference frame
Power Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_abc_to_dqz_power_invariant.gif']

where

θ represents the instantaneous angle between d-axis and a-axis in radians

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents the signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>d</sub>, v<sub>q</sub>, v<sub>z</sub>] represents the signals in the DQZ reference frame

## TOPIC 00003: AC DC Coupling VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `ac-dc-coupling-vi.html`

- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Utility VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Eliminates the DC component of voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. Details voltage waveforms in specifies voltage wav

### AC DC Coupling VI

**Owning Palette:** Utility VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Eliminates the DC component of voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/AC_DC_Coupling.gif']

|  | voltage waveforms in specifies voltage waveforms. Each array element represents one phase. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveforms in specifies current waveforms. Each array element represents one phase. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | coupling specifies the type of coupling. 0DC coupling (default)1AC coupling |
| 0 | DC coupling (default) |
| 1 | AC coupling |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | voltage waveforms out returns voltage waveforms after coupling. Each array element represents one phase. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveforms out returns current waveforms after coupling. Each array element represents one phase. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

AC DC Coupling Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations

## TOPIC 00004: Advanced VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `advanced-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/advanced-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Measurement VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Advanced VIs to perform common electrical power measurement using integral-cycle volt

### Advanced VIs

**Owning Palette:** Power Measurement VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Advanced VIs to perform common electrical power measurement using integral-cycle voltage and current signals and to perform harmonic analysis using resampled integral-cycle voltage and current signals.

To perform common electrical power measurement using integral-cycle voltage and current signals, first use the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI and then the To Integral Cycles VI to get integral number of cycles of the voltage and current signals. You then use the VIs on the Power Measurement palette to measure common electrical power parameters.

To perform harmonic analysis using resampled integral-cycle voltage and current signals, first use the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI, the Resampling for Power Measurement VI, and then the Build Data Blocks VI to get integral-cycle data blocks of the resampled voltage and current signals. You then use the VIs on the Harmonics palette to perform harmonic analysis.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) | Calculates the zero crossing times and the average fundamental frequency of a reference signal. |
| To Integral Cycles | Divides the voltage and current signals into integral-cycle data for power measurement, according to the trigger times that you specify. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Harmonics VIs | Use the Harmonics VIs to preprocess the voltage and current signals and then to perform harmonic analysis. |

Parent topic:

Power Measurement VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=advanced-vis2.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00005: Advanced VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `advanced-vis2.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/advanced-vis2.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: COMTRADE VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Advanced VIs to perform advanced file I/O operations. The read VIs support Windows only when y

### Advanced VIs

**Owning Palette:** COMTRADE VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Note

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| COMTRADE List Private Contents | Lists all the section names of the specified manufacturer name. If manufacturer name is empty, this VI returns all the manufacturer names and section names. You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .hdr file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Read Header | Read header information from the specified .hdr file. You must set open mode to Open (read only) and set include header file? to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .hdr file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Read Private Inf | Reads information from the private sections of the optionally specified .inf file. You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Read Public Inf | Reads information from the public sections of the optionally specified .inf file. You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Write Header | Writes data to the optionally specified .hdr file. You must set include header file? to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .hdr file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Write Private Inf | Writes data to the private sections of the optionally specified .inf file. If the entry name you specify already exists, this VI replaces the existing entry. You must set include information file? to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Write Public Inf | Writes data to the public sections of the optionally specified .inf file. You can set include configuration information? to TRUE to write the content of the specified .cfg file to this .inf file. You must set include information file? to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |

Parent topic:

COMTRADE VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=aggregation-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00006: Aggregation VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `aggregation-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/aggregation-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Quality VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Aggregation VIs to aggregate data at a specific interval according to Class S of IEC 6100

### Aggregation VIs

**Owning Palette:** Power Quality VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Aggregation VIs to aggregate data at a specific interval according to Class S of IEC 61000-4-30:2008.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Custom Aggregation | Calculates the aggregated minimum, maximum, and averaged or RMS value of an input signal for a specified aggregation interval, according to Class S in IEC 61000-4-30:2008. There is no overlap between aggregation intervals. This VI automatically restarts accumulation at the beginning of the next time interval after the previous interval expires. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems except the DBL instance. Use the DBL instance to measure one channel on one line at one time. |
| EN50160 Aggregation | Calculates aggregated values of power quality parameters for specific intervals. You use the aggregated values for further evaluations according to EN 50160:2007. The aggregation methods are compliant with Class S in IEC 61000-4-30:2008. The aggregation is based on a 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. There is no overlap between aggregation intervals. This VI automatically restarts accumulation at the beginning of the next 10/12-cycle time interval after the previous interval expires. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. |

Parent topic:

Power Quality VIs

### Alpha-Beta-Gamma to ABC Transform VI

**Owning Palette:** Transform VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Transforms signals from the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame to the ABC reference frame. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Alpha-Beta-Gamma to ABC Transform (Magnitude Invariant)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Alpha-Beta-Gamma_to_ABC_Transform__Magnitude_Invariant.gif']

|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |

|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | beta specifies the signal of beta. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

Alpha-Beta-Gamma to ABC Transform Details

The equations for transforming voltage also apply to current. This section takes the voltage equations for an example.

This VI uses the following equations to transform signals from the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame to the ABC reference frame:
Magnitude Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_alpha_beta_to_abc_magnitude_invariant.gif']

where

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents the signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>α</sub>, v<sub>β</sub>, v<sub>γ</sub>] represents the signals in the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame
Power Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_alpha_beta_to_abc_power_invariant.gif']

where

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents the signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>α</sub>, v<sub>β</sub>, v<sub>γ</sub>] represents the signals in the alpha-beta-gamma reference frame

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=battery-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00009: Battery VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `battery-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/battery-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: EV Power Test VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Battery VIs to perform measurements on the battery pack of an electric vehicle. Palette O

### Battery VIs

**Owning Palette:** EV Power Test VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Battery VIs to perform measurements on the battery pack of an electric vehicle.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| DC Power | Calculates the DC power of a battery pack. |
| Integrate Ampere to Coulomb | Measures the electrical charge of a battery pack in a specific amount of time. |
| Integrate Watt to Joule | Measures the electrical energy of a battery pack in a specific amount of time. |

Parent topic:

EV Power Test VIs

## TOPIC 00010: Broken Rotor Bar Harmonics VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `broken-rotor-bar-harmonics-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/broken-rotor-bar-harmonics-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Motor VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the sideband harmonics to detect broken rotor bars. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. Details Example Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI. N Motors fundamental frequency speci

### Broken Rotor Bar Harmonics VI

**Owning Palette:** Motor VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the sideband harmonics to detect broken rotor bars. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

N Motors

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Broken_Rotor_Bar_Harmonics_N_Motors.gif']

|  | fundamental frequency specifies the instantaneous fundamental frequency, in hertz, of the motor signal. Use the corresponding upstream Electrical Power VIs, such as the Frequency VI to measure the fundamental frequency. The value must be greater than zero. |
| --- | --- |
|  | spectrum information specifies spectrum information for multiple motors. spectra specifies the spectra for multiple motors. The array size of spectra must be equal to the array size of slip. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude specifies the magnitude, in decibels, of the spectrum. demodulated? specifies whether the values of the spectra input are demodulated. The default is FALSE. |
|  | spectra specifies the spectra for multiple motors. The array size of spectra must be equal to the array size of slip. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude specifies the magnitude, in decibels, of the spectrum. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude, in decibels, of the spectrum. |
|  | demodulated? specifies whether the values of the spectra input are demodulated. The default is FALSE. |
|  | slip specifies the slip value that the VI uses to calculate the sidebands. |
|  | max sideband order specifies the maximum sideband order to observe. The default is 10. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | upper sidebands returns the upper sideband harmonics. Each array element represents the upper sidebands of a motor. The order value of max sideband order coerces the order value of upper sidebands if the order value of upper sidebands is greater than that of max sideband order. sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is greater than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. The value of sideband frequency is greater than zero if the spectrum is demodulated. sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference is 1 Ampere if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is demodulated. The decibel reference is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is greater than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. The value of sideband frequency is greater than zero if the spectrum is demodulated. |
|  | sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference is 1 Ampere if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is demodulated. The decibel reference is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | lower sidebands returns the lower sideband harmonics. Each array element represents the lower sidebands of a motor. This VI does not return any lower sideband harmonics if demodulated? is TRUE. sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is less than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference of sideband magnitude is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is less than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. |
|  | sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference of sideband magnitude is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | max sideband magnitude returns the maximum magnitude, in decibels, of all sidebands that you observe. The decibel reference is 1 Ampere if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is demodulated. The decibel reference is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

1 Motor

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Broken_Rotor_Bar_Harmonics_1_Motor.gif']

|  | fundamental frequency specifies the instantaneous fundamental frequency, in hertz, of the motor signal. Use the corresponding upstream Electrical Power VIs, such as the Frequency VI to measure the fundamental frequency. The value must be greater than zero. |
| --- | --- |
|  | spectrum information specifies spectrum information for the motor. spectrum specifies the spectrum for the motor. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude specifies the magnitude, in decibels, of the spectrum. demodulated? specifies whether the values of the spectrum input are demodulated. The default is FALSE. |
|  | spectrum specifies the spectrum for the motor. f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. magnitude specifies the magnitude, in decibels, of the spectrum. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in hertz, of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitude specifies the magnitude, in decibels, of the spectrum. |
|  | demodulated? specifies whether the values of the spectrum input are demodulated. The default is FALSE. |
|  | slip specifies the slip value that the VI uses to calculate the sidebands. |
|  | max sideband order specifies the maximum sideband order to observe. The default is 10. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | upper sidebands returns the upper sideband harmonics. The order of max sideband order coerces the order of upper sidebands if the order value of upper sidebands is greater than that of max sideband order. sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is greater than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. The value of sideband frequency is greater than zero if the spectrum is demodulated. sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference is 1 Ampere if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is demodulated. The decibel reference is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is greater than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. The value of sideband frequency is greater than zero if the spectrum is demodulated. |
|  | sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference is 1 Ampere if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is demodulated. The decibel reference is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | lower sidebands returns the lower sideband harmonics. Each array element represents the lower sidebands of a motor. This VI does not return any lower sideband harmonics if demodulated? is TRUE. sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is less than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference of sideband magnitude is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | sideband frequency returns the frequencies, in hertz, of the sideband harmonics. The value of sideband frequency is less than that of fundamental frequency if the spectrum is not demodulated. |
|  | sideband magnitude returns the magnitudes, in decibels, of the sideband harmonics. The decibel reference of sideband magnitude is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | max sideband magnitude returns the maximum magnitude, in decibels, of all sidebands that you observe. The decibel reference is 1 Ampere if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is demodulated. The decibel reference is the fundamental component magnitude value if the spectrum of sideband magnitude is not demodulated. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Broken Rotor Bar Harmonics Details

Use the Speed Estimation VI and the MCSA Spectra VI to get the speed and spectrum information before calculating the sideband harmonics.

Use the **upper sidebands** and **lower sidebands** values of this VI to monitor the rotor status. Compare the values at different time points. The values remain the same under an ideal condition. If the rotors are running well, the values have small differences as time passes. If the values vary significantly, check the rotors to ensure that the rotors are not broken.


Example

Refer to the Motor Monitoring.lvproj in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Motor Monitoring directory for an example of using the Broken Rotor Bar Harmonics VI.

Parent topic:

Motor VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=build-configuration-frame-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00011: Build Configuration Frame VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `build-configuration-frame-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/build-configuration-frame-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: C37.118 Frame VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Builds a configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Examples CFG-3 PMU location specifies the location information of the phasor measurement unit (PMU) as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. latitude specifies the l

### Build Configuration Frame VI

**Owning Palette:** C37.118 Frame VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Builds a configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Build_Configuration_Frame.gif']

|  | CFG-3 PMU location specifies the location information of the phasor measurement unit (PMU) as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. latitude specifies the latitude of the PMU in degrees. latitude corresponds to the PMU_LAT field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is Inf. longitude specifies the longitude of the PMU in degrees. longitude corresponds to the PMU_LON field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is Inf. elevation specifies the elevation of the PMU in meters. elevation corresponds to the PMU_ELEV field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is Inf. |
| --- | --- |
|  | latitude specifies the latitude of the PMU in degrees. latitude corresponds to the PMU_LAT field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is Inf. |
|  | longitude specifies the longitude of the PMU in degrees. longitude corresponds to the PMU_LON field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is Inf. |
|  | elevation specifies the elevation of the PMU in meters. elevation corresponds to the PMU_ELEV field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is Inf. |
|  | CFG-3 algorithm factors specifies the window length and group delay of the PMU as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. window length specifies the window length of the PMU in microseconds. window length corresponds to the WINDOW field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. window length includes all filters and estimation windows in effect. group delay specifies the group delay of the PMU in microseconds. group delay corresponds to the GRP_DLY field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. group delay includes all filters and estimation windows in effect. |
|  | window length specifies the window length of the PMU in microseconds. window length corresponds to the WINDOW field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. window length includes all filters and estimation windows in effect. |
|  | group delay specifies the group delay of the PMU in microseconds. group delay corresponds to the GRP_DLY field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. group delay includes all filters and estimation windows in effect. |
|  | configuration frame type specifies the type of the configuration frame. 0CFG-1 (default)Uses the measurement configuration of CFG-1 that you specify in the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI.1CFG-2Uses the measurement configuration of CFG-2 that you specify in the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI.2CFG-3Uses the measurement configuration of CFG-3 that you specify in the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI. |
| 0 | CFG-1 (default)Uses the measurement configuration of CFG-1 that you specify in the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI. |
| 1 | CFG-2Uses the measurement configuration of CFG-2 that you specify in the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI. |
| 2 | CFG-3Uses the measurement configuration of CFG-3 that you specify in the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI. |
|  | configuration change count specifies the number of changes in the configuration frame. configuration change count corresponds to the CFGCNT field in the configuration frames and increments each time a change occurs in the phasor measurement unit (PMU) configuration as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is 0. The values for configuration change count are the same in CFG-1, CFG-2, and CFG-3 for equivalent messages. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the PMU system. Use the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | configuration frame returns the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
|  | system info out returns the basic configuration of the PMU system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Build Configuration Frame VI:

- Synchrophasor Measurement and Communication (TCP) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Synchrophasor
- Synchrophasor Measurement and Communication (UDP) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Synchrophasor

Parent topic:

C37.118 Frame VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=build-data-blocks-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00012: Build Data Blocks VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `build-data-blocks-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/build-data-blocks-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Builds even-angle-spaced voltage and current signals into data blocks. Use the Resampling for Power Measurement VI to resample even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals before using this VI. Example re

### Build Data Blocks VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Builds even-angle-spaced voltage and current signals into data blocks.

Use the Resampling for Power Measurement VI to resample even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals before using this VI.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/PM_Build_Data_Blocks.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the building process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the building process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | resampled data specifies the resampled signal for building data blocks. Wire the resampled data output of the Resampling for Power Measurement VI to the resampled data input of this VI. times specifies the time of the resampled signal in seconds. resampled signal specifies the resampled voltage and current signals. |
|  | times specifies the time of the resampled signal in seconds. |
|  | resampled signal specifies the resampled voltage and current signals. |
|  | cycles per block specifies the number of cycles to build in each data block. The default is 10. |
|  | samples per cycle specifies the number of samples per cycle in the resampled signals. The default is 192. You must set samples per cycle to be greater than or equal to (2 * maximum harmonic order) + 1. If you set samples per cycle too low, the measurement result might not be accurate enough. A greater samples per cycle value requires more real-time computation resources to process the resampled signal. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | data blocks returns the data blocks that this VI builds from the resampled data. times returns the time of the resampled signal in each data block in seconds. resampled signal returns the resampled voltage and current signal that this VI builds, based on cycles per block and samples per cycle. |
|  | times returns the time of the resampled signal in each data block in seconds. |
|  | resampled signal returns the resampled voltage and current signal that this VI builds, based on cycles per block and samples per cycle. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Advanced Power Measurement - Harmonics (DAQmx) VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement directory for an example of using the Build Data Blocks VI.

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=build-data-blocks-vi2.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00013: Build Data Blocks VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `build-data-blocks-vi2.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/build-data-blocks-vi2.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Preprocessing VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Builds even-angle-spaced signals into data blocks. You must use the Resampling for Power Quality VI to resample even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals. By default, this VI builds data blocks acc

### Build Data Blocks VI

**Owning Palette:** Preprocessing VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Builds even-angle-spaced signals into data blocks. You must use the Resampling for Power Quality VI to resample even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals. By default, this VI builds data blocks according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. You can use a customized setting when your application does not follow IEC 61000-4-30:2008.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Build_Data_Blocks.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the building process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the building process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data block settings specifies the number of cycles to build in each data block according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008 or a customized setting. If you do not wire this input, this VI builds data blocks according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. mode specifies the mode in which you build the data block. 0IEC 61000-4-30 (default)Sets the block size as 10 cycles for a 50 Hz electrical power system and 12 cycles for a 60 Hz electrical power system, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008.1CustomUses the value that you specify in cycles per block. Set mode to Custom when your application does not follow IEC 61000-4-30:2008. cycles per block specifies the number of cycles to build in each data block. cycles per block is valid only when you set mode to Custom. |
|  | mode specifies the mode in which you build the data block. 0IEC 61000-4-30 (default)Sets the block size as 10 cycles for a 50 Hz electrical power system and 12 cycles for a 60 Hz electrical power system, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008.1CustomUses the value that you specify in cycles per block. Set mode to Custom when your application does not follow IEC 61000-4-30:2008. |
| 0 | IEC 61000-4-30 (default)Sets the block size as 10 cycles for a 50 Hz electrical power system and 12 cycles for a 60 Hz electrical power system, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. |
| 1 | CustomUses the value that you specify in cycles per block. Set mode to Custom when your application does not follow IEC 61000-4-30:2008. |
|  | cycles per block specifies the number of cycles to build in each data block. cycles per block is valid only when you set mode to Custom. |
|  | resampled data specifies the resampled data that you acquire from the Resampling for Power Quality VI. resampled signal specifies the resampled even-angle-spaced signal. sample rate specifies the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of the resampled signal. timestamp specifies the timestamp of the end of the resampled signal. |
|  | resampled signal specifies the resampled even-angle-spaced signal. |
|  | sample rate specifies the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of the resampled signal. |
|  | timestamp specifies the timestamp of the end of the resampled signal. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | data blocks returns the data blocks that this VI builds from the resampled data. resampled signal returns the resampled signal that this VI builds from the input data based on the data block settings you specify. sample rate returns the instantaneous sample rate of each data block, in hertz. timestamp returns the timestamp of the end of the data block. |
|  | resampled signal returns the resampled signal that this VI builds from the input data based on the data block settings you specify. |
|  | sample rate returns the instantaneous sample rate of each data block, in hertz. |
|  | timestamp returns the timestamp of the end of the data block. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Build Data Blocks Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Build Data Blocks VI:

- labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)\Power Quality Applications (DAQmx).lvproj
- labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Calibration (DAQmx)\Calibration for Power Quality Measurement (DAQmx).lvproj

Preprocessing VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=build-data-frame-std-2011-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00014: Build Data Frame (Std 2011) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `build-data-frame-std-2011-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/build-data-frame-std-2011-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: C37.118 Frame VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Builds a data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Examples measurement data specifies the transmitting or reporting data for building the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. data error specifies the data erro

### Build Data Frame (Std 2011) VI

**Owning Palette:** C37.118 Frame VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Builds a data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Build_Data_Frame.gif']

|  | measurement data specifies the transmitting or reporting data for building the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. data error specifies the data error in the phasor measurement unit (PMU). data error corresponds to Bits 15-14 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is 00. You can use data error to set Bits 15-14 of the STAT field as follows. 00No error. 01PMU error. No information about the data is available. 10PMU is in test mode or absent data tags have been inserted. Do not use the data when this data error returns. 11PMU error. Do not use the data when this data error returns. PMU sync? specifies whether the PMU synchronizes with a UTC traceable timing source. The default is FALSE. PMU sync? corresponds to Bit 13 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. When PMU sync? is TRUE, the value of Bit 13 is 0. When PMU sync? is FALSE, the value of Bit 13 is 1. data sorting specifies whether to sort the reporting data by the timestamp or by the arrival time. data sorting corresponds to Bit 12 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0by timestamp (default)Sorts data by the timestamp, which corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 12.1by arrivalSorts data by the arrival time, which corresponds to the value 1 for Bit 12. PMU trigger detected? specifies whether LabVIEW detects a PMU trigger. The default is FALSE. PMU trigger detected? corresponds to Bit 11 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. When PMU trigger detected? is TRUE, the value of Bit 11 is 1. When PMU trigger detected? is FALSE, the value of Bit 11 is 0. configuration change? specifies whether PMU configuration will change in the reporting data. The default is FALSE. configuration change? corresponds to Bit 10 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. When configuration change? is TRUE, the value of Bit 10 is 1. When configuration change? is FALSE, the value of Bit 10 is 0. data modified? specifies whether a post-processing device, such as a PDC, modifies the phasor data. data modified? corresponds to Bits 9 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default value is FALSE. If a post-processing device modifies the phasor data, data modified? turns to TRUE. PMU time quality specifies the maximum time uncertainty that the timing source and the PMU measuring processing introduce. PMU time quality corresponds to Bits 8-6 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is 000. You can use PMU time quality to set Bits 8-6 of the STAT field as follows. 111Estimated maximum time error is greater than 10 ms or the time error is unknown. 110Estimated maximum time error is less than 10 ms. 101Estimated maximum time error is less than 1 ms. 100Estimated maximum time error is less than 100 µs. 011Estimated maximum time error is less than 10 µs. 010Estimated maximum time error is less than 1 µs. 001Estimated maximum time error is less than 100 ns. 000Not used. trigger reason specifies the cause of a trigger. trigger reason corresponds to Bits 3-0 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can use trigger reason to set Bits 3-0 of the STAT field for the following causes of the trigger. 1111–1000Available for user definition 0111Digital 0101df/dt high 0011Phase-angle diff 0001Magnitude low 0110Reserved 0100Frequency high/low 0010Magnitude high 0000Manual timestamp specifies the current reporting timestamp. time quality flag specifies the quality of the time being reported and the leap second status. time quality flag corresponds to Bits 31-24 of the FRACSEC field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The following list indicates the meanings of Bits 31-24. Bit 31Reserved. Bit 30Leap Second Direction. 0 indicates to add the leap second and 1 indicates to delete the leap second. Bit 29Leap Second Occurred. Set to 1 in the first second after the leap second occurs and remains set for 24 hours. Bit 28Leap Second Pending. Set to 1 for greater than or equal to 1 second and less than or equal to 60 seconds before a leap second occurs. Set to 0 in the second after the leap second occurs. Bits 27-24Message Time Quality indicator code. The following list indicates the meanings of Bits 27-24 for the Message Time Quality. 1111Fault. Clock failure. Time is not reliable. 1011Estimated maximum time error is less than 10 s of UTC. 1010Estimated maximum time error is less than 1 s of UTC. 1001Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–1 s of UTC. 1000Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–2 s of UTC. 0111Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–3 s of UTC. 0110Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–4 s of UTC. 0101Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–5 s of UTC. 0100Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–6 s of UTC. 0011Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–7 s of UTC. 0010Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–8 s of UTC. 0001Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–9 s of UTC. 0000Normal operation. Clock locked to UTC traceable source. unlocked seconds specifies the time since the GPS signal was unlocked. unlocked seconds corresponds to Bits 5-4 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can use unlocked seconds to set Bits 5-4 of the STAT field as follows. 00Locked or unlocked less than 10 s 01Unlocked 10 s or longer but less than 100 s 10Unlocked 100 s or longer but less than 1000 s 11Unlocked 1000 s or more phasors specifies the phasor data of the phasor channels at the reporting timestamp as complex numbers. frequency specifies the frequency at the reporting timestamp. df/dt specifies the rate of change of frequency at the reporting timestamp. analogs specifies user-defined analog data at the reporting timestamp, such as sampled data, control signal, and transducer values. digitals specifies user-defined digital data at the reporting timestamp, such as bitmapped status or flag. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data error specifies the data error in the phasor measurement unit (PMU). data error corresponds to Bits 15-14 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is 00. You can use data error to set Bits 15-14 of the STAT field as follows. 00No error. 01PMU error. No information about the data is available. 10PMU is in test mode or absent data tags have been inserted. Do not use the data when this data error returns. 11PMU error. Do not use the data when this data error returns. |
|  | PMU sync? specifies whether the PMU synchronizes with a UTC traceable timing source. The default is FALSE. PMU sync? corresponds to Bit 13 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. When PMU sync? is TRUE, the value of Bit 13 is 0. When PMU sync? is FALSE, the value of Bit 13 is 1. |
|  | data sorting specifies whether to sort the reporting data by the timestamp or by the arrival time. data sorting corresponds to Bit 12 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0by timestamp (default)Sorts data by the timestamp, which corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 12.1by arrivalSorts data by the arrival time, which corresponds to the value 1 for Bit 12. |
| 0 | by timestamp (default)Sorts data by the timestamp, which corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 12. |
| 1 | by arrivalSorts data by the arrival time, which corresponds to the value 1 for Bit 12. |
|  | PMU trigger detected? specifies whether LabVIEW detects a PMU trigger. The default is FALSE. PMU trigger detected? corresponds to Bit 11 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. When PMU trigger detected? is TRUE, the value of Bit 11 is 1. When PMU trigger detected? is FALSE, the value of Bit 11 is 0. |
|  | configuration change? specifies whether PMU configuration will change in the reporting data. The default is FALSE. configuration change? corresponds to Bit 10 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. When configuration change? is TRUE, the value of Bit 10 is 1. When configuration change? is FALSE, the value of Bit 10 is 0. |
|  | data modified? specifies whether a post-processing device, such as a PDC, modifies the phasor data. data modified? corresponds to Bits 9 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default value is FALSE. If a post-processing device modifies the phasor data, data modified? turns to TRUE. |
|  | PMU time quality specifies the maximum time uncertainty that the timing source and the PMU measuring processing introduce. PMU time quality corresponds to Bits 8-6 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The default is 000. You can use PMU time quality to set Bits 8-6 of the STAT field as follows. 111Estimated maximum time error is greater than 10 ms or the time error is unknown. 110Estimated maximum time error is less than 10 ms. 101Estimated maximum time error is less than 1 ms. 100Estimated maximum time error is less than 100 µs. 011Estimated maximum time error is less than 10 µs. 010Estimated maximum time error is less than 1 µs. 001Estimated maximum time error is less than 100 ns. 000Not used. |
|  | trigger reason specifies the cause of a trigger. trigger reason corresponds to Bits 3-0 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can use trigger reason to set Bits 3-0 of the STAT field for the following causes of the trigger. 1111–1000Available for user definition 0111Digital 0101df/dt high 0011Phase-angle diff 0001Magnitude low 0110Reserved 0100Frequency high/low 0010Magnitude high 0000Manual |
|  | timestamp specifies the current reporting timestamp. |
|  | time quality flag specifies the quality of the time being reported and the leap second status. time quality flag corresponds to Bits 31-24 of the FRACSEC field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The following list indicates the meanings of Bits 31-24. Bit 31Reserved. Bit 30Leap Second Direction. 0 indicates to add the leap second and 1 indicates to delete the leap second. Bit 29Leap Second Occurred. Set to 1 in the first second after the leap second occurs and remains set for 24 hours. Bit 28Leap Second Pending. Set to 1 for greater than or equal to 1 second and less than or equal to 60 seconds before a leap second occurs. Set to 0 in the second after the leap second occurs. Bits 27-24Message Time Quality indicator code. The following list indicates the meanings of Bits 27-24 for the Message Time Quality. 1111Fault. Clock failure. Time is not reliable. 1011Estimated maximum time error is less than 10 s of UTC. 1010Estimated maximum time error is less than 1 s of UTC. 1001Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–1 s of UTC. 1000Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–2 s of UTC. 0111Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–3 s of UTC. 0110Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–4 s of UTC. 0101Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–5 s of UTC. 0100Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–6 s of UTC. 0011Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–7 s of UTC. 0010Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–8 s of UTC. 0001Estimated maximum time error is less than 10–9 s of UTC. 0000Normal operation. Clock locked to UTC traceable source. |
|  | unlocked seconds specifies the time since the GPS signal was unlocked. unlocked seconds corresponds to Bits 5-4 of the STAT field in the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can use unlocked seconds to set Bits 5-4 of the STAT field as follows. 00Locked or unlocked less than 10 s 01Unlocked 10 s or longer but less than 100 s 10Unlocked 100 s or longer but less than 1000 s 11Unlocked 1000 s or more |
|  | phasors specifies the phasor data of the phasor channels at the reporting timestamp as complex numbers. |
|  | frequency specifies the frequency at the reporting timestamp. |
|  | df/dt specifies the rate of change of frequency at the reporting timestamp. |
|  | analogs specifies user-defined analog data at the reporting timestamp, such as sampled data, control signal, and transducer values. |
|  | digitals specifies user-defined digital data at the reporting timestamp, such as bitmapped status or flag. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the PMU system. Use the Initialize System (Std 2011) VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | data frame returns the data frame that this VI builds as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
|  | system info out returns the basic configuration of the PMU system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Build Data Frame (Std 2011) VI:

- Synchrophasor Measurement and Communication (TCP) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Synchrophasor
- Synchrophasor Measurement and Communication (UDP) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Synchrophasor

## TOPIC 00016: C37.118 Frame VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `c37-118-frame-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/c37-118-frame-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Synchrophasor VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the C37.118 Frame VIs to build and parse synchrophasor message frames for real-time communica

### C37.118 Frame VIs

**Owning Palette:** Synchrophasor VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the C37.118 Frame VIs to build and parse synchrophasor message frames for real-time communication of synchrophasor measurement data between phasor measurement units (PMUs) and phasor data concentrators (PDCs) as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Build Configuration Frame | Builds a configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
| Build Data Frame (Std 2011) | Builds a data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
| Build Header Frame | Builds a header frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
| Parse Command Frame | Parses command frames from a phasor data concentrator (PDC) as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |

Parent topic:

Synchrophasor VIs

## TOPIC 00019: COMTRADE Configure VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-configure-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/comtrade-configure-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: COMTRADE VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Specifies the record and channel information that you want to write into .cfg and .dat files. You use the COMTRADE Write Data VI to write the record and channel information into .cfg and .dat files. Examples record info specifies the re

### COMTRADE Configure VI

**Owning Palette:** COMTRADE VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Specifies the record and channel information that you want to write into .cfg and .dat files. You use the COMTRADE Write Data VI to write the record and channel information into .cfg and .dat files.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Write_Configure.gif']

|  | record info specifies the record information for the specified .cfg and .dat files. station name specifies the name of the substation location. recording device name specifies the identification number or name of the recording device. line frequency specifies the nominal line frequency in hertz. The default is 50. timestamp multiplier specifies the multiplication factor for the time differential field in the data file. The default is 1. |
| --- | --- |
|  | station name specifies the name of the substation location. |
|  | recording device name specifies the identification number or name of the recording device. |
|  | line frequency specifies the nominal line frequency in hertz. The default is 50. |
|  | timestamp multiplier specifies the multiplication factor for the time differential field in the data file. The default is 1. |
|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
|  | analog channel info specifies the analog channel information for the specified .cfg and .dat files. channel identifier specifies the identifier of the analog channel. channel phase identification specifies the phase identification of the analog channel. monitored circuit component specifies the circuit component that is under monitoring. channel units specifies the units of the analog channel. channel time skew specifies the channel time skew, in microseconds, from the start of the sample period. min data value specifies the minimum range data value of the analog channel. The default is –2048. If the analog data value is less than min data value, this VI coerces the value to min data value. max data value specifies the maximum range data value of the analog channel. The default is 2048. If the analog data value is greater than max data value, this VI coerces the value to max data value. transformer ratio primary factor specifies the voltage or current transformer ratio primary factor of the analog channel. transformer ratio secondary factor specifies the voltage or current transformer ratio secondary factor of the analog channel. primary/secondary data scaling identifier specifies the primary or secondary data scaling identifier of the analog channel. |
|  | channel identifier specifies the identifier of the analog channel. |
|  | channel phase identification specifies the phase identification of the analog channel. |
|  | monitored circuit component specifies the circuit component that is under monitoring. |
|  | channel units specifies the units of the analog channel. |
|  | channel time skew specifies the channel time skew, in microseconds, from the start of the sample period. |
|  | min data value specifies the minimum range data value of the analog channel. The default is –2048. If the analog data value is less than min data value, this VI coerces the value to min data value. |
|  | max data value specifies the maximum range data value of the analog channel. The default is 2048. If the analog data value is greater than max data value, this VI coerces the value to max data value. |
|  | transformer ratio primary factor specifies the voltage or current transformer ratio primary factor of the analog channel. |
|  | transformer ratio secondary factor specifies the voltage or current transformer ratio secondary factor of the analog channel. |
|  | primary/secondary data scaling identifier specifies the primary or secondary data scaling identifier of the analog channel. |
|  | status channel info specifies the status channel information for the specified .cfg and .dat files. channel name specifies the name of the status channel. channel phase identification specifies the phase identification of the status channel. monitored circuit component specifies the circuit component that is under monitoring. normal state? specifies whether the status channel is in normal state. |
|  | channel name specifies the name of the status channel. |
|  | channel phase identification specifies the phase identification of the status channel. |
|  | monitored circuit component specifies the circuit component that is under monitoring. |
|  | normal state? specifies whether the status channel is in normal state. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | trigger timestamp specifies the date and time of the trigger point. If you leave trigger timestamp unwired, this VI uses the first data time as the trigger time. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the COMTRADE Configure VI:

- COMTRADE 1 Sample Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE N Samples Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE Waveform Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging

Parent topic:

COMTRADE VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=comtrade-list-private-contents-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00020: COMTRADE List Private Contents VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-list-private-contents-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/comtrade-list-private-contents-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Advanced VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Lists all the section names of the specified manufacturer name. If manufacturer name is empty, this VI returns all the manufacturer names and section names. You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the

### COMTRADE List Private Contents VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Lists all the section names of the specified manufacturer name. If **manufacturer name** is empty, this VI returns all the manufacturer names and section names. You must set **open mode** to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .hdr file. Otherwise this VI returns an error.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_List_Private_Contents.gif']

|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
| --- | --- |
|  | manufacturer name specifies the name of the manufacturer. Manufacturer names must start with letters. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | manufacturer names returns the specified manufacturer names, or returns all the manufacturer names if there is no specified manufacturer name. |
|  | manufacturer/section names returns all the section names and the corresponding manufacturer names. If there is no specified manufacturer name, this VI returns all the section names. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the COMTRADE Reading (Windows Only) VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging directory for an example of using the COMTRADE List Private Contents VI.

Parent topic:

Advanced VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=comtrade-open-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00021: COMTRADE Open VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-open-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/comtrade-open-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: COMTRADE VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Opens a .cfg and the corresponding .dat file for reading or writing. You also can use this VI to create a new file or replace an existing file. Set include header file? or include information file? to TRUE if you also want to create or

### COMTRADE Open VI

**Owning Palette:** COMTRADE VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Opens a .cfg and the corresponding .dat file for reading or writing. You also can use this VI to create a new file or replace an existing file. Set **include header file?** or **include information file?** to TRUE if you also want to create or open a .hdr or .inf file. Use the COMTRADE Close VI to close references to the files. IEEE Std C37.111-1999 specifies that a filename must not exceed eight characters. However, this VI accepts filenames that have more than eight characters, but returns a warning message.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Open.gif']

|  | include information file? specifies whether this VI also creates or opens a .inf file. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | include header file? specifies whether this VI also creates or opens a .hdr file. The default is FALSE. |
|  | configuration file path specifies the absolute path to the .cfg file. If configuration file path is empty (default) or is Not A Path, this VI displays the COMTRADE File Path dialog box from which you can select a .cfg file. |
|  | open mode specifies the operation to perform while opening files. 0Create (default)Creates new files.1Create or replaceCreates new files or replaces files if they exist.2Open (read only)Opens existing files for read only. |
| 0 | Create (default)Creates new files. |
| 1 | Create or replaceCreates new files or replaces files if they exist. |
| 2 | Open (read only)Opens existing files for read only. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the COMTRADE Logging and Reading.lvproj in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging directory for an example of using the COMTRADE Open VI.

Parent topic:

COMTRADE VIs

|  | analog channel info returns the analog channel information that this VI reads from the specified .cfg and .dat files. channel identifier returns the identifier of the analog channel. channel phase identification returns the phase identification of the analog channel. monitored circuit component returns the circuit component that is under monitoring. channel units returns the units of the analog channel. channel time skew returns the channel time skew, in microseconds, from the start of the sample period. min data value returns the minimum range data value of the analog channel. max data value returns the maximum range data value of the analog channel. transformer ratio primary factor returns the voltage or current transformer ratio primary factor of the analog channel. transformer ratio secondary factor returns the voltage or current transformer ratio secondary factor of the analog channel. primary/secondary data scaling identifier returns the primary or secondary data scaling identifier of the analog channel. |
|  | channel identifier returns the identifier of the analog channel. |
|  | channel phase identification returns the phase identification of the analog channel. |
|  | monitored circuit component returns the circuit component that is under monitoring. |
|  | channel units returns the units of the analog channel. |
|  | channel time skew returns the channel time skew, in microseconds, from the start of the sample period. |
|  | min data value returns the minimum range data value of the analog channel. |
|  | max data value returns the maximum range data value of the analog channel. |
|  | transformer ratio primary factor returns the voltage or current transformer ratio primary factor of the analog channel. |
|  | transformer ratio secondary factor returns the voltage or current transformer ratio secondary factor of the analog channel. |
|  | primary/secondary data scaling identifier returns the primary or secondary data scaling identifier of the analog channel. |
|  | status channel info returns the status channel information that this VI reads from the specified .cfg and .dat files. channel name returns the name of the status channel. channel phase identification returns the phase identification of the status channel. monitored circuit component returns the circuit component that is under monitoring. normal state? returns TRUE when the status channel is in normal state. |

|  | channel phase identification returns the phase identification of the status channel. |
|  | monitored circuit component returns the circuit component that is under monitoring. |

### COMTRADE Read Data VI

**Owning Palette:** COMTRADE VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Reads analog data and digital data from the specified .dat file. You must set **open mode** to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error.

|  | digital data returns the digital data that this VI reads from the input data. |

## TOPIC 00025: COMTRADE Read Private Inf VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-read-private-inf-vi.html`

You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. You must manually select th

### COMTRADE Read Private Inf VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Reads information from the private sections of the optionally specified .inf file. You must set **open mode** to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error.

You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

COMTRADE Read Private Inf (1 Entry)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Read_Private_Inf__1_Entry.gif']

|  | entry name specifies the name of the entry. |
| --- | --- |
|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
|  | manufacturer name specifies the name of the manufacturer. |
|  | section name specifies the name of the section. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | entry value returns the content of the entry. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | entry found? returns TRUE if this VI finds the specified entry. |

COMTRADE Read Private Inf (N Entries)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Read_Private_Inf__N_Entries.gif']

|  | entry names specifies the name of each entry. |
| --- | --- |
|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
|  | manufacturer name specifies the name of the manufacturer. |
|  | section name specifies the name of the section. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | entry values returns the content of each entry. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | entries found? returns TRUE for each entry if this VI finds the specified entries. |

COMTRADE Read Private Inf (All Entries)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Read_Private_Inf__All_Entries.gif']

|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
| --- | --- |

|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | entry names returns the name of each entry. |
|  | entry values returns the content of each entry. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
