You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. Example task in specifies th

### COMTRADE Read Public Inf VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Reads information from the public sections of the optionally specified .inf file. You must set **open mode** to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Read_Public_Inf.gif']

|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
| --- | --- |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | public record information returns the public record information this VI reads from the .inf file. Refer to IEEE Std C37.111-1999 for details about public record information. source returns description of the software that writes the record information. record type returns the type of the record information. phase ID returns the phase identifier. event description returns the description of the event. device ID returns the device identifier. distance of fault returns the distance to fault. distance unit returns the unit of the distance. max current returns the maximum current value. min current returns the minimum current value. max voltage returns the maximum voltage value. min voltage returns the minimum voltage value. |
|  | source returns description of the software that writes the record information. |
|  | record type returns the type of the record information. |
|  | phase ID returns the phase identifier. |
|  | event description returns the description of the event. |
|  | device ID returns the device identifier. |
|  | distance of fault returns the distance to fault. |
|  | distance unit returns the unit of the distance. |
|  | max current returns the maximum current value. |
|  | min current returns the minimum current value. |
|  | max voltage returns the maximum voltage value. |
|  | min voltage returns the minimum voltage value. |
|  | public event information returns the public event information this VI reads from the .inf file. Refer to IEEE Std C37.111-1999 for details about public event information. channel number returns the COMTRADE record channel number to which the public event information refers. max value returns the maximum values of voltage and current. min value returns the minimum values of voltage and current. max sample number returns the sample number at which the maximum recorded value occurs. min sample number returns the sample number at which the minimum recorded value occurs. max sample text returns text notes for the event on which the maximum recorded value occurs. min sample text returns text notes for the event on which the minimum recorded value occurs. |
|  | channel number returns the COMTRADE record channel number to which the public event information refers. |
|  | max value returns the maximum values of voltage and current. |
|  | min value returns the minimum values of voltage and current. |
|  | max sample number returns the sample number at which the maximum recorded value occurs. |
|  | min sample number returns the sample number at which the minimum recorded value occurs. |
|  | max sample text returns text notes for the event on which the maximum recorded value occurs. |
|  | min sample text returns text notes for the event on which the minimum recorded value occurs. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=comtrade-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00027: COMTRADE VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/comtrade-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: File I/O VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the COMTRADE VIs to log data using the Common Format for Transient Data Exchange (COMTRADE) format

### COMTRADE VIs

**Owning Palette:** File I/O VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Note

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| COMTRADE Close | Closes the file references you open with the COMTRADE Open VI. |
| COMTRADE Configure | Specifies the record and channel information that you want to write into .cfg and .dat files. You use the COMTRADE Write Data VI to write the record and channel information into .cfg and .dat files. |
| COMTRADE Open | Opens a .cfg and the corresponding .dat file for reading or writing. You also can use this VI to create a new file or replace an existing file. Set include header file? or include information file? to TRUE if you also want to create or open a .hdr or .inf file. Use the COMTRADE Close VI to close references to the files. IEEE Std C37.111-1999 specifies that a filename must not exceed eight characters. However, this VI accepts filenames that have more than eight characters, but returns a warning message. |
| COMTRADE Read Configure | Reads record and channel information from the specified .cfg and .dat files. You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Read Data | Reads analog data and digital data from the specified .dat file. You must set open mode to Open (read only) in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |
| COMTRADE Write Data | Writes configuration information and data to the specified .cfg and .dat files. You must set open mode to Create or Create or replace in the COMTRADE Open VI to open files. Otherwise this VI returns an error. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Advanced VIs | Use the Advanced VIs to perform advanced file I/O operations. |

Parent topic:

File I/O VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=comtrade-write-data-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00028: COMTRADE Write Data VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-write-data-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/comtrade-write-data-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: COMTRADE VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Writes configuration information and data to the specified .cfg and .dat files. You must set open mode to Create or Create or replace in the COMTRADE Open VI to open files. Otherwise this VI returns an error. The LabVIEW Electrical Powe

### COMTRADE Write Data VI

**Owning Palette:** COMTRADE VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

.cfg

.dat

open mode

Create

Create or replace

Note

.dat

.dat

.dat

.dat

You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use.

Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

COMTRADE Write Data (Waveform)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Write__Waveform.gif']

|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
| --- | --- |
|  | analog data specifies the input analog data. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | digital data specifies the input digital data. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |

You must set include header file? to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .hdr file. Otherwise this VI returns an error. Examples replace? specifies whether to replace the existing

### COMTRADE Write Header VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Writes data to the optionally specified .hdr file. You must set **include header file?** to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .hdr file. Otherwise this VI returns an error.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Write_Header.gif']

|  | replace? specifies whether to replace the existing header information in the .hdr file. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
|  | header information specifies the header information you want to write to the .hdr file. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the COMTRADE Write Header VI:

- COMTRADE N Samples Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE Waveform Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE 1 Sample Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging

## TOPIC 00030: COMTRADE Write Private Inf VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-write-private-inf-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/comtrade-write-private-inf-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Advanced VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Writes data to the private sections of the optionally specified .inf file. If the entry name you specify already exists, this VI replaces the existing entry. You must set include information file? to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open

### COMTRADE Write Private Inf VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Writes data to the private sections of the optionally specified .inf file. If the **entry name** you specify already exists, this VI replaces the existing entry. You must set **include information file?** to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error.

You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

COMTRADE Write Private Inf (1 Entry)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Write_Private_Inf__1_Entry.gif']

|  | entry value specifies the content of the entry. |
| --- | --- |
|  | entry name specifies the name of the entry. An entry name must start with one word that contains 3-32 characters. The ASCII code of characters for entry names must be between 33 and 127 in decimal. |
|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
|  | manufacturer name specifies the name of the manufacturer. Manufacturer names must start with letters. |
|  | section name specifies the name of the section. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | manufacturer name out returns manufacturer name unchanged. |
|  | section name out returns section name unchanged. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

COMTRADE Write Private Inf (N Entries)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Write_Private_Inf__N_Entries.gif']

|  | entry values specifies the content of each entry. |
| --- | --- |
|  | entry names specifies the name of each entry. An entry name must start with one word that contains 3-32 characters. The ASCII code of characters for entry names must be between 33 and 127 in decimal. |
|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
|  | manufacturer name specifies the name of the manufacturer. Manufacturer names must start with letters. |
|  | section name specifies the name of the section. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | manufacturer name out returns manufacturer name unchanged. |
|  | section name out returns section name unchanged. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the COMTRADE Write Private Inf VI:

- COMTRADE N Samples Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE Waveform Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE 1 Sample Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging

Parent topic:

Advanced VIs

## TOPIC 00031: COMTRADE Write Public Inf VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `comtrade-write-public-inf-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/comtrade-write-public-inf-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Advanced VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Writes data to the public sections of the optionally specified .inf file. You can set include configuration information? to TRUE to write the content of the specified .cfg file to this .inf file. You must set include information file? t

### COMTRADE Write Public Inf VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Writes data to the public sections of the optionally specified .inf file. You can set **include configuration information?** to TRUE to write the content of the specified .cfg file to this .inf file. You must set **include information file?** to TRUE in the COMTRADE Open VI to open the .inf file. Otherwise this VI returns an error.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/COMTRADE_Write_Public_Inf.gif']

|  | include configuration information? specifies whether to include the configuration information when this VI writes data to the .inf file. The default is FALSE. |
| --- | --- |
|  | task in specifies the configuration information and references. |
|  | public record information specifies the public record information to write to the .inf file. Refer to IEEE Std C37.111-1999 for details about public record information. source specifies description of the software that writes the record information. record type specifies the type of the record information. 0Fault1Unknown (default)2Misoperation3Close4Trip5Reclose6Power swing7Simulation phase ID specifies the phase identifier. event description specifies the description of the event. device ID specifies the device identifier. distance of fault specifies the distance to fault. The default is –1. distance unit specifies the unit for distance. 0Miles (default)1Kilometers2Percent of line3Percent of setting4Ohms max current specifies the maximum current value. The default is –Inf. min current specifies the minimum current value. The default is Inf. max voltage specifies the maximum voltage value. The default is –Inf. min voltage specifies the minimum voltage value. The default is Inf. |
|  | source specifies description of the software that writes the record information. |
|  | record type specifies the type of the record information. 0Fault1Unknown (default)2Misoperation3Close4Trip5Reclose6Power swing7Simulation |
| 0 | Fault |
| 1 | Unknown (default) |
| 2 | Misoperation |
| 3 | Close |
| 4 | Trip |
| 5 | Reclose |
| 6 | Power swing |
| 7 | Simulation |
|  | phase ID specifies the phase identifier. |
|  | event description specifies the description of the event. |
|  | device ID specifies the device identifier. |
|  | distance of fault specifies the distance to fault. The default is –1. |
|  | distance unit specifies the unit for distance. 0Miles (default)1Kilometers2Percent of line3Percent of setting4Ohms |
| 0 | Miles (default) |
| 1 | Kilometers |
| 2 | Percent of line |
| 3 | Percent of setting |
| 4 | Ohms |
|  | max current specifies the maximum current value. The default is –Inf. |
|  | min current specifies the minimum current value. The default is Inf. |
|  | max voltage specifies the maximum voltage value. The default is –Inf. |
|  | min voltage specifies the minimum voltage value. The default is Inf. |
|  | public event information specifies the public event information to write to the .inf file. Refer to IEEE Std C37.111-1999 for details about public event information. channel number specifies the COMTRADE record channel number to which the public event information refers. The default is 0. max value specifies the maximum values of voltage and current. The default is –Inf. min value specifies the minimum values of voltage and current. The default is Inf. max sample number specifies the sample number at which the maximum recorded value occurs. The default is 0. min sample number specifies the sample number at which the minimum recorded value occurs. The default is 0. max sample text specifies text notes for the event on which the maximum recorded value occurs. min sample text specifies text notes for the event on which the minimum recorded value occurs. |
|  | channel number specifies the COMTRADE record channel number to which the public event information refers. The default is 0. |
|  | max value specifies the maximum values of voltage and current. The default is –Inf. |
|  | min value specifies the minimum values of voltage and current. The default is Inf. |
|  | max sample number specifies the sample number at which the maximum recorded value occurs. The default is 0. |
|  | min sample number specifies the sample number at which the minimum recorded value occurs. The default is 0. |
|  | max sample text specifies text notes for the event on which the maximum recorded value occurs. |
|  | min sample text specifies text notes for the event on which the minimum recorded value occurs. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | task out returns updated configuration information and references. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the COMTRADE Write Public Inf VI:

- COMTRADE N Samples Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE Waveform Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging
- COMTRADE 1 Sample Logging VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\COMTRADE Logging

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=convert-data-to-waveforms-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00032: Convert Data to Waveforms VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `convert-data-to-waveforms-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/convert-data-to-waveforms-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Quality VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Converts resampled voltage and current data to voltage and current waveforms of different line configurations. This VI also arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms of each three-phase electrical power system for further

### Convert Data to Waveforms VI

**Owning Palette:** Power Quality VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Converts resampled voltage and current data to voltage and current waveforms of different line configurations. This VI also arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms of each three-phase electrical power system for further measurement. For example, the orders are A-B-C-N for the four-channel method and A-B-C for the three-channel method. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Note

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Convert_to_Waveforms.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | physical channels specifies the order of the physical channels that you use to acquire data from physical data acquisition modules. Note When you specify the wiring input of the Initialize System VI as 3xUph + 2xI or 3xUline + 2xI, the Convert Data to Waveforms VI converts data only from Current Phase A (L1) and Current Phase C (L3). When you specify the wiring input of the Initialize System VI as 1xUph + 1xI, the Convert Data to Waveform VI converts data only from Voltage Phase A (L1) and Current Phase A (L1). |
|  | data in specifies multichannel resampled voltage and current signals using single-precision numbers. The number of rows corresponds to the number of samples per 10/12 cycles and the number of columns corresponds to the number of physical channels. |
|  | sample rate specifies the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of every 10/12 cycles. sample rate is the multiplication of the instantaneous frequency and the number of samples per cycle. |
|  | timestamp specifies the absolute timestamp of the end of every 10/12 cycles. You acquire timestamp from timing resources such as GPS or real-time clock. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | voltage waveforms returns 10/12-cycle voltage waveforms using double-precision numbers. This VI arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms for further measurement, for example, A-B-C-N for four channels method and A-B-C for three channels method. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveforms returns 10/12-cycle current waveforms using double-precision numbers. This VI arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms for further measurement. For example, A-B-C-N is for the four-channel method and A-B-C for the three-channel method. Note When you specify the wiring input of the Initialize System VI as 3xUph + 2xI or 3xUline + 2xI, the Convert Data to Waveforms VI returns the data for Current Phase B (L2) from Current Phase A (L1) and Current Phase C (L3). t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | interval length returns the duration, in seconds, of every 10/12-cycle interval. |

Note

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Convert_Cluster_to_Waveforms.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | physical channels specifies the order of the physical channels that you use to acquire data from physical data acquisition modules. Note When you specify the wiring input of the Initialize System VI as 3xUph + 2xI or 3xUline + 2xI, the Convert Data to Waveforms VI converts data only from Current Phase A (L1) and Current Phase C (L3). When you specify the wiring input of the Initialize System VI as 1xUph + 1xI, the Convert Data to Waveform VI converts data only from Voltage Phase A (L1) and Current Phase A (L1). |
|  | data block specifies the data block that you build with the Build Data Blocks VI. resampled signal specifies the resampled even-angle-spaced signal that you build with the Build Data Blocks VI. The number of rows corresponds to the number of physical channels and the number of columns corresponds to the number of samples per 10/12 cycles. sample rate specifies the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of every data block. sample rate is the multiplication of the instantaneous frequency and the number of samples per cycle. timestamp specifies the timestamp of the end of every data block. |
|  | resampled signal specifies the resampled even-angle-spaced signal that you build with the Build Data Blocks VI. The number of rows corresponds to the number of physical channels and the number of columns corresponds to the number of samples per 10/12 cycles. |
|  | sample rate specifies the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of every data block. sample rate is the multiplication of the instantaneous frequency and the number of samples per cycle. |
|  | timestamp specifies the timestamp of the end of every data block. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | voltage waveforms returns 10/12-cycle voltage waveforms using double-precision numbers. This VI arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms for further measurement, for example, A-B-C-N for four channels method and A-B-C for three channels method. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveforms returns 10/12-cycle current waveforms using double-precision numbers. This VI arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms for further measurement. For example, A-B-C-N is for the four-channel method and A-B-C for the three-channel method. Note When you specify the wiring input of the Initialize System VI as 3xUph + 2xI or 3xUline + 2xI, the Convert Data to Waveforms VI returns the data for Current Phase B (L2) from Current Phase A (L1) and Current Phase C (L3). t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | interval length returns the duration, in seconds, of every 10/12-cycle interval. |

Note

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Convert_to_Waveforms_MLine_2D.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies multichannel resampled voltage and current signals using single-precision numbers. The number of rows corresponds to the number of samples per 10/12 cycles and the number of columns corresponds to the number of physical channels. |
|  | sample rate specifies the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of every 10/12 cycles. sample rate is the multiplication of the instantaneous frequency and the number of samples per cycle. |
|  | timestamp specifies the absolute timestamp of the end of every 10/12 cycles. You acquire timestamp from timing resources such as GPS or real-time clock. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | voltage waveforms returns 10/12-cycle voltage waveforms using double-precision numbers one three-phase system after another in the sequence defined in the Initialize System VI. This VI arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms for each system, for example, A-B-C-N for four channels method and A-B-C for three channels method. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveforms returns 10/12-cycle current waveforms using double-precision numbers one three-phase system after another in the sequence defined in the Initialize System VI. This VI arranges the order of the elements in the waveforms for each system, for example, A-B-C-N for four channels method and A-B-C for three channels method. Note When a three-phase system has only two current channels, this VI does not automatically calculate the third current. For example, if you specify Current Phase A (L1) and Current Phase C (L3), this VI does not calculate Current Phase B (L2) automatically. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | interval length returns the duration, in seconds, of every 10/12-cycle interval. |

Note

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Convert_to_Waveforms_MLine_Cluster.gif']

## TOPIC 00033: Convert Line Current to Phase Current (Delta Configuration) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `convert-line-current-to-phase-current-delta-c.html`

This VI supports only nonsinusoidal, three-phase, three-wire, balanced signals. Details Example line current waveforms specifies the current signals, in amperes, measured thr

### Convert Line Current to Phase Current (Delta Configuration) VI

**Owning Palette:** Utility VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Converts line current to phase current in Delta configuration. This VI supports only nonsinusoidal, three-phase, three-wire, balanced signals.

Details Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Convert_Line_Current_to_Phase_Current__Delta_Configuration.gif']

|  | line current waveforms specifies the current signals, in amperes, measured through line a, line b, and line c. Ia specifies the current signal, in amperes, measured through line a. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. Ib specifies the current signal, in amperes, measured through line b. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. Ic specifies the current signal, in amperes, through measured line c. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Ia specifies the current signal, in amperes, measured through line a. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | Ib specifies the current signal, in amperes, measured through line b. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | Ic specifies the current signal, in amperes, through measured line c. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | phase current waveforms returns the current signals, in amperes, flowing through the load between two lines. Iab returns the current signal, in amperes, flowing through the load between line a and line b. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. Ibc returns the current signal, in amperes, flowing through the load between line b and line c. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. Ica returns the current signal, in amperes, flowing through the load between line c and line a. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | Iab returns the current signal, in amperes, flowing through the load between line a and line b. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | Ibc returns the current signal, in amperes, flowing through the load between line b and line c. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | Ica returns the current signal, in amperes, flowing through the load between line c and line a. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Convert Line Current to Phase Current (Delta Configuration) Details

This VI uses the following equations to convert line current signals to phase current signals in Delta configuration:

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_line_current_to_phase_current_iab.gif']

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_line_current_to_phase_current_ibc.gif']

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_line_current_to_phase_current_ica.gif']

where

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_ia.gif'], [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_ib.gif'], and [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_ic.gif'] represent the line current signals in amperes

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_iab.gif'], [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_ibc.gif'], and [IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_ica.gif'] represent the phase current signals in amperes

The following figure shows the circuit of the three-phase Delta configuration:

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/ep_3_phase_delta.gif']


Example

Refer to the Frequency Domain Power Value of 3-Phase System VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\EV Power Test\Frequency Analysis directory for an example of using the Convert Line Current to Phase Current (Delta Configuration) VI.

### Convert Line Voltage to Phase Voltage (Y Configuration) VI

**Owning Palette:** Utility VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Converts line voltage to phase voltage in Y configuration. This VI supports only nonsinusoidal, three-phase, three-wire, balanced signals.

Details

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Convert_Line_Voltage_to_Phase_Voltage__Y_Configuration.gif']

|  | line voltage waveforms specifies the voltage signals, in volts, measured between two lines. Vab specifies the voltage signal, in volts, between line a and line b. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. Vbc specifies the voltage signal, in volts, between line b and line c. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. Vca specifies the voltage signal, in volts, between line c and line a. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Vab specifies the voltage signal, in volts, between line a and line b. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |

|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | phase voltage waveforms specifies the voltage signals, in volts, measured between any phase and neutral. Van returns the voltage signal, in volts, between phase a and neutral. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. Vbn returns the voltage signal, in volts, between phase b and neutral. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. Vcn returns the voltage signal, in volts, between phase c and neutral. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | Van returns the voltage signal, in volts, between phase a and neutral. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |

|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |

## TOPIC 00036: Cross Power Spectrum VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `cross-power-spectrum-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/cross-power-spectrum-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Three Phase Power Measurement VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Measures the cross power spectrum of a time-domain voltage and current signal. Wire data to the voltage waveform input or the current waveform input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select th

### Cross Power Spectrum VI

**Owning Palette:** Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Measures the cross power spectrum of a time-domain voltage and current signal. Wire data to the **voltage waveform** input or the **current waveform** input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Details Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Cross Power Spectrum (1 Channel)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Cross_Power_Spectrum__1-phase_1_Ch.gif']

|  | voltage waveform specifies the voltage signal in volts. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveform specifies the current signal in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | cross power spectrum returns the start frequency, frequency resolution, and the cross power spectrum of one channel. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum in hertz. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum in hertz. power returns the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum in hertz. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum in hertz. |
|  | power returns the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Cross Power Spectrum (N Channels)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Cross_Power_Spectra__N_Ch.gif']

|  | voltage waveforms specifies the waveforms of the voltage signals for multiple channels in volts. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveforms specifies the waveforms of the current signals, in amperes, for multiple channels. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | cross power spectra returns the start frequency, frequency resolution, and the cross power spectrum of each channel. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum in hertz. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum in hertz. power returns the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum in hertz. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum in hertz. |
|  | power returns the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Cross Power Spectrum Details

This VI calculates the instantaneous cross power spectrum using the following equation.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_cross_power_spectrum.gif']

where

I<sup>*</sup> represents the complex conjugate of the complex FFT of the current signal. I is the RMS value of the current signal in amperes

V represents the complex FFT of the voltage signal, which is the RMS value of the voltage signal in volts


Example

Refer to the Frequency Domain Power Value of 3-Phase System VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\EV Power Test\Frequency Analysis directory for an example of using the Cross Power Spectrum VI.

Parent topic:

Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=custom-aggregation-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00037: Custom Aggregation VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `custom-aggregation-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/custom-aggregation-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Aggregation VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the aggregated minimum, maximum, and averaged or RMS value of an input signal for a specified aggregation interval, according to Class S in IEC 61000-4-30:2008. There is no overlap between aggregation intervals. This VI au

### Custom Aggregation VI

**Owning Palette:** Aggregation VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the aggregated minimum, maximum, and averaged or RMS value of an input signal for a specified aggregation interval, according to Class S in IEC 61000-4-30:2008. There is no overlap between aggregation intervals. This VI automatically restarts accumulation at the beginning of the next time interval after the previous interval expires. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems except the DBL instance. Use the DBL instance to measure one channel on one line at one time.

Details Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Custom Aggregation (DBL)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Custom_Aggregation__DBL.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the aggregation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time or at the end of each aggregation interval, the aggregation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the input data for aggregation. |
|  | aggregation settings specifies settings for the aggregation process. interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. 0Average (default)1RMS |
|  | interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. |
|  | interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks |
| 0 | Runs (default) |
| 1 | Seconds |
| 2 | Minutes |
| 3 | Hours |
| 4 | Days |
| 5 | Weeks |
|  | synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded |
| 0 | Not synchronized (default) |
| 1 | Interval rounded |
|  | algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. 0Average (default)1RMS |
| 0 | Average (default) |
| 1 | RMS |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | data timestamp specifies the timestamp of the input data. |
|  | aggregated minimum value returns the minimum value calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |
|  | aggregated averaged/RMS value returns averaged or RMS value calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |
|  | aggregated maximum value returns the maximum value calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | aggregated data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. |

Custom Aggregation (1D DBL Array)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Custom_Aggregation__1D_DBL_Array.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the aggregation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time or at the end of each aggregation interval, the aggregation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the input data for aggregation. |
|  | aggregation settings specifies settings for the aggregation process. interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. 0Average (default)1RMS |
|  | interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. |
|  | interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks |
| 0 | Runs (default) |
| 1 | Seconds |
| 2 | Minutes |
| 3 | Hours |
| 4 | Days |
| 5 | Weeks |
|  | synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded |
| 0 | Not synchronized (default) |
| 1 | Interval rounded |
|  | algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. 0Average (default)1RMS |
| 0 | Average (default) |
| 1 | RMS |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | data timestamp specifies the timestamp of the input data. |
|  | aggregated minimum values returns the minimum values calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |
|  | aggregated averaged/RMS values returns average or RMS values calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |
|  | aggregated maximum values returns the maximum values calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | aggregated data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. |

Custom Aggregation (Deviation)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Custom_Aggregation__Deviation.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the aggregation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time or at the end of each aggregation interval, the aggregation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Urms-under specifies the underdeviation parameters, in volts, for aggregation. You calculate Urms-under based on the 10/12-cycle RMS value. |
|  | Urms-over specifies the overdeviation parameters, in volts, for aggregation. You calculate Urms-over based on the 10/12-cycle RMS value. |
|  | aggregation settings specifies settings for the aggregation process. interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. IEC 61000-4-30:2008 defines that the aggregation algorithm for underdeviation and overdeviation is RMS. 0Average1RMS (default) |
|  | interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. |
|  | interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks |
| 0 | Runs (default) |
| 1 | Seconds |
| 2 | Minutes |
| 3 | Hours |
| 4 | Days |
| 5 | Weeks |
|  | synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded |
| 0 | Not synchronized (default) |
| 1 | Interval rounded |
|  | algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. IEC 61000-4-30:2008 defines that the aggregation algorithm for underdeviation and overdeviation is RMS. 0Average1RMS (default) |
| 0 | Average |
| 1 | RMS (default) |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | data timestamp specifies the timestamp of the input data. |
|  | Uunder returns underdeviation assessment calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |
|  | Uover returns overdeviation assessment calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. |

Custom Aggregation (Power Values)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Custom_Aggregation__Power_Values.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the aggregation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time or at the end of each aggregation interval, the aggregation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | power values specifies power values for aggregation. active power specifies the active power value in watts. Each element of active power corresponds to one channel. reactive power specifies the reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. Each element of reactive power corresponds to one channel. apparent power specifies the apparent power value in volt-amperes. Each element of apparent power corresponds to one channel. fundamental active power specifies the fundamental active power value in watts. Each element of fundamental active power corresponds to one channel. fundamental reactive power specifies the fundamental reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. Each element of fundamental reactive power corresponds to one channel. cosine specifies the fundamental power factor. Each element of cosine corresponds to one channel. power factor specifies the power factor value. Each element of power factor corresponds to one channel. |

|  | cosine specifies the fundamental power factor. Each element of cosine corresponds to one channel. |
|  | power factor specifies the power factor value. Each element of power factor corresponds to one channel. |

Custom Aggregation (Symmetrical Components)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Custom_Aggregation__Symm_Comp.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the aggregation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time or at the end of each aggregation interval, the aggregation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | symmetrical components specifies the input data for aggregation. All the symmetrical components have the following array order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component voltage specifies voltage symmetrical components. fundamental voltage specifies fundamental voltage symmetrical components. current specifies current symmetrical components. fundamental current specifies fundamental current symmetrical components. |
| 0 | Zero sequence symmetrical component |
| 1 | Positive sequence symmetrical component |
| 2 | Negative sequence symmetrical component |
|  | voltage specifies voltage symmetrical components. |
|  | fundamental voltage specifies fundamental voltage symmetrical components. |
|  | current specifies current symmetrical components. |
|  | fundamental current specifies fundamental current symmetrical components. |
|  | aggregation settings specifies settings for the aggregation process. interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. 0Average (default)1RMS |

Custom Aggregation (Unbalance)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Custom_Aggregation__Unbalance.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the aggregation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time or at the end of each aggregation interval, the aggregation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | unbalance specifies the input data for aggregation. The first element of each array is the negative sequence unbalance, which is represented as sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. The second element of each array is zero sequence unbalance, which is represented as zero sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. voltage specifies negative sequence and zero sequence voltage unbalance in percentages. fundamental voltage specifies negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental voltage unbalance in percentages. current specifies negative sequence and zero sequence current unbalance in percentages. fundamental current specifies negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental current unbalance in percentages. |
|  | voltage specifies negative sequence and zero sequence voltage unbalance in percentages. |
|  | fundamental voltage specifies negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental voltage unbalance in percentages. |
|  | current specifies negative sequence and zero sequence current unbalance in percentages. |
|  | fundamental current specifies negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental current unbalance in percentages. |
|  | aggregation settings specifies settings for the aggregation process. interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. 0Average (default)1RMS |
|  | interval specifies the length of the aggregation interval. The default is 1. This VI synchronizes aggregation intervals in such a way that the aggregation interval must contain integer multiple times before the next higher time unit of aggregation interval. For example, if the aggregation interval is 12 minutes, the aggregator returns aggregated values at each 00, 12, 24, 36, and 48 minutes clock ticks. |
|  | interval unit specifies the unit of aggregation interval. The default is Runs, which specifies that the aggregation interval is in number of calls to this VI. 0Runs (default)1Seconds2Minutes3Hours4Days5Weeks |
| 0 | Runs (default) |
| 1 | Seconds |
| 2 | Minutes |
| 3 | Hours |
| 4 | Days |
| 5 | Weeks |
|  | synchronization of intervals specifies whether to align the timestamps of aggregated output with absolute time or with the start of aggregation. For example, if the aggregation interval is ten minutes, this VI returns aggregated values at each 10-minute clock tick if you select Interval rounded, or this VI returns aggregated values each 10 minutes from the start of aggregation if you select Not synchronized. When interval unit is Run, this VI does not synchronize aggregation intervals. 0Not synchronized (default)1Interval rounded |
| 0 | Not synchronized (default) |
| 1 | Interval rounded |
|  | algorithm specifies the algorithm for calculating aggregated values. 0Average (default)1RMS |
| 0 | Average (default) |
| 1 | RMS |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | data timestamp specifies the timestamp of the input data. |
|  | aggregated minimum value returns the minimum value calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. The first element of each array is the negative sequence unbalance, which is represented as sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. The second element of each array is zero sequence unbalance, which is represented as zero sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. voltage returns negative sequence and zero sequence voltage unbalance in percentages. fundamental voltage returns negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental voltage unbalance in percentages. current returns negative sequence and zero sequence current unbalance in percentages. fundamental current returns negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental current unbalance in percentages. |

|  | aggregated averaged/RMS value returns averaged or RMS value calculated from the input data over the aggregation interval. The first element of each array is the negative sequence unbalance, which is represented as sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. The second element of each array is zero sequence unbalance, which is represented as zero sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. voltage returns negative sequence and zero sequence voltage unbalance in percentages. fundamental voltage returns negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental voltage unbalance in percentages. current returns negative sequence and zero sequence current unbalance in percentages. fundamental current returns negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental current unbalance in percentages. |

The first element of each array is the negative sequence unbalance, which is represented as sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. The second element of each array is zero sequence unbalance, which is represented as zero sequence symmetrical component/positive sequence symmetrical component. voltage returns negative sequence and zero sequence voltage unbalance in percentages. fundamental voltage returns negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental voltage unbalance in percentages. current returns negative sequence and zero sequence current unbalance in percentages. fundamental current returns negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental current unbalance in percentages. |

|  | fundamental current returns negative sequence and zero sequence fundamental current unbalance in percentages. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | aggregated data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. |
