## TOPIC 00038: D to Y Conversion VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `d-to-y-conversion-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/d-to-y-conversion-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Utility VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Converts line voltages to phase voltages. This VI calculates an approximate value for the phase voltage in an unbalanced system because the neutral point is placed at the center of the gravity of the line voltages triangle. When calculat

### D to Y Conversion VI

**Owning Palette:** Utility VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Converts line voltages to phase voltages. This VI calculates an approximate value for the phase voltage in an unbalanced system because the neutral point is placed at the center of the gravity of the line voltages triangle. When calculating phase voltage values, this VI places the neutral point at the center of gravity of the line voltage triangle, which are the characteristics of a balanced three-phase electrical power system. Thus the phase voltage values this VI returns are approximate values for unbalanced system. You must use this VI before performing power measurement, one-cycle power measurement, and signaling measurement, if the input wiring is line voltage.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use.

Details

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

D to Y Conversion (Spectra)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/D_to_Y__Spectra__Conversion.gif']

|  | line voltage spectra specifies 10/12-cycle voltage complex spectra, in volts, one three-phase system after another. Ensure that you specify the spectra of Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C for each three-phase system. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. For line voltages, Voltage Phase A (L1), Voltage Phase B (L2), and Voltage Phase C (L3) equal Phase AB, Phase BC, and Phase CA respectively. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. For line voltages, Voltage Phase A (L1), Voltage Phase B (L2), and Voltage Phase C (L3) equal Phase AB, Phase BC, and Phase CA respectively. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | phase voltage spectra returns the phase voltage spectra information one three-phase system after another. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes returns the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases returns the phase value of the spectrum. channel returns the channel of the spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes returns the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases returns the phase value of the spectrum. |
|  | channel returns the channel of the spectrum. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

D to Y Conversion (Vectors)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/D_to_Y__Vectors__Conversion.gif']

|  | one-cycle fundamental line voltage vectors specifies one-cycle fundamental line voltage vectors, in volts, one three-phase system after another. Each array element represents one measurement channel and contains one-cycle fundamental vectors from 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Ensure that you specify the vectors of Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C for each three-phase system. timestamps specifies the timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. vectors specifies the voltage vector value. channel specifies the channel of the voltage vector. For line voltages, Voltage Phase A (L1), Voltage Phase B (L2), and Voltage Phase C (L3) equal Phase AB, Phase BC, and Phase CA respectively. |
| --- | --- |
|  | timestamps specifies the timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. |
|  | vectors specifies the voltage vector value. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage vector. For line voltages, Voltage Phase A (L1), Voltage Phase B (L2), and Voltage Phase C (L3) equal Phase AB, Phase BC, and Phase CA respectively. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | one-cycle fundamental phase voltage vectors returns one-cycle fundamental phase voltage vectors, in volts, one three-phase system after another. Each array element represents one measurement channel and contains one-cycle fundamental vectors from 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. timestamps returns the timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. vectors returns the phase voltage vector value. channel returns the channel of the phase voltage vector. |
|  | timestamps returns the timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. |
|  | vectors returns the phase voltage vector value. |
|  | channel returns the channel of the phase voltage vector. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

D to Y Conversion Details

In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that the input signals of this VI are from the line voltage channels based on the physical connection type. If the input signals are from phase voltage channels, this VI returns incorrect values.

**Related Information**
Conversion
Delta Connections
Line and Bus Configurations
Power Transmission

Parent topic:

Utility VIs

## TOPIC 00039: DC Power VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `dc-power-vi.html`

- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Battery VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the DC power of a battery pack. Examples voltage waveform specifies the voltage signal in volts. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y s

### DC Power VI

**Owning Palette:** Battery VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the DC power of a battery pack.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/DC_Power.gif']

|  | voltage waveform specifies the voltage signal in volts. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | current waveform specifies the current signal in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | DC power returns the DC power, in watts, of the battery pack. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the DC Power VI:

- Efficiency VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\EV Power Test\Efficiency
- Traction Inverter and Electric Motor Efficiency Measurement (TDMS) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\EV Power Test\Traction Inverter

## TOPIC 00040: Deviation VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `deviation-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/deviation-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: U, I & Freq VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates underdeviation and overdeviation values from 10/12-cycle voltage RMS values. You can use the RMS (10 or 12 Cycles) VI to calculate voltage RMS values. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. Detai

### Deviation VI

**Owning Palette:** U, I & Freq VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates underdeviation and overdeviation values from 10/12-cycle voltage RMS values. You can use the RMS (10 or 12 Cycles) VI to calculate voltage RMS values.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Deviation.gif']

|  | voltage RMS values specifies 10/12-cycle voltage RMS values, according to IEC-61000-4-30:2008. |
| --- | --- |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | Urms-under returns the underdeviation values of the 10/12-cycle voltage RMS values. |
|  | Urms-over returns the overdeviation values of the 10/12-cycle voltage RMS values. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Deviation Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Deviation VI:

- Magnitude & Frequency (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Magnitude & Frequency VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Magnitude & Frequency VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

U, I & Freq VIs

### DQZ to ABC Transform VI

**Owning Palette:** Transform VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Transforms signals from the DQZ reference frame to the ABC reference frame. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

DQZ to ABC Transform Details

The equations for transforming voltage also apply to current. This section takes the voltage equations for an example.

This VI uses the following equations to transform signals from the DQZ reference frame to the ABC reference frame:
Magnitude Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_dqz_to_abc_magnitude_invariant.gif']

where

θ represents the instantaneous angle between d-axis and a-axis in radians

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents the signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>d</sub>, v<sub>q</sub>, v<sub>z</sub>] represents the signals in the DQZ reference frame
Power Invariant

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_dqz_to_abc_power_invariant.gif']

where

θ represents the instantaneous angle between d-axis and a-axis in radians

[v<sub>a</sub>, v<sub>b</sub>, v<sub>c</sub>] represents the signals in the ABC reference frame

[v<sub>d</sub>, v<sub>q</sub>, v<sub>z</sub>] represents the signals in the DQZ reference frame


Example

Refer to the Transforms VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\EV Power Test\Transforms directory for an example of using the DQZ to ABC Transform VI.

## TOPIC 00043: Efficiency VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `efficiency-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/efficiency-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Three Phase Power Measurement VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Efficiency VIs to calculate the power efficiency of motors and inverters.

### Efficiency VIs

**Owning Palette:** Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Efficiency VIs to calculate the power efficiency of motors and inverters.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Inverter Efficiency | Calculates the power efficiency of an inverter. |
| Mechanical Power | Calculates the output mechanical power of a motor. |
| Motor Efficiency | Calculates the power efficiency of a motor. |

Parent topic:

Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

Palette ObjectDescriptionVector DiagramDisplays vectors using an arrow style, especially for vectors of voltage and current. A vector diagram uses arrays of double com

### Electrical Power Controls

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Use the Electrical Power controls with the Electrical Power VIs to display electrical power data.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Vector Diagram | Displays vectors using an arrow style, especially for vectors of voltage and current. A vector diagram uses arrays of double complex data type and the vector elements appear in sequence in clockwise. |

Parent topic:

Electrical Power VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=electrical-power-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00045: Electrical Power VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `electrical-power-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/electrical-power-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: May 2020, 373376J-01Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Electrical Power VIs to perform electrical power analysis. The VIs on this palette can return general L

### Electrical Power VIs

May 2020, 373376J-01

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Electrical Power VIs to perform electrical power analysis.

The VIs on this palette can return general LabVIEW error codes or specific Electrical Power error codes.

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| EV Power Test VIs | Use the EV Power Test VIs to perform power tests on electric vehicles. |
| File I/O VIs | Use the File I/O VIs to log data for electrical power analysis. |
| FPGA VIs | Use the FPGA VIs to measure electrical power data on FPGA targets. |
| Motor VIs | Use the Motor VIs to conduct condition monitoring on AC induction motors, including performing the motor current signature analysis (MCSA). |
| Power Measurement VIs | Use the Power Measurement VIs to measure common electrical power parameters and to perform harmonic analysis. |
| Power Quality VIs | Use the Power Quality VIs to perform power quality measurement in compliance with IEC 61000-4-7:2009, IEC 61000-4-15:2010, and IEC 61000-4-30:2008. |
| Protection VIs | Use the Protection VIs to generate data for the Protection VIs on FPGA targets to use. |
| Synchrophasor VIs | Use the Synchrophasor VIs to perform synchrophasor measurement and to build and parse synchrophasor message frames. The VIs for measuring synchrophasors are in compliance with IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. The C37.118 Frame VIs build and parse synchrophasor message frames for real-time communication of synchrophasor measurement data between phasor measurement units (PMUs) and phasor data concentrators (PDCs) as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |

© 2011–2020 National Instruments. All rights reserved.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=en50160-aggregation-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00046: EN50160 Aggregation VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `en50160-aggregation-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/en50160-aggregation-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Aggregation VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates aggregated values of power quality parameters for specific intervals. You use the aggregated values for further evaluations according to EN 50160:2007. The aggregation methods are compliant with Class S in IEC 61000-4-30:2

### EN50160 Aggregation VI

**Owning Palette:** Aggregation VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates aggregated values of power quality parameters for specific intervals. You use the aggregated values for further evaluations according to EN 50160:2007. The aggregation methods are compliant with Class S in IEC 61000-4-30:2008. The aggregation is based on a 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. There is no overlap between aggregation intervals. This VI automatically restarts accumulation at the beginning of the next 10/12-cycle time interval after the previous interval expires. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems.

Details Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

EN50160 Aggregation (3s, 10s, 10min)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/EN50160_Aggregation__3s_10s_10min.gif']

|  | data timestamp specifies the timestamp of the input data. |
| --- | --- |
|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the aggregation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time or at the end of each aggregation interval, the aggregation process resets automatically. |
|  | half-cycle frequency specifies frequency values measured over half cycle. The measurement starts at fundamental zero crossing and refreshes every half cycle. timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each half cycle. frequencies specifies the frequency value for each half cycle. |
|  | timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each half cycle. |
|  | frequencies specifies the frequency value for each half cycle. |
|  | voltage RMS values specifies RMS value of the voltage magnitude over a basic measurement time interval, which is 10 or 12 cycles. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | fundamental voltage unbalance u2 specifies negative sequence voltage unbalance expressed as (U2/U1)*100%, where U1 is positive sequence symmetrical components and U2 is negative sequence symmetrical components. |
|  | signaling voltage RMS values specifies mains signaling voltage on the supply voltage according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | voltage THD specifies total harmonic distortion of voltage, in percentage, as defined in IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | harmonic subgroups complex spectra specifies complex spectra of harmonic subgroups with fundamental frequency step. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. vectors specifies harmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | vectors specifies harmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
|  | voltage flags? specifies voltage flags of a basic measurement time interval, which is 10 or 12 cycles. Interval is flagged during a voltage dip, swells or interruption event. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. The default is FALSE. |
|  | 10min values returns aggregated values for the 10 minutes period. data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. voltage RMS values returns aggregated RMS value of the voltage magnitude. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. voltage underdeviation returns the absolute value of the difference between the measured voltage and the nominal voltage, only when the measured voltage is lower than the nominal voltage. Voltage underdeviation is in percentage of nominal voltage. voltage overdeviation returns the absolute value of the difference between the measured voltage and the nominal voltage, only when the measured voltage is greater than the nominal voltage. Voltage overdeviation is in percent of nominal voltage. fundamental voltage unbalance u2 returns aggregated negative sequence voltage unbalance expressed as (U2/U1) * 100%, where U1 is positive sequence symmetrical components and U2 is negative sequence symmetrical components. voltage THD returns aggregated total harmonic distortion of voltage as defined in IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. harmonic voltage RMS values returns aggregated first 25th harmonic voltage amplitudes. Each row of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. Each column of this array corresponds to one harmonic. interval flagged? returns TRUE if any value during the interval is flagged. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. |
|  | voltage RMS values returns aggregated RMS value of the voltage magnitude. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | voltage underdeviation returns the absolute value of the difference between the measured voltage and the nominal voltage, only when the measured voltage is lower than the nominal voltage. Voltage underdeviation is in percentage of nominal voltage. |
|  | voltage overdeviation returns the absolute value of the difference between the measured voltage and the nominal voltage, only when the measured voltage is greater than the nominal voltage. Voltage overdeviation is in percent of nominal voltage. |
|  | fundamental voltage unbalance u2 returns aggregated negative sequence voltage unbalance expressed as (U2/U1) * 100%, where U1 is positive sequence symmetrical components and U2 is negative sequence symmetrical components. |
|  | voltage THD returns aggregated total harmonic distortion of voltage as defined in IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | harmonic voltage RMS values returns aggregated first 25th harmonic voltage amplitudes. Each row of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. Each column of this array corresponds to one harmonic. |
|  | interval flagged? returns TRUE if any value during the interval is flagged. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | 10s values returns aggregated values for the 10 seconds period. data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. frequency returns aggregated frequency of the reference channel or phase. interval flagged? returns TRUE if any value during the interval is flagged. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. |
|  | frequency returns aggregated frequency of the reference channel or phase. |
|  | interval flagged? returns TRUE if any value during the interval is flagged. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | 3s values returns aggregated values for the three seconds period. data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. signaling voltage RMS values returns aggregated mains signalling voltage on the supply voltage according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. interval flagged? returns TRUE if any value during the interval is flagged. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | data valid? returns TRUE if the aggregated data is valid. |
|  | signaling voltage RMS values returns aggregated mains signalling voltage on the supply voltage according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | interval flagged? returns TRUE if any value during the interval is flagged. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel or phase. |
|  | aggregated data timestamp returns the end time of the aggregation interval. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

## TOPIC 00047: Energy Values VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `energy-values-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/energy-values-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power & Energy VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates energy values for a specified interval length. This VI returns apparent, active, and reactive energy values. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. Details Examples reset? specifies whether to

### Energy Values VI

**Owning Palette:** Power & Energy VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates energy values for a specified interval length. This VI returns apparent, active, and reactive energy values.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Energy_Values.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | power values specifies power values for calculating energy values. active power specifies the active power value in watts. Each element of active power corresponds to one channel. reactive power specifies the reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. Each element of reactive power corresponds to one channel. apparent power specifies the apparent power value in volt-amperes. Each element of apparent power corresponds to one channel. fundamental active power specifies the fundamental active power value in watts. Each element of fundamental active power corresponds to one channel. fundamental reactive power specifies the fundamental reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. Each element of fundamental reactive power corresponds to one channel. cosine specifies the fundamental power factor. Each element of cosine corresponds to one channel. power factor specifies the power factor value. Each element of power factor corresponds to one channel. |
|  | active power specifies the active power value in watts. Each element of active power corresponds to one channel. |
|  | reactive power specifies the reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. Each element of reactive power corresponds to one channel. |
|  | apparent power specifies the apparent power value in volt-amperes. Each element of apparent power corresponds to one channel. |
|  | fundamental active power specifies the fundamental active power value in watts. Each element of fundamental active power corresponds to one channel. |
|  | fundamental reactive power specifies the fundamental reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. Each element of fundamental reactive power corresponds to one channel. |
|  | cosine specifies the fundamental power factor. Each element of cosine corresponds to one channel. |
|  | power factor specifies the power factor value. Each element of power factor corresponds to one channel. |
|  | energy accumulation specifies whether this VI calculates energy values through a basic measurement time interval or by accumulating from start or restart of the measurement. 0Interval only (default)1Accumulated |
| 0 | Interval only (default) |
| 1 | Accumulated |
|  | interval length specifies the interval length, in seconds, of the input power values. The default is 0.2. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | energy values returns energy values calculated through a basic measurement time interval or accumulated from the start or restart of the measurement. active energy returns the active energy value in watt-hours. reactive energy returns the reactive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. apparent energy returns the apparent energy value in volt-ampere hours. fundamental active energy returns the fundamental active energy value in watt hours. fundamental reactive energy returns the fundamental reactive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. active positive energy returns the active positive energy value in watt hours. active negative energy returns the active negative energy value in watt hours. reactive inductive energy returns the reactive inductive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. reactive capacitive energy returns the reactive capacitive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. |
|  | active energy returns the active energy value in watt-hours. |
|  | reactive energy returns the reactive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. |
|  | apparent energy returns the apparent energy value in volt-ampere hours. |
|  | fundamental active energy returns the fundamental active energy value in watt hours. |
|  | fundamental reactive energy returns the fundamental reactive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. |
|  | active positive energy returns the active positive energy value in watt hours. |
|  | active negative energy returns the active negative energy value in watt hours. |
|  | reactive inductive energy returns the reactive inductive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. |
|  | reactive capacitive energy returns the reactive capacitive energy value in volt-ampere-reactive hours. |
|  | accumulation length returns the length of the accumulation process in seconds. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Energy Values Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Energy Values VI:

- Power & Energy (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Power & Energy VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Power & Energy VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

Power & Energy VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=energy-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00048: Energy VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `energy-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/energy-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Measurement VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the energy value from the power value for a specified interval. This VI returns the value of active energy, apparent energy, and reactive energy. Wire data to the power value input to determine the polymorphic instan

### Energy VI

**Owning Palette:** Power Measurement VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the energy value from the power value for a specified interval. This VI returns the value of active energy, apparent energy, and reactive energy. Wire data to the **power value** input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Energy (1 Channel)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Energy__1_Channel.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | power value specifies power values for calculating energy values. active power specifies the value of active power in watts. apparent power specifies the value of apparent power in volt-amperes. reactive power specifies the value of reactive power in volt-ampere reactives. power factor specifies the value of power factor. |
|  | active power specifies the value of active power in watts. |
|  | apparent power specifies the value of apparent power in volt-amperes. |
|  | reactive power specifies the value of reactive power in volt-ampere reactives. |
|  | power factor specifies the value of power factor. |
|  | interval length specifies the interval length, in seconds, of the power values. The default is 0.2. |
|  | start timestamp specifies the start time for calculating energy values. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | energy value returns calculated energy values. active energy returns the value of active energy in watt-hours. apparent energy returns the value of apparent energy in volt-ampere-hours. reactive energy returns the value of reactive energy in volt-ampere-reactive-hours. |
|  | active energy returns the value of active energy in watt-hours. |
|  | apparent energy returns the value of apparent energy in volt-ampere-hours. |
|  | reactive energy returns the value of reactive energy in volt-ampere-reactive-hours. |
|  | measurement timestamps returns the start and end timestamps of the energy calculation process. start timestamp returns the start time of the energy calculation process. end timestamp returns the end time of the energy calculation process. |
|  | start timestamp returns the start time of the energy calculation process. |
|  | end timestamp returns the end time of the energy calculation process. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Energy (N Channels)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Energy__N_Channels.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | power values specifies power values for calculating energy values for each channel. active power specifies the value of active power in watts. apparent power specifies the value of apparent power in volt-amperes. reactive power specifies the value of reactive power in volt-ampere reactives. power factor specifies the value of power factor. |
|  | active power specifies the value of active power in watts. |
|  | apparent power specifies the value of apparent power in volt-amperes. |
|  | reactive power specifies the value of reactive power in volt-ampere reactives. |
|  | power factor specifies the value of power factor. |
|  | interval length specifies the interval length, in seconds, of the power values. The default is 0.2. |
|  | start timestamp specifies the start time for calculating energy values. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | energy values returns calculated energy values for each channel. active energy returns the value of active energy in watt-hours. apparent energy returns the value of apparent energy in volt-ampere-hours. reactive energy returns the value of reactive energy in volt-ampere-reactive-hours. |
|  | active energy returns the value of active energy in watt-hours. |
|  | apparent energy returns the value of apparent energy in volt-ampere-hours. |
|  | reactive energy returns the value of reactive energy in volt-ampere-reactive-hours. |
|  | measurement timestamps returns the start and end timestamps of the energy calculation process. start timestamp returns the start time of the energy calculation process. end timestamp returns the end time of the energy calculation process. |
|  | start timestamp returns the start time of the energy calculation process. |
|  | end timestamp returns the end time of the energy calculation process. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Energy VI:

- Basic Power Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement
- Advanced Power Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement

## TOPIC 00050: EV Power Test VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `ev-power-test-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/ev-power-test-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Electrical Power VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the EV Power Test VIs to perform power tests on electric vehicles. SubpaletteDescriptionBa

### EV Power Test VIs

**Owning Palette:** Electrical Power VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the EV Power Test VIs to perform power tests on electric vehicles.

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Battery VIs | Use the Battery VIs to perform measurements on the battery pack of an electric vehicle. |
| Three Phase Power Measurement VIs | Use the Three Phase Power Measurement VIs to perform power measurements on three-phase, nonsinusoidal signals as defined in IEEE Std 1459-2010, IEEE Standard Definitions for the Measurement of Electric Power Quantities Under Sinusoidal, Nonsinusoidal, Balanced, or Unbalanced Conditions. |

Parent topic:

Electrical Power VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=events-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00051: Events VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `events-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/events-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Quality VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Events VIs to detect voltage dips, swells, interruptions, and rapid change events as defi

### Events VIs

**Owning Palette:** Power Quality VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Events VIs to detect voltage dips, swells, interruptions, and rapid change events as defined in IEC 61000-4-30:2008.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Rapid Voltage Changes | Detects rapid RMS voltage changes between two steady state conditions according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. The variation in voltage must not exceed the voltage dip threshold, the voltage swell threshold, or the thresholds that contain both voltage dip and voltage swell. EN 50160:2007 defines default threshold values. You also can specify custom threshold values. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. |
| Voltage Dips, Swells, and Interruptions | Detects RMS voltage dips, swells, and interruptions, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. EN 50160:2007 defines the default threshold values. You also can specify custom threshold values. You can use EN 50160:2007 to evaluate voltage events that this VI detects. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. |

Parent topic:

Power Quality VIs

### File I/O VIs

**Owning Palette:** Electrical Power VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the File I/O VIs to log data for electrical power analysis.

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| COMTRADE VIs | Use the COMTRADE VIs to log data using the Common Format for Transient Data Exchange (COMTRADE) format as defined in IEEE Std C37.111-1999. |

## TOPIC 00053: Flicker VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `flicker-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/flicker-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Quality VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Flicker VIs to measure instantaneous flicker sensation, short-term flicker severity, and

### Flicker VIs

**Owning Palette:** Power Quality VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Flicker VIs to measure instantaneous flicker sensation, short-term flicker severity, and long-term flicker severity as defined in IEC 61000-4-15:2010.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Instantaneous Flicker Sensation | Calculates the instantaneous flicker values of a voltage waveform, according to IEC 61000-4-15:2010. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. |
| Long-Term Flicker Severity | Aggregates short-term flicker values and returns the long-term flicker of a voltage waveform, according to IEC 61000-4-15:2010. Long-term flicker is the cubic average of short-term flicker values recorded over a two-hour period. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. |
| Short-Term Flicker Severity | Aggregates instantaneous flicker values and returns the short-term flicker of a voltage waveform for a 10-minute period, according to IEC 61000-4-15:2010. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. |

Parent topic:

Power Quality VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=fpga-definite-time-delay-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00054: FPGA Definite Time Delay VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `fpga-definite-time-delay-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/fpga-definite-time-delay-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Protection VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Delays for the time interval that you specify in time delay settings, which includes the operate time and the reset time. Use this VI to build a protection relay with definite time delay characteristics, such as a 27 undervoltage rela

### FPGA Definite Time Delay VI

**Owning Palette:** Protection VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Delays for the time interval that you specify in **time delay settings**, which includes the operate time and the reset time. Use this VI to build a protection relay with definite time delay characteristics, such as a 27 undervoltage relay, 50 instantaneous overcurrent relay, 59 overvoltage relay, and 81 frequency relay. You can compensate for the processing delay of the input energizing quantity to achieve higher operate time accuracy.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/FPGA_Definite_Time_Delay.gif']

|  | number of channels specifies the number of channels of the input data. The default is 1. This VI supports up to 16 channels. |
| --- | --- |
|  | pick-up signal? specifies whether the input energizing quantity exceeds or falls below a pick-up setting value. The default is FALSE. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel pick-up signal? must be interleaved. |
|  | time delay settings specifies the settings of operate time, reset time, and operate time compensation. operate time specifies the time period, in milliseconds, from the instant when pick-up signal? turns to TRUE, to the instant when trip signal? turns to TRUE. If operate time is smaller than operate time compensation, trip signal? turns to TRUE at the instant when pick-up signal? turns to TRUE. reset time specifies the time period, in milliseconds, from the instant when pick-up signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE, to the instant when trip signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE. operate time compensation specifies the time, in milliseconds, to compensate for the processing delay of the input energizing quantity to achieve higher operate time accuracy. The processing delay of the input energizing quantity may include the internal measurement delay, the delay of the digital I/O (DIO) modules, and so on. |
|  | operate time specifies the time period, in milliseconds, from the instant when pick-up signal? turns to TRUE, to the instant when trip signal? turns to TRUE. If operate time is smaller than operate time compensation, trip signal? turns to TRUE at the instant when pick-up signal? turns to TRUE. |
|  | reset time specifies the time period, in milliseconds, from the instant when pick-up signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE, to the instant when trip signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE. |
|  | operate time compensation specifies the time, in milliseconds, to compensate for the processing delay of the input energizing quantity to achieve higher operate time accuracy. The processing delay of the input energizing quantity may include the internal measurement delay, the delay of the digital I/O (DIO) modules, and so on. |
|  | input valid? specifies whether the next data point has arrived for processing. The default is TRUE. Wire the output valid? output of an upstream node to this input to transfer data from the upstream node to this node. |
|  | instantaneous reset after tripping? specifies whether to instantaneously reset the time delay settings after tripping. The default is FALSE. If you set instantaneous reset after tripping? to TRUE, trip signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE at the instant when pick-up signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE. |
|  | trip signal? returns TRUE when the time delay completes. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel trip signal? is interleaved. |

Example

Refer to the Recloser Controller.lvproj in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Protection\Recloser Controller directory for an example of using the FPGA Definite Time Delay VI.

Parent topic:

Protection VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=fpga-inverse-time-delay-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00055: FPGA Inverse Time Delay VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `fpga-inverse-time-delay-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/fpga-inverse-time-delay-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Protection VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Delays for the time interval that you specify in the Inverse Time Curves VI before the trip signal asserts. The Inverse Time Curves VI generates inverse time curves for this VI to use. You can compensate for the processing delay of th

### FPGA Inverse Time Delay VI

**Owning Palette:** Protection VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Delays for the time interval that you specify in the Inverse Time Curves VI before the trip signal asserts. The Inverse Time Curves VI generates inverse time curves for this VI to use. You can compensate for the processing delay of the input energizing quantity under a specific G/Gs in the Inverse Time Curves VI to achieve higher operate time accuracy.

**Related Information**
Handshaking Protocol

Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

FPGA Inverse Time Delay (Dependent Reset)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/FPGA_Inverse_Time_Delay__Dependent_Reset.gif']

|  | memory info of curve specifies the memory information of the curve representation. reference specifies the FPGA memory reference as <+,51,51> fixed-point numbers. You must use the memory initial values output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to initialize the memory before you run this VI. size specifies the memory size of reference. You must wire the memory size output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to size. The default is 240. |
| --- | --- |
|  | reference specifies the FPGA memory reference as <+,51,51> fixed-point numbers. You must use the memory initial values output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to initialize the memory before you run this VI. |
|  | size specifies the memory size of reference. You must wire the memory size output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to size. The default is 240. |
|  | number of channels specifies the number of channels of the input data. The default is 1. This VI supports up to 16 channels. |
|  | pick-up signal? specifies whether the input energizing quantity G exceeds or falls below a pick-up setting value Gs. The default is FALSE. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel pick-up signal? must be interleaved. |
|  | G specifies the value of the input energizing quantity. G and Gs must have the same scale. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel G must be interleaved. |
|  | Gs specifies the pick-up setting value. G and Gs must have the same scale. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel Gs must be interleaved. |
|  | input valid? specifies whether the next data point has arrived for processing. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the output valid? output of an upstream node to this input to transfer data from the upstream node to this node. |
|  | ready for output? specifies whether downstream nodes are ready for this node to return a new value. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the ready for input? output of a downstream node through a Feedback Node to this input of the current node. Note If this input is FALSE during a given cycle, the output valid? output returns FALSE during that cycle. |
|  | instantaneous reset after tripping? specifies whether to instantaneously reset the time delay settings after tripping. The default is FALSE. If you set instantaneous reset after tripping? to TRUE, trip signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE at the instant when pick-up signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE. |
|  | trip signal? returns TRUE when the time delay completes. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel trip signal? is interleaved. |
|  | output valid? returns TRUE when this node has computed a result that downstream nodes can use. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output to the input valid? input of a downstream node to transfer data from the node to the downstream node. |
|  | error returns error information about the delaying process. |
|  | ready for input? returns TRUE when this node is ready to accept new input data. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output through a Feedback Node to the ready for output? input of an upstream node. Note If this output returns FALSE during a given cycle, LabVIEW discards any data that other nodes send to this node during the following cycle. LabVIEW discards this data even if the input valid? input is TRUE during the following cycle. |

FPGA Inverse Time Delay (Definite Reset)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/FPGA_Inverse_Time_Delay__Definite_Reset.gif']

|  | memory info of curve specifies the memory information of the curve representation. reference specifies the FPGA memory reference as <+,51,51> fixed-point numbers. You must use the memory initial values output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to initialize the memory before you run this VI. size specifies the memory size of reference. You must wire the memory size output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to size. The default is 240. |
| --- | --- |
|  | reference specifies the FPGA memory reference as <+,51,51> fixed-point numbers. You must use the memory initial values output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to initialize the memory before you run this VI. |
|  | size specifies the memory size of reference. You must wire the memory size output of the Inverse Time Curves VI to size. The default is 240. |
|  | number of channels specifies the number of channels of the input data. The default is 1. This VI supports up to 16 channels. |
|  | pick-up signal? specifies whether the input energizing quantity G exceeds or falls below a pick-up setting value Gs. The default is FALSE. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel pick-up signal? must be interleaved. |
|  | G specifies the value of the input energizing quantity. G and Gs must have the same scale. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel G must be interleaved. |
|  | Gs specifies the pick-up setting value. G and Gs must have the same scale. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel Gs must be interleaved. |
|  | input valid? specifies whether the next data point has arrived for processing. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the output valid? output of an upstream node to this input to transfer data from the upstream node to this node. |
|  | ready for output? specifies whether downstream nodes are ready for this node to return a new value. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the ready for input? output of a downstream node through a Feedback Node to this input of the current node. Note If this input is FALSE during a given cycle, the output valid? output returns FALSE during that cycle. |
|  | instantaneous reset after tripping? specifies whether to instantaneously reset the time delay settings after tripping. The default is FALSE. If you set instantaneous reset after tripping? to TRUE, trip signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE at the instant when pick-up signal? turns from TRUE to FALSE. |
|  | trip signal? returns TRUE when the time delay completes. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel trip signal? is interleaved. |
|  | output valid? returns TRUE when this node has computed a result that downstream nodes can use. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output to the input valid? input of a downstream node to transfer data from the node to the downstream node. |
|  | error returns error information about the delaying process. |
|  | ready for input? returns TRUE when this node is ready to accept new input data. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output through a Feedback Node to the ready for output? input of an upstream node. Note If this output returns FALSE during a given cycle, LabVIEW discards any data that other nodes send to this node during the following cycle. LabVIEW discards this data even if the input valid? input is TRUE during the following cycle. |

Example

Refer to the Recloser Controller.lvproj in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Protection\Recloser Controller directory for an example of using the FPGA Inverse Time Delay VI.

Parent topic:

Protection VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=fpga-measurement-for-protection-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00056: FPGA Measurement for Protection VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `fpga-measurement-for-protection-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/fpga-measurement-for-protection-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Protection VIs Computes the instantaneous magnitude, phase, and frequency values of the fundamental frequency component for protection applications. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. You must use the FPGA Resampling VI to resample even-time-spaced voltage and current signals i

### FPGA Measurement for Protection VI

**Owning Palette:** Protection VIs

Computes the instantaneous magnitude, phase, and frequency values of the fundamental frequency component for protection applications. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. You must use the FPGA Resampling VI to resample even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals before running this VI.

**Related Information**
Handshaking Protocol

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/FPGA_Measurement_for_Protection.gif']

|  | number of channels specifies the number of channels of the input data. The default is 1. This VI supports up to 16 channels. |
| --- | --- |
|  | resampled data specifies information about the instantaneous resampled data that you acquire from the FPGA Resampling VI. The resampling rate must be 192 samples per cycle. signal specifies the instantaneous resampled signal at even-spaced angle intervals. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel resampled signal must be interleaved. timestamp specifies the timestamp, in seconds, of the resampled signal. frequency specifies the instantaneous frequency at the timestamp of the resampled signal. |
|  | signal specifies the instantaneous resampled signal at even-spaced angle intervals. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel resampled signal must be interleaved. |
|  | timestamp specifies the timestamp, in seconds, of the resampled signal. |
|  | frequency specifies the instantaneous frequency at the timestamp of the resampled signal. |
|  | resampling info specifies information about the measurement configuration for the resampled data. You acquire the resampled data from the FPGA Resampling VI. nominal frequency specifies the nominal line frequency of the resampled signal. The default is 50 Hz. resampling valid? specifies whether the resampled signal is valid. The default is FALSE. samples per cycle specifies the number of samples per cycle used for the resampling process. The default is 192. |
|  | nominal frequency specifies the nominal line frequency of the resampled signal. The default is 50 Hz. |
|  | resampling valid? specifies whether the resampled signal is valid. The default is FALSE. |
|  | samples per cycle specifies the number of samples per cycle used for the resampling process. The default is 192. |
|  | input valid? specifies whether the next data point has arrived for processing. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the output valid? output of an upstream node to this input to transfer data from the upstream node to this node. |
|  | ready for output? specifies whether downstream nodes are ready for this node to return a new value. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the ready for input? output of a downstream node through a Feedback Node to this input of the current node. Note If this input is FALSE during a given cycle, the output valid? output returns FALSE during that cycle. |
|  | output phasor returns the instantaneous magnitude and phase values. magnitude returns the instantaneous magnitude, in RMS value, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. Note If you scaled the input data from the data type of the analog input (AI) module to <±,24,1> fixed-point, you must scale the output back using the same scale factor. phase returns the instantaneous phase, in pi radians, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. phasor timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of the instantaneous even-angle-spaced magnitude and phase values. |
|  | magnitude returns the instantaneous magnitude, in RMS value, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. Note If you scaled the input data from the data type of the analog input (AI) module to <±,24,1> fixed-point, you must scale the output back using the same scale factor. |
|  | phase returns the instantaneous phase, in pi radians, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. |
|  | phasor timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of the instantaneous even-angle-spaced magnitude and phase values. |
|  | output frequency returns the instantaneous frequency value. frequency returns the instantaneous frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. frequency timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of the instantaneous even-angle-spaced frequency value. The value of frequency timestamp differs from the value of phasor timestamp because the FPGA Resampling VI, rather than this VI, measures the frequency. This VI just bundles the frequency value and the frequency timestamp into output frequency. |
|  | frequency returns the instantaneous frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. |
|  | frequency timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of the instantaneous even-angle-spaced frequency value. The value of frequency timestamp differs from the value of phasor timestamp because the FPGA Resampling VI, rather than this VI, measures the frequency. This VI just bundles the frequency value and the frequency timestamp into output frequency. |
|  | output valid? returns TRUE when this node has computed a result that downstream nodes can use. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output to the input valid? input of a downstream node to transfer data from the node to the downstream node. |
|  | error returns error information about resampled data and resampling info. |
|  | ready for input? returns TRUE when this node is ready to accept new input data. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output through a Feedback Node to the ready for output? input of an upstream node. Note If this output returns FALSE during a given cycle, LabVIEW discards any data that other nodes send to this node during the following cycle. LabVIEW discards this data even if the input valid? input is TRUE during the following cycle. |

Example

Refer to the Recloser Controller.lvproj in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Protection\Recloser Controller directory for an example of using the FPGA Measurement for Protection VI.

Parent topic:

Protection VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=fpga-phasor-measurement-for-m-class-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00057: FPGA Phasor Measurement for M Class VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `fpga-phasor-measurement-for-m-class-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/fpga-phasor-measurement-for-m-class-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Synchrophasor VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the instantaneous magnitude, phase, and frequency values of the fundamental frequency component as defined in the M class of performance in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. This VI resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current

### FPGA Phasor Measurement for M Class VI

**Owning Palette:** Synchrophasor VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the instantaneous magnitude, phase, and frequency values of the fundamental frequency component as defined in the M class of performance in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. This VI resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals. The input signals must have a sample rate of 50 kHz. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use.

**Related Information**
Handshaking Protocol

Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

FPGA Phasor Measurement for M Class (50 Hz)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/FPGA_Phasor_Measurement_for_M_Class__50_Hz.gif']

|  | memory information specifies two FPGA memory references and the reporting rate for a 50 Hz electrical power system. first memory reference specifies the first FPGA memory reference as <±,18,-4> fixed-point numbers. first memory reference has a memory size of 1215 elements. second memory reference specifies the second FPGA memory reference as <±,19,-3> fixed-point numbers. second memory reference has a memory size of 856 elements. reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 25 fps, and 50 fps for 50 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
| --- | --- |
|  | first memory reference specifies the first FPGA memory reference as <±,18,-4> fixed-point numbers. first memory reference has a memory size of 1215 elements. |
|  | second memory reference specifies the second FPGA memory reference as <±,19,-3> fixed-point numbers. second memory reference has a memory size of 856 elements. |
|  | reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 25 fps, and 50 fps for 50 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
|  | number of channels specifies the number of channels of the input data. The default is 1. This VI supports up to 16 channels. |
|  | signal in specifies the voltage signals or current signals whose sample rate must be 50 kHz. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel signal in must be interleaved. Note Because the input data type is <±,24,1> fixed-point and different analog acquisition modules have different fixed-point data types, the raw data from the data acquisition module must be divisible by a certain number so that the full range of the divided result can be represented as <±,24,1> fixed-point numbers without overflow. For example, the data type of NI 9225 is <±,24,10>, which means the raw data must be divisible by 512. Alternatively, you can right-shift the data by 9 bits and then convert the data type to <±,24,1> fixed-point. |
|  | timestamp specifies the timestamp, in seconds, of each sample of the input signal. The timestamp is the number of seconds since the International Atomic Time (TAI) epoch of 00:00:00, January 1, 1970, UTC time. The timestamp is not adjusted for leap seconds. |
|  | reference channel index specifies the channel of the input data that this VI uses as a reference to detect the resampling time and frequency. The default is 0. This VI usually uses a voltage channel as the reference channel. reference channel index must be less than number of channels. |
|  | input valid? specifies whether the next data point has arrived for processing. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the output valid? output of an upstream node to this input to transfer data from the upstream node to this node. |
|  | ready for output? specifies whether downstream nodes are ready for this node to return a new value. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the ready for input? output of a downstream node through a Feedback Node to this input of the current node. Note If this input is FALSE during a given cycle, the output valid? output returns FALSE during that cycle. |
|  | output data returns the instantaneous magnitude, phase, frequency, and timestamp values. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. magnitude returns the instantaneous magnitude, in RMS value, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. Note If you scaled the input data from the data type of the analog input (AI) module to <±,24,1> fixed-point, you must scale the output back using the same scale factor. phase returns the instantaneous phase, in pi radians, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of each sample of the output signal. The timestamp is the number of seconds since the International Atomic Time (TAI) epoch of 00:00:00, January 1, 1970, UTC time. The timestamp is not adjusted for leap seconds. frequency returns the instantaneous frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. |
|  | magnitude returns the instantaneous magnitude, in RMS value, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. Note If you scaled the input data from the data type of the analog input (AI) module to <±,24,1> fixed-point, you must scale the output back using the same scale factor. |
|  | phase returns the instantaneous phase, in pi radians, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. |
|  | timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of each sample of the output signal. The timestamp is the number of seconds since the International Atomic Time (TAI) epoch of 00:00:00, January 1, 1970, UTC time. The timestamp is not adjusted for leap seconds. |
|  | frequency returns the instantaneous frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. |
|  | output valid? returns TRUE when this node has computed a result that downstream nodes can use. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output to the input valid? input of a downstream node to transfer data from the node to the downstream node. |
|  | error returns error information about the phasor measurement process. |
|  | ready for input? returns TRUE when this node is ready to accept new input data. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output through a Feedback Node to the ready for output? input of an upstream node. Note If this output returns FALSE during a given cycle, LabVIEW discards any data that other nodes send to this node during the following cycle. LabVIEW discards this data even if the input valid? input is TRUE during the following cycle. |

FPGA Phasor Measurement for M Class (60 Hz)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/FPGA_Phasor_Measurement_for_M_Class__60_Hz.gif']

|  | memory information specifies two FPGA memory references and the reporting rate for a 60 Hz electrical power system. first memory reference specifies the first FPGA memory reference as <±,18,-4> fixed-point numbers. first memory reference has a memory size of 1321 elements. second memory reference specifies the second FPGA memory reference as <±,19,-3> fixed-point numbers. second memory reference has a memory size of 1188 elements. reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 12 fps, 15 fps, 20 fps, 30 fps, and 60 fps for 60 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
| --- | --- |
|  | first memory reference specifies the first FPGA memory reference as <±,18,-4> fixed-point numbers. first memory reference has a memory size of 1321 elements. |
|  | second memory reference specifies the second FPGA memory reference as <±,19,-3> fixed-point numbers. second memory reference has a memory size of 1188 elements. |
|  | reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 12 fps, 15 fps, 20 fps, 30 fps, and 60 fps for 60 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
|  | number of channels specifies the number of channels of the input data. The default is 1. This VI supports up to 16 channels. |
|  | signal in specifies the voltage signals or current signals whose sample rate must be 50 kHz. When number of channels is greater than 2, the multichannel signal in must be interleaved. Note Because the input data type is <±,24,1> fixed-point and different analog acquisition modules have different fixed-point data types, the raw data from the data acquisition module must be divisible by a certain number so that the full range of the divided result can be represented as <±,24,1> fixed-point numbers without overflow. For example, the data type of NI 9225 is <±,24,10>, which means the raw data must be divisible by 512. Alternatively, you can right-shift the data by 9 bits and then convert the data type to <±,24,1> fixed-point. |
|  | timestamp specifies the timestamp, in seconds, of each sample of the input signal. The timestamp is the number of seconds since the International Atomic Time (TAI) epoch of 00:00:00, January 1, 1970, UTC time. The timestamp is not adjusted for leap seconds. |
|  | reference channel index specifies the channel of the input data that this VI uses as a reference to detect the resampling time and frequency. The default is 0. This VI usually uses a voltage channel as the reference channel. reference channel index must be less than number of channels. |
|  | input valid? specifies whether the next data point has arrived for processing. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the output valid? output of an upstream node to this input to transfer data from the upstream node to this node. |
|  | ready for output? specifies whether downstream nodes are ready for this node to return a new value. The default is TRUE. Use a handshaking protocol to wire the ready for input? output of a downstream node through a Feedback Node to this input of the current node. Note If this input is FALSE during a given cycle, the output valid? output returns FALSE during that cycle. |
|  | output data returns the instantaneous magnitude, phase, frequency, and timestamp values. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. magnitude returns the instantaneous magnitude, in RMS value, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. Note If you scaled the input data from the data type of the analog input (AI) module to <±,24,1> fixed-point, you must scale the output back using the same scale factor. phase returns the instantaneous phase, in pi radians, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of each sample of the output signal. The timestamp is the number of seconds since the International Atomic Time (TAI) epoch of 00:00:00, January 1, 1970, UTC time. The timestamp is not adjusted for leap seconds. frequency returns the instantaneous frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. |
|  | magnitude returns the instantaneous magnitude, in RMS value, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. Note If you scaled the input data from the data type of the analog input (AI) module to <±,24,1> fixed-point, you must scale the output back using the same scale factor. |
|  | phase returns the instantaneous phase, in pi radians, of the fundamental frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. |
|  | timestamp returns the timestamp, in seconds, of each sample of the output signal. The timestamp is the number of seconds since the International Atomic Time (TAI) epoch of 00:00:00, January 1, 1970, UTC time. The timestamp is not adjusted for leap seconds. |
|  | frequency returns the instantaneous frequency at even-spaced angle intervals. |
|  | output valid? returns TRUE when this node has computed a result that downstream nodes can use. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output to the input valid? input of a downstream node to transfer data from the node to the downstream node. |
|  | error returns error information about the phasor measurement process. |
|  | ready for input? returns TRUE when this node is ready to accept new input data. Use a handshaking protocol to wire this output through a Feedback Node to the ready for output? input of an upstream node. Note If this output returns FALSE during a given cycle, LabVIEW discards any data that other nodes send to this node during the following cycle. LabVIEW discards this data even if the input valid? input is TRUE during the following cycle. |

Example

Refer to the Synchrophasor Measurement.lvproj in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Synchrophasor directory for an example of using the FPGA Phasor Measurement for M Class VI.

Parent topic:

Synchrophasor VIs

## TOPIC 00058: FPGA Phasor Measurement for P Class VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
