<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=get-range-utilization-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00072: Get Range Utilization VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `get-range-utilization-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/get-range-utilization-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Scaling & Calibration VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the range utilization, in percentage, of voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Range utilization is the ratio of the actual RMS value to the nominal RMS value o

### Get Range Utilization VI

**Owning Palette:** Scaling & Calibration VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the range utilization, in percentage, of voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Range utilization is the ratio of the actual RMS value to the nominal RMS value of the voltage or current.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Get_Range_Utilization.gif']

|  | waveforms in specifies the voltage waveforms, the current waveforms, or the waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | full-range RMS values specifies the RMS value of a 100% range utilization. full-range RMS values is in volts for voltage channels and in amperes for current channels. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | waveforms out returns waveforms in unchanged. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | actual ranges returns actual range utilization in percentages, which is the waveform amplitude relative to RMS full range. Each element of actual ranges corresponds to one element of waveforms in. |

Get Range Utilization Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Get Range Utilization VI:

- Calibration for Power Quality Measurement (Host) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Calibration
- Calibration for Power Quality Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Calibration (DAQmx)

Parent topic:

Scaling & Calibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=group-spectrum-lines-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00073: Group Spectrum Lines VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `group-spectrum-lines-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/group-spectrum-lines-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Groups 10/12-cycle spectra to harmonic and interharmonic spectra according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Use the Complex To Polar function to convert the results of this VI to magnitude and phase spectra. Use the Complex To Re/Im function to

### Group Spectrum Lines VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Groups 10/12-cycle spectra to harmonic and interharmonic spectra according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Use the Complex To Polar function to convert the results of this VI to magnitude and phase spectra. Use the Complex To Re/Im function to convert the results of this VI to real and imaginary spectra.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Group_Spectrum_Lines.gif']

|  | spectra specifies 10/12-cycle complex spectra. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | harmonic groups complex spectra returns complex spectra of harmonic groups with fundamental frequency step. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. vectors returns harmonic group represented as a vector. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | vectors returns harmonic group represented as a vector. |
|  | interharmonic groups complex spectra returns complex spectra of interharmonic groups with fundamental frequency step. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. vectors returns interharmonic group represented as a vector. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | vectors returns interharmonic group represented as a vector. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Group Spectrum Lines Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Group Spectrum Lines VI:

- Harmonics Analysis (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

## TOPIC 00074: Group THD and Subgroup THD VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `group-thd-and-subgroup-thd-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/group-thd-and-subgroup-thd-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates group total harmonic distortion and subgroup total harmonic distortion, in percentage, according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use. This VI supports measuring single-line and mul

### Group THD and Subgroup THD VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates group total harmonic distortion and subgroup total harmonic distortion, in percentage, according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

THDS

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/THDS.gif']

|  | harmonic subgroups complex spectra specifies complex spectra of harmonic subgroups with fundamental frequency step. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. vectors specifies harmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | vectors specifies harmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | THDS returns the subgroup total harmonic distortion value in percentages. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

THDG

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/THDG.gif']

|  | harmonic groups complex spectra specifies complex spectra of harmonic groups with fundamental frequency step. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. vectors specifies harmonic group represented as a vector. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | vectors specifies harmonic group represented as a vector. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | THDG returns the group total harmonic distortion value in percentages. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Group THD and Subgroup THD Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Group THD and Subgroup THD VI:

- Harmonics Analysis (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=harmonic-power-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00075: Harmonic Power VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `harmonic-power-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/harmonic-power-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the harmonic power values from the phase voltage spectra and the current spectra. Example voltage spectra specifies the complex phase voltage spectra up to a harmonic order that you specify in maximum harmonic order in the H

### Harmonic Power VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the harmonic power values from the phase voltage spectra and the current spectra.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Harmonic_Power.gif']

|  | voltage spectra specifies the complex phase voltage spectra up to a harmonic order that you specify in maximum harmonic order in the Harmonic Spectrum VI. Each element of this array corresponds to one voltage channel. Each array element of voltage spectra and the corresponding array element of current spectra must be in the same phase of the electrical power system. order 0 specifies the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. delta order specifies the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum specifies the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | order 0 specifies the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | delta order specifies the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
|  | current spectra specifies complex current spectra up to a harmonic order that you specify in maximum harmonic order in the Harmonic Spectrum VI. Each element of this array corresponds to one current channel. Each array element of current spectra and the corresponding array element of voltage spectra must be in the same phase of the electrical power system. order 0 specifies the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. delta order specifies the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum specifies the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
|  | order 0 specifies the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | delta order specifies the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | harmonic power values returns the harmonic power values of each harmonic. The number of rows corresponds to the number of channels in the harmonic spectra. The number of columns corresponds to maximum harmonic order in the Harmonic Spectrum VI. active power returns the active power value of each harmonic in watts. apparent power returns the apparent power value of each harmonic in volt-amperes. reactive power returns the reactive power value of each harmonic in volt-ampere reactives. power factor returns the power factor value of each harmonic. |
|  | active power returns the active power value of each harmonic in watts. |
|  | apparent power returns the apparent power value of each harmonic in volt-amperes. |
|  | reactive power returns the reactive power value of each harmonic in volt-ampere reactives. |
|  | power factor returns the power factor value of each harmonic. |
|  | power values returns the power values of each phase. Each element corresponds to one phase. active power returns the active power value of each phase in watts. apparent power returns the apparent power value of each phase in volt-amperes. reactive power returns the reactive power value of each phase in volt-ampere reactives. power factor returns the power factor value of each phase. |
|  | active power returns the active power value of each phase in watts. |
|  | apparent power returns the apparent power value of each phase in volt-amperes. |
|  | reactive power returns the reactive power value of each phase in volt-ampere reactives. |
|  | power factor returns the power factor value of each phase. |
|  | total power value returns the total power value. active power returns the total active power value in watts. apparent power returns the total apparent power value in volt-amperes. reactive power returns the total reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. power factor returns the total power factor. |
|  | active power returns the total active power value in watts. |
|  | apparent power returns the total apparent power value in volt-amperes. |
|  | reactive power returns the total reactive power value in volt-ampere reactives. |
|  | power factor returns the total power factor. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Advanced Power Measurement - Harmonics (DAQmx) VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement directory for an example of using the Harmonic Power VI.

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=harmonic-power-vi2.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00076: Harmonic Power VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `harmonic-power-vi2.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/harmonic-power-vi2.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Measures the harmonic real powers and harmonic reactive powers from cross power spectrum or cross power spectra inputs. For accurate measurements of harmonic power, this VI requires that all harmonics be resolvable within the spectrum. This requires at least four cycles of the electrical signal in t

### Harmonic Power VI

Measures the **harmonic real powers** and **harmonic reactive powers** from **cross power spectrum** or **cross power spectra** inputs. For accurate measurements of harmonic power, this VI requires that all harmonics be resolvable within the spectrum. This requires at least four cycles of the electrical signal in the original waveform segment. Harmonic components may indicate design and production characteristics that lead to decreased power conversion efficiency.

Wire data to **cross power spectrum** or **cross power spectra** to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Harmonic Power (1 Ch)

Measures the **harmonic real powers** and **harmonic reactive powers** from the input **cross power spectrum**. For accurate measurements of harmonic power, this VI requires that all harmonics be resolvable within the spectrum. This requires at least four cycles of the electrical signal in the original waveform segment. Harmonic components may indicate design and production characteristics that lead to decreased power conversion efficiency.

Wire data to **cross power spectrum** to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Harmonic_Power_(1_Ch).gif']

|  | cross power spectrum specifies the single-sided, complex cross power spectrum and signifies the real and reactive power at each spectral frequency. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum in Hz. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum in Hz. power specifies the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum in Hz. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum in Hz. |
|  | power specifies the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
|  | maximum harmonic specifies the maximum harmonic or highest multiple of the fundamental, to be measured. Specify a value of -1 to specify the highest available harmonic less than the Nyquist frequency. The default maximum harmonic is 20. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | electrical frequency specifies the expected electrical speed of the system in Hz. The default value -1 specifies that this VI will assume that it is processing exactly one cycle of fundamental component. 1 cycle of fundamental component (fundamental frequency = 1 / (dt*size(Y)=df). |
|  | harmonic real powers returns the real power for all harmonics between DC and input maximum harmonic. harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. real powers returns the real power, expressed in watts, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. |
|  | real powers returns the real power, expressed in watts, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | harmonic reactive powers returns the reactive power for all harmonics between DC and input maximum harmonic. harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. reactive powers returns the reactive power, in VAR, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. |
|  | reactive powers returns the reactive power, in VAR, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Harmonic Power (N Ch)

Measures the **harmonic real powers** and **harmonic reactive powers** from **cross power spectra**. For accurate measurements of harmonic power, this VI requires that all harmonics be resolvable within the spectrum. This requires at least four cycles of the electrical signal in the original waveform segment. Harmonic components may indicate design and production characteristics that lead to decreased power conversion efficiency.

Wire data to **cross power spectra** to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Harmonic_Power_(N_Ch).gif']

|  | cross power spectra specifies the cross power spectrum for each pair of signals. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum in Hz. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum in Hz. power specifies the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum in Hz. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum in Hz. |
|  | power specifies the complex power spectrum. The real component corresponds with the active power and the imaginary component corresponds with the reactive power. |
|  | maximum harmonic specifies the maximum harmonic or highest multiple of the fundamental, to be measured. Specify a value of -1 to specify the highest available harmonic less than the Nyquist frequency. The default maximum harmonic is 20. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | electrical frequency specifies the expected electrical speed of the system in Hz. The default value -1 specifies that this VI will assume that it is processing exactly one cycle of fundamental component. 1 cycle of fundamental component (fundamental frequency = 1 / (dt*size(Y)=df). |
|  | harmonic real powers returns the real power for all harmonics between DC and input maximum harmonic. harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. real powers returns the real power, expressed in watts, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. |
|  | real powers returns the real power, expressed in watts, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | harmonic reactive powers returns the reactive power for all harmonics between DC and input maximum harmonic. harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. reactive powers returns the reactive power, in VAR, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | harmonics returns the multiple of the fundamental frequency. 0.0 corresponds to DC, 1.0 corresponds to the fundamental frequency (f), 2.0 corresponds to 2*f, and so forth. |
|  | reactive powers returns the reactive power, in VAR, of the corresponding harmonic component. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=harmonic-spectrum-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00077: Harmonic Spectrum VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `harmonic-spectrum-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/harmonic-spectrum-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the fast Fourier transform (FFT) spectra from the resampled signals. This VI returns FFT spectra as complex spectra up to maximum harmonic order. Examples resampled data specifies the resampled signal for calculating the har

### Harmonic Spectrum VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the fast Fourier transform (FFT) spectra from the resampled signals. This VI returns FFT spectra as complex spectra up to **maximum harmonic order**.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Harmonic_Spectrum.gif']

|  | resampled data specifies the resampled signal for calculating the harmonic spectra. Wire the resampled data output of the Build Data Blocks VI to the resampled data input of this VI. times specifies the time of the resampled signal in each data block in seconds. resampled signal specifies the resampled signal for calculating the harmonic spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | times specifies the time of the resampled signal in each data block in seconds. |
|  | resampled signal specifies the resampled signal for calculating the harmonic spectra. |
|  | maximum harmonic order specifies the maximum harmonic order of the output harmonic spectra. The default is 50. maximum harmonic order must be less than or equal to (samples per cycle - 1) / 2. |
|  | samples per cycle specifies the number of samples per cycle in the resampled signals. The default is 192. You must set samples per cycle to be greater than or equal to (2 * maximum harmonic order) + 1. If you set samples per cycle too low, the measurement result might not be accurate enough. A greater samples per cycle value requires more real-time computation resources to process the resampled signal. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | harmonic spectra returns the complex spectra up to maximum harmonic order, including interharmonics. order 0 returns the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. delta order returns the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum returns the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
|  | order 0 returns the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | delta order returns the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum returns the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Harmonic Spectrum VI:

- Advanced Power Measurement - Harmonics (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement
- Advanced Motor Monitoring VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Motor Monitoring

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=harmonics-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00078: Harmonics VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `harmonics-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/harmonics-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Advanced VIs Requires: . This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Harmonics VIs to preprocess the voltage and current signals and then to perform harmonic analysis. Palette ObjectDescr

### Harmonics VIs

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** . This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Harmonics VIs to preprocess the voltage and current signals and then to perform harmonic analysis.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Build Data Blocks | Builds even-angle-spaced voltage and current signals into data blocks. |
| Harmonic Power | Calculates the harmonic power values from the phase voltage spectra and the current spectra. |
| Harmonic Spectrum | Calculates the fast Fourier transform (FFT) spectra from the resampled signals. This VI returns FFT spectra as complex spectra up to maximum harmonic order. |
| Resampling for Power Measurement | Resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals for harmonic analysis, according to the trigger times that you specify. |
| THD | Calculates the total harmonic distortion (THD), harmonic distortion factors, and total values from voltage harmonic spectra, current harmonic spectra, or harmonic spectra that contain both voltage and current. |

Parent topic:

Advanced VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=harmonics-vis2.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00079: Harmonics VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `harmonics-vis2.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/harmonics-vis2.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Quality VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Harmonics VIs to perform harmonic analysis according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009 and IEC 61000-

### Harmonics VIs

**Owning Palette:** Power Quality VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Harmonics VIs to perform harmonic analysis according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009 and IEC 61000-4-30:2008.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Fundamental Vector | Calculates the fundamental vector for each cycle contained in the input waveforms. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Group Spectrum Lines | Groups 10/12-cycle spectra to harmonic and interharmonic spectra according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Use the Complex To Polar function to convert the results of this VI to magnitude and phase spectra. Use the Complex To Re/Im function to convert the results of this VI to real and imaginary spectra. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Group THD and Subgroup THD | Calculates group total harmonic distortion and subgroup total harmonic distortion, in percentage, according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Spectrum | Calculates the fast Fourier transform (FFT) spectra from voltage signals, current signals, or signals that contain both voltage and current. This VI returns FFT spectra as complex spectra up to maximum harmonic order with a frequency resolution around 5 Hz. The frequency resolution is the fundamental frequency divided by 10 or 12. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Subgroup Spectrum Lines | Performs subgrouping of 10/12-cycle step spectra to harmonic and interharmonic spectra according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Use the Complex To Polar function to convert the results of this VI to magnitude and phase spectra. Use the Complex To Re/Im function to convert the results of this VI to real and imaginary spectra. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| THD | Calculates the total harmonic distortion (THD) of voltage spectra, harmonic spectra, or spectra that contain both voltage and current as defined in IEC 61000-4-7:2009. This VI calculates the THD for all harmonics up to the 40th order as defined in EN 50160:2007. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |

Parent topic:

Power Quality VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=initialize-system-std-2011-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00080: Initialize System (Std 2011) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `initialize-system-std-2011-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/initialize-system-std-2011-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Synchrophasor VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Sets the configuration for a phasor measurement unit (PMU) system as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Details Example Add to the block diagram Find on the palette performance class specifies the two p

### Initialize System (Std 2011) VI

**Owning Palette:** Synchrophasor VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Sets the configuration for a phasor measurement unit (PMU) system as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011.

Details Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Initialize_System__Std_2011.gif']

| Add to the block diagram | Find on the palette |
| --- | --- |

|  | performance class specifies the two performance classes of the PMU as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. performance class corresponds to the SVC_CLASS field in the CFG-3 frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0M Class (default)1P Class |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | M Class (default) |  |
| 1 | P Class |  |
|  | physical channels specifies the configuration of the physical channels. channel phase specifies the voltage or current phase. 0Voltage Phase A (L1) (default)1Voltage Phase B (L2)2Voltage Phase C (L3)3Voltage Phase N (Neutral)4Current Phase A (L1)5Current Phase B (L2)6Current Phase C (L3)7Current Phase N (Neutral)8Voltage Phase AB (L1L2)9Voltage Phase BC (L2L3)10Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) 3-phase system ID specifies the ID of the three-phase system that the physical channel belongs to. sensor sensitivity specifies the sensitivity of the sensors of the voltage/current transformer or transducer. The default is 1. sensor group delay specifies the delay, in seconds, that the data acquisition module or the voltage/current transformer or the voltage/current transducer introduces. |  |
|  | channel phase specifies the voltage or current phase. 0Voltage Phase A (L1) (default)1Voltage Phase B (L2)2Voltage Phase C (L3)3Voltage Phase N (Neutral)4Current Phase A (L1)5Current Phase B (L2)6Current Phase C (L3)7Current Phase N (Neutral)8Voltage Phase AB (L1L2)9Voltage Phase BC (L2L3)10Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) |  |
| 0 | Voltage Phase A (L1) (default) |  |
| 1 | Voltage Phase B (L2) |  |
| 2 | Voltage Phase C (L3) |  |
| 3 | Voltage Phase N (Neutral) |  |
| 4 | Current Phase A (L1) |  |
| 5 | Current Phase B (L2) |  |
| 6 | Current Phase C (L3) |  |
| 7 | Current Phase N (Neutral) |  |
| 8 | Voltage Phase AB (L1L2) |  |
| 9 | Voltage Phase BC (L2L3) |  |
| 10 | Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) |  |
|  | 3-phase system ID specifies the ID of the three-phase system that the physical channel belongs to. |  |
|  | sensor sensitivity specifies the sensitivity of the sensors of the voltage/current transformer or transducer. The default is 1. |  |
|  | sensor group delay specifies the delay, in seconds, that the data acquisition module or the voltage/current transformer or the voltage/current transducer introduces. |  |
|  | CFG-1 specifies the configuration frame 1 that indicates all the data that the PMU is capable of reporting as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. PMU ID specifies the ID of the PMU. PMU ID defines the two IDCODE fields of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can assign values within the range 1 to 65,534. Values 0 and 65,535 are reserved values. time base specifies the resolution of the FRACSEC field in all message frames. time base corresponds to the 4-byte TIME_BASE field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Bits 31–24 of TIME_BASE are reserved for flags. Bits 23–0 of TIME_BASE indicate the subdivision of the second that the FRACSEC field is based on. The FRACSEC field is the multiplication of the actual fractional second of the message frame by the TIME_BASE field. nominal frequency specifies the nominal line frequency of the input signal. nominal frequency corresponds to Bit 0 of the FNOM field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 050 HzCorresponds to the value 1 for Bit 0.160 Hz (default)Corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 0. reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). The default is 60. Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 25 fps, and 50 fps for 50 Hz nominal line frequency and 10 fps, 12 fps, 15 fps, 20 fps, 30 fps, and 60 fps for 60 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. station name specifies the station name by using no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format. station name corresponds to the STN field in the configuration frame as defined in IEEE C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. data format specifies the data format in the data frames. data format corresponds to the FORMAT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the following bits to define the data format in the data frames. Bits 15-4Unused. Bit 3Specifies the format of the FREQ and DFREQ fields in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 2Specifies the format of the ANALOG field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 1Specifies the format of the PHASORS field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 0Specifies the phasor format in the PHASORS field. 0 indicates the rectangular format and 1 indicates the polar format. phasor channels specifies the configuration of the reporting phasor channels. phasor name specifies the name of the phasor channel. phasor name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each phasor as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. phasor unit specifies the most significant byte of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the CFG-1 and CFG-2 configuration frames as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0Voltage [V] (default)Corresponds to the value 0.1Current [A]Corresponds to the value 1. phasor phase specifies the measurement phase of the phasor channel. When phasor unit is Voltage [V], the value of phasor phase and the value of channel phase of the corresponding physical channels determine the connection type of phasor channels. The following table lists the connection type based on different combinations of phasor phase and channel phase. Phasor Phase Channel Phase Connection Type Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase A (L1) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase AN) Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase AB (L1L2) Phase-to-Phase (Phase AB) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase B (L2) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase BN) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase BC (L2L3) Phase-to-Phase (Phase BC) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase C (L3) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase CN) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) Phase-to-Phase (Phase CA) The following table lists values of phasor phase. 0Phase A (L1) (default)1Phase B (L2)2Phase C (L3)3Phase N (Neutral)4Positive sequence5Negative sequence6Zero sequence 3-phase system ID specifies the ID of the three-phase system that the phasor channel belongs to. phasor unit scaling specifies the conversion factor for phasor channels. phasor unit scaling corresponds to the least significant three bytes of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. phasor unit scaling is an unsigned 24-bit integer, in 10–5 V or in amperes per bit, that scales 16-bit integer data. The default is 0186A0 in hexadecimal notation, which means the conversion factor is 1. If the transmitted data is in floating-point format, you can ignore phasor unit scaling by using the default value. analog channels specifies the configuration of the reporting analog channels. analog name specifies the name of the analog channel. analog name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each analog channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. analog unit specifies the conversion factor for analog channels. analog unit corresponds to the 4-byte ANUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the most significant byte to define the following items. Use the least significant three bytes, which are a signed 24-bit word, for user-defined scaling. 0Single point-on-wave 1RMS of the analog input 2Peak of the analog input 5-64Reserved for future definition 65-255User-defined values digital channels specifies the configuration of the reporting digital channels. digital names specifies the names of the digital channels in the status words. digital names uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each digital channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. digital unit specifies the mask words for digital status words. digital unit corresponds to the DIGUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. digital unit uses two 16-bit words for each digital word. The first 16-bit word indicates the normal status of the digital inputs by returning the logical exclusive or (XOR) result between the user-defined normal status and the status word. The second 16-bit word indicates the current valid digital inputs to the PMU, by setting the binary position of the digital input to 1 and setting the binary position of all the other bits to 0. You can customize the use of masks if you do not use digital status words as a Boolean. |  |
|  | PMU ID specifies the ID of the PMU. PMU ID defines the two IDCODE fields of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can assign values within the range 1 to 65,534. Values 0 and 65,535 are reserved values. |  |
|  | time base specifies the resolution of the FRACSEC field in all message frames. time base corresponds to the 4-byte TIME_BASE field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Bits 31–24 of TIME_BASE are reserved for flags. Bits 23–0 of TIME_BASE indicate the subdivision of the second that the FRACSEC field is based on. The FRACSEC field is the multiplication of the actual fractional second of the message frame by the TIME_BASE field. |  |
|  | nominal frequency specifies the nominal line frequency of the input signal. nominal frequency corresponds to Bit 0 of the FNOM field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 050 HzCorresponds to the value 1 for Bit 0.160 Hz (default)Corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 0. |  |
| 0 | 50 HzCorresponds to the value 1 for Bit 0. |  |
| 1 | 60 Hz (default)Corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 0. |  |
|  | reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). The default is 60. Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 25 fps, and 50 fps for 50 Hz nominal line frequency and 10 fps, 12 fps, 15 fps, 20 fps, 30 fps, and 60 fps for 60 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |  |
|  | station name specifies the station name by using no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format. station name corresponds to the STN field in the configuration frame as defined in IEEE C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. |  |
|  | data format specifies the data format in the data frames. data format corresponds to the FORMAT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the following bits to define the data format in the data frames. Bits 15-4Unused. Bit 3Specifies the format of the FREQ and DFREQ fields in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 2Specifies the format of the ANALOG field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 1Specifies the format of the PHASORS field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 0Specifies the phasor format in the PHASORS field. 0 indicates the rectangular format and 1 indicates the polar format. |  |
|  | phasor channels specifies the configuration of the reporting phasor channels. phasor name specifies the name of the phasor channel. phasor name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each phasor as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. phasor unit specifies the most significant byte of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the CFG-1 and CFG-2 configuration frames as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0Voltage [V] (default)Corresponds to the value 0.1Current [A]Corresponds to the value 1. phasor phase specifies the measurement phase of the phasor channel. When phasor unit is Voltage [V], the value of phasor phase and the value of channel phase of the corresponding physical channels determine the connection type of phasor channels. The following table lists the connection type based on different combinations of phasor phase and channel phase. Phasor Phase Channel Phase Connection Type Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase A (L1) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase AN) Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase AB (L1L2) Phase-to-Phase (Phase AB) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase B (L2) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase BN) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase BC (L2L3) Phase-to-Phase (Phase BC) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase C (L3) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase CN) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) Phase-to-Phase (Phase CA) The following table lists values of phasor phase. 0Phase A (L1) (default)1Phase B (L2)2Phase C (L3)3Phase N (Neutral)4Positive sequence5Negative sequence6Zero sequence 3-phase system ID specifies the ID of the three-phase system that the phasor channel belongs to. phasor unit scaling specifies the conversion factor for phasor channels. phasor unit scaling corresponds to the least significant three bytes of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. phasor unit scaling is an unsigned 24-bit integer, in 10–5 V or in amperes per bit, that scales 16-bit integer data. The default is 0186A0 in hexadecimal notation, which means the conversion factor is 1. If the transmitted data is in floating-point format, you can ignore phasor unit scaling by using the default value. |  |
|  | phasor name specifies the name of the phasor channel. phasor name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each phasor as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. |  |
|  | phasor unit specifies the most significant byte of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the CFG-1 and CFG-2 configuration frames as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0Voltage [V] (default)Corresponds to the value 0.1Current [A]Corresponds to the value 1. |  |
| 0 | Voltage [V] (default)Corresponds to the value 0. |  |
| 1 | Current [A]Corresponds to the value 1. |  |
|  | phasor phase specifies the measurement phase of the phasor channel. When phasor unit is Voltage [V], the value of phasor phase and the value of channel phase of the corresponding physical channels determine the connection type of phasor channels. The following table lists the connection type based on different combinations of phasor phase and channel phase. Phasor Phase Channel Phase Connection Type Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase A (L1) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase AN) Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase AB (L1L2) Phase-to-Phase (Phase AB) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase B (L2) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase BN) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase BC (L2L3) Phase-to-Phase (Phase BC) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase C (L3) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase CN) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) Phase-to-Phase (Phase CA) The following table lists values of phasor phase. 0Phase A (L1) (default)1Phase B (L2)2Phase C (L3)3Phase N (Neutral)4Positive sequence5Negative sequence6Zero sequence |  |
| Phasor Phase | Channel Phase | Connection Type |
| Phase A (L1) | Voltage Phase A (L1) | Phase-to-Neutral (Phase AN) |
| Phase A (L1) | Voltage Phase AB (L1L2) | Phase-to-Phase (Phase AB) |
| Phase B (L2) | Voltage Phase B (L2) | Phase-to-Neutral (Phase BN) |
| Phase B (L2) | Voltage Phase BC (L2L3) | Phase-to-Phase (Phase BC) |
| Phase C (L3) | Voltage Phase C (L3) | Phase-to-Neutral (Phase CN) |
| Phase C (L3) | Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) | Phase-to-Phase (Phase CA) |
| 0 | Phase A (L1) (default) |  |
| 1 | Phase B (L2) |  |
| 2 | Phase C (L3) |  |
| 3 | Phase N (Neutral) |  |
| 4 | Positive sequence |  |
| 5 | Negative sequence |  |
| 6 | Zero sequence |  |
|  | 3-phase system ID specifies the ID of the three-phase system that the phasor channel belongs to. |  |
|  | phasor unit scaling specifies the conversion factor for phasor channels. phasor unit scaling corresponds to the least significant three bytes of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. phasor unit scaling is an unsigned 24-bit integer, in 10–5 V or in amperes per bit, that scales 16-bit integer data. The default is 0186A0 in hexadecimal notation, which means the conversion factor is 1. If the transmitted data is in floating-point format, you can ignore phasor unit scaling by using the default value. |  |
|  | analog channels specifies the configuration of the reporting analog channels. analog name specifies the name of the analog channel. analog name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each analog channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. analog unit specifies the conversion factor for analog channels. analog unit corresponds to the 4-byte ANUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the most significant byte to define the following items. Use the least significant three bytes, which are a signed 24-bit word, for user-defined scaling. 0Single point-on-wave 1RMS of the analog input 2Peak of the analog input 5-64Reserved for future definition 65-255User-defined values |  |
|  | analog name specifies the name of the analog channel. analog name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each analog channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. |  |
|  | analog unit specifies the conversion factor for analog channels. analog unit corresponds to the 4-byte ANUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the most significant byte to define the following items. Use the least significant three bytes, which are a signed 24-bit word, for user-defined scaling. 0Single point-on-wave 1RMS of the analog input 2Peak of the analog input 5-64Reserved for future definition 65-255User-defined values |  |
|  | digital channels specifies the configuration of the reporting digital channels. digital names specifies the names of the digital channels in the status words. digital names uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each digital channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. digital unit specifies the mask words for digital status words. digital unit corresponds to the DIGUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. digital unit uses two 16-bit words for each digital word. The first 16-bit word indicates the normal status of the digital inputs by returning the logical exclusive or (XOR) result between the user-defined normal status and the status word. The second 16-bit word indicates the current valid digital inputs to the PMU, by setting the binary position of the digital input to 1 and setting the binary position of all the other bits to 0. You can customize the use of masks if you do not use digital status words as a Boolean. |  |
|  | digital names specifies the names of the digital channels in the status words. digital names uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each digital channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. |  |
|  | digital unit specifies the mask words for digital status words. digital unit corresponds to the DIGUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. digital unit uses two 16-bit words for each digital word. The first 16-bit word indicates the normal status of the digital inputs by returning the logical exclusive or (XOR) result between the user-defined normal status and the status word. The second 16-bit word indicates the current valid digital inputs to the PMU, by setting the binary position of the digital input to 1 and setting the binary position of all the other bits to 0. You can customize the use of masks if you do not use digital status words as a Boolean. |  |
|  | CFG-2 specifies the configuration frame 2 that indicates the measurements that the data frame is transmitting or reporting as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. PMU ID specifies the ID of the PMU. PMU ID defines the two IDCODE fields of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can assign values within the range 1 to 65,534. Values 0 and 65,535 are reserved values. time base specifies the resolution of the FRACSEC field in all message frames. time base corresponds to the 4-byte TIME_BASE field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Bits 31–24 of TIME_BASE are reserved for flags. Bits 23–0 of TIME_BASE indicate the subdivision of the second that the FRACSEC field is based on. The FRACSEC field is the multiplication of the actual fractional second of the message frame by the TIME_BASE field. nominal frequency specifies the nominal line frequency of the input signal. nominal frequency corresponds to Bit 0 of the FNOM field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 050 HzCorresponds to the value 1 for Bit 0.160 Hz (default)Corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 0. reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). The default is 60. Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 25 fps, and 50 fps for 50 Hz nominal line frequency and 10 fps, 12 fps, 15 fps, 20 fps, 30 fps, and 60 fps for 60 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. station name specifies the station name by using no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format. station name corresponds to the STN field in the configuration frame as defined in IEEE C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. data format specifies the data format in the data frames. data format corresponds to the FORMAT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the following bits to define the data format in the data frames. Bits 15-4Unused. Bit 3Specifies the format of the FREQ and DFREQ fields in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 2Specifies the format of the ANALOG field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 1Specifies the format of the PHASORS field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 0Specifies the phasor format in the PHASORS field. 0 indicates the rectangular format and 1 indicates the polar format. phasor channels specifies the configuration of the reporting phasor channels. phasor name specifies the name of the phasor channel. phasor name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each phasor as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. phasor unit specifies the most significant byte of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the CFG-1 and CFG-2 configuration frames as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0Voltage [V] (default)Corresponds to the value 0.1Current [A]Corresponds to the value 1. phasor phase specifies the measurement phase of the phasor channel. When phasor unit is Voltage [V], the value of phasor phase and the value of channel phase of the corresponding physical channels determine the connection type of phasor channels. The following table lists the connection type based on different combinations of phasor phase and channel phase. Phasor Phase Channel Phase Connection Type Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase A (L1) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase AN) Phase A (L1) Voltage Phase AB (L1L2) Phase-to-Phase (Phase AB) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase B (L2) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase BN) Phase B (L2) Voltage Phase BC (L2L3) Phase-to-Phase (Phase BC) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase C (L3) Phase-to-Neutral (Phase CN) Phase C (L3) Voltage Phase CA (L3L1) Phase-to-Phase (Phase CA) The following table lists values of phasor phase. 0Phase A (L1) (default)1Phase B (L2)2Phase C (L3)3Phase N (Neutral)4Positive sequence5Negative sequence6Zero sequence 3-phase system ID specifies the ID of the three-phase system that the phasor channel belongs to. phasor unit scaling specifies the conversion factor for phasor channels. phasor unit scaling corresponds to the least significant three bytes of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. phasor unit scaling is an unsigned 24-bit integer, in 10–5 V or in amperes per bit, that scales 16-bit integer data. The default is 0186A0 in hexadecimal notation, which means the conversion factor is 1. If the transmitted data is in floating-point format, you can ignore phasor unit scaling by using the default value. analog channels specifies the configuration of the reporting analog channels. analog name specifies the name of the analog channel. analog name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each analog channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. analog unit specifies the conversion factor for analog channels. analog unit corresponds to the 4-byte ANUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the most significant byte to define the following items. Use the least significant three bytes, which are a signed 24-bit word, for user-defined scaling. 0Single point-on-wave 1RMS of the analog input 2Peak of the analog input 5-64Reserved for future definition 65-255User-defined values digital channels specifies the configuration of the reporting digital channels. digital names specifies the names of the digital channels in the status words. digital names uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each digital channel as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. digital unit specifies the mask words for digital status words. digital unit corresponds to the DIGUNIT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. digital unit uses two 16-bit words for each digital word. The first 16-bit word indicates the normal status of the digital inputs by returning the logical exclusive or (XOR) result between the user-defined normal status and the status word. The second 16-bit word indicates the current valid digital inputs to the PMU, by setting the binary position of the digital input to 1 and setting the binary position of all the other bits to 0. You can customize the use of masks if you do not use digital status words as a Boolean. |  |
|  | PMU ID specifies the ID of the PMU. PMU ID defines the two IDCODE fields of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. You can assign values within the range 1 to 65,534. Values 0 and 65,535 are reserved values. |  |
|  | time base specifies the resolution of the FRACSEC field in all message frames. time base corresponds to the 4-byte TIME_BASE field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Bits 31–24 of TIME_BASE are reserved for flags. Bits 23–0 of TIME_BASE indicate the subdivision of the second that the FRACSEC field is based on. The FRACSEC field is the multiplication of the actual fractional second of the message frame by the TIME_BASE field. |  |
|  | nominal frequency specifies the nominal line frequency of the input signal. nominal frequency corresponds to Bit 0 of the FNOM field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 050 HzCorresponds to the value 1 for Bit 0.160 Hz (default)Corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 0. |  |
| 0 | 50 HzCorresponds to the value 1 for Bit 0. |  |
| 1 | 60 Hz (default)Corresponds to the value 0 for Bit 0. |  |
|  | reporting rate specifies the reporting frequency of the PMU in frames per second (fps). The default is 60. Valid reporting frequencies are 10 fps, 25 fps, and 50 fps for 50 Hz nominal line frequency and 10 fps, 12 fps, 15 fps, 20 fps, 30 fps, and 60 fps for 60 Hz nominal line frequency as defined in both IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |  |
|  | station name specifies the station name by using no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format. station name corresponds to the STN field in the configuration frame as defined in IEEE C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. |  |
|  | data format specifies the data format in the data frames. data format corresponds to the FORMAT field of the configuration frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. Use the following bits to define the data format in the data frames. Bits 15-4Unused. Bit 3Specifies the format of the FREQ and DFREQ fields in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 2Specifies the format of the ANALOG field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 1Specifies the format of the PHASORS field in the data frames. 0 indicates 16-bit integer and 1 indicates 32-bit floating-point. Bit 0Specifies the phasor format in the PHASORS field. 0 indicates the rectangular format and 1 indicates the polar format. |  |
|  | phasor channels specifies the configuration of the reporting phasor channels. phasor name specifies the name of the phasor channel. phasor name uses no more than 16 bytes in ASCII format for each phasor as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. The LabVIEW Electrical Power Toolkit does not support the UTF-8 format. phasor unit specifies the most significant byte of the 4-byte PHUNIT field of the CFG-1 and CFG-2 configuration frames as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. 0Voltage [V] (default)Corresponds to the value 0.1Current [A]Corresponds to the value 1. phasor phase specifies the measurement phase of the phasor channel. When phasor unit is Voltage [V], the value of phasor phase and the value of channel phase of the corresponding physical channels determine the connection type of phasor channels. The following table lists the connection type based on different combinations of phasor phase and channel phase. The default is 0186A0 in hexadecimal notation, which means the conversion factor is 1. If the transmitted data is in floating-point format, you can ignore phasor unit scaling by using the default value. |  |
