Utility VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=real-and-reactive-power-in-band-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00110: Real and Reactive Power in Band VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `real-and-reactive-power-in-band-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/real-and-reactive-power-in-band-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Measures the power in the specified frequency band. Real power is calculated from the sum of in-phase power, and reactive power is calculated from the sum of quadrature (imaginary) power. Use this VI to calculate the power in band from cross power spectrum and cross power spectra. This VI measures p

### Real and Reactive Power in Band VI

Measures the power in the specified frequency band. Real power is calculated from the sum of in-phase power, and reactive power is calculated from the sum of quadrature (imaginary) power. Use this VI to calculate the power in band from **cross power spectrum** and **cross power spectra**. This VI measures power accurately when processing the integer and noninteger cycles of the fundamental frequency component.

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Real and Reactive Power in Band (1 Ch)

Measures the power in the specified frequency band. Real power is calculated from the sum of in-phase power, and reactive power is calculated from the sum of quadrature (imaginary) power. Use this VI to calculate the power in band from **cross power spectrum**. This VI measures power accurately when processing the integer and noninteger cycles of the fundamental frequency component.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Real_and_Reactive_Power_in_Band_(1_Ch).gif']

|  | cross power spectrum specifies the single-sided, complex cross power spectrum and signifies the real and reactive power at each spectral frequency. f0 specifies the start frequency, in Hz. df specifies the frequency resolution, in Hz. power spectrum specifies the single-sided, complex power spectrum where, at each spectral frequency, the real component specifies the active power and the imaginary component specifies the reactive power. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in Hz. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in Hz. |
|  | power spectrum specifies the single-sided, complex power spectrum where, at each spectral frequency, the real component specifies the active power and the imaginary component specifies the reactive power. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | center frequency specifies the center frequency, in Hz, of the measurement frequency band. The default value -1 specifies use of bin with maximum apparent power as the center of the frequency band. |
|  | bandwidth specifies the width of the frequency band, expressed in spectral lines or bins. The default is 3 bins which is tight enough to capture energy from single frequency component when processing integer cycles. Increase bandwidth when processing noninteger cycles and leakage spreads energy across more bins. |
|  | real power in band returns the real power calculated in the specified frequency band, expressed in Watt. |
|  | reactive power in band returns the reactive power in the specified frequency band, expressed as VAR. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Real and Reactive Power in Band (N Ch)

Measures the power in the specified frequency band. Real power is calculated from the sum of in-phase power, and reactive power is calculated from the sum of quadrature (imaginary) power. Use this VI to calculate the powers in band from **cross power spectra**. This VI measures power accurately when processing the integer and noninteger cycles of the fundamental frequency component.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Real_and_Reactive_Power_in_Band_(N_Ch).gif']

|  | cross power spectra specifies the cross power spectrum for each pair of signals. f0 specifies the start frequency, in Hz. df specifies the frequency resolution, in Hz. power spectrum specifies the single-sided, complex power spectrum expressed in VA where, at each spectral frequency, the real component specifies the active power and the imaginary component specifies the reactive power. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency, in Hz. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution, in Hz. |
|  | power spectrum specifies the single-sided, complex power spectrum expressed in VA where, at each spectral frequency, the real component specifies the active power and the imaginary component specifies the reactive power. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | center frequency specifies the center frequency, in Hz, of the measurement frequency band. The default value -1 specifies use of bin with maximum apparent power as the center of the frequency band. |
|  | bandwidth specifies the width of the frequency band, expressed in spectral lines or bins. The default is 3 bins which is tight enough to capture energy from single frequency component when processing integer cycles. Increase bandwidth when processing noninteger cycles and leakage spreads energy across more bins. |
|  | real powers in band returns the real power calculated in the specified frequency band for each cross power spectrum, expressed in Watt. |
|  | reactive powers in band returns the reactive power in the specified frequency band for each cross power spectrum, expressed as VAR. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Parent topic:

Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=reference-signal-processing-zero-crossing-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00111: Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `reference-signal-processing-zero-crossing-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/reference-signal-processing-zero-crossing-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Advanced VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the zero crossing times and the average fundamental frequency of a reference signal. For common power measurement, use the To Integral Cycles VI to divide the voltage and current signals into integral cycles based on the zero

### Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the zero crossing times and the average fundamental frequency of a reference signal.

For common power measurement, use the To Integral Cycles VI to divide the voltage and current signals into integral cycles based on the zero crossing times you calculate with this VI. For harmonic analysis, use the Resampling for Power Measurement VI to resample the voltage and current signals based on the zero crossing times you calculate with this VI.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Reference_Signal_Processing__Zero_Crossing.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to redesign the lowpass filter coefficients, to reset the internal lowpass filter states to zero, and to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the filter and calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | reference signal specifies the input reference signal. reference signal must be synchronous with the voltage and current signals you want to measure. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | frequency range specifies the range of the fundamental frequency of the reference signal that this VI detects in hertz. minimum specifies the minimum fundamental frequency of the reference signal that this VI detects in hertz. The default is 45. If you set minimum too low, the To Integral Cycles VI and the Resampling for Power Measurement VI require a large amount of memory to process the voltage and current signals that you want to measure. maximum specifies the maximum fundamental frequency of the reference signal that this VI detects in hertz. The default is 55. |
|  | minimum specifies the minimum fundamental frequency of the reference signal that this VI detects in hertz. The default is 45. If you set minimum too low, the To Integral Cycles VI and the Resampling for Power Measurement VI require a large amount of memory to process the voltage and current signals that you want to measure. |
|  | maximum specifies the maximum fundamental frequency of the reference signal that this VI detects in hertz. The default is 55. |
|  | filter for frequency measurement specifies the settings of a lowpass filter. The lowpass filter attenuates the distortions in the reference signal for more accurate zero crossing times and average fundamental frequency. This VI redesigns the lowpass filter coefficients and resets the internal lowpass filter states to zero only when you call this VI for the first time or when you set reset? to TRUE. If you set the transition band too narrow, the To Integral Cycles VI and the Resampling for Power Measurement VI require a large amount of memory to process the voltage and current signals that you want to measure. enable filter? specifies whether to enable the lowpass filter. The default is TRUE. passband edge frequency specifies the passband edge frequency of the lowpass filter in hertz. The default is 55. passband ripple specifies the passband ripple of the lowpass filter in decibels. The default is 0.1. stopband edge frequency specifies the stopband edge frequency of the lowpass filter in hertz. The default is 85. stopband attenuation specifies the stopband attenuation of the lowpass filter in decibels. The default is 60. |
|  | enable filter? specifies whether to enable the lowpass filter. The default is TRUE. |
|  | passband edge frequency specifies the passband edge frequency of the lowpass filter in hertz. The default is 55. |
|  | passband ripple specifies the passband ripple of the lowpass filter in decibels. The default is 0.1. |
|  | stopband edge frequency specifies the stopband edge frequency of the lowpass filter in hertz. The default is 85. |
|  | stopband attenuation specifies the stopband attenuation of the lowpass filter in decibels. The default is 60. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | zero crossing information returns the information about zero crossings for the reference signal. zero crossing times returns the time, in seconds, when zero crossings of the reference signal occur. zero crossings per cycle returns the number of zero crossings in one cycle of the reference signal. |
|  | zero crossing times returns the time, in seconds, when zero crossings of the reference signal occur. |
|  | zero crossings per cycle returns the number of zero crossings in one cycle of the reference signal. |
|  | average frequency returns the average fundamental frequency of the reference signal in hertz. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | filtered reference signal returns the reference signal after filtering when enable filter? is TRUE. filtered reference signal returns the original reference signal when enable filter? is FALSE. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI:

- Advanced Power Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement
- Advanced Power Measurement - Harmonics (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement

Parent topic:

Advanced VIs

Examples circular buffer specifies the data value reference to the circular buffer. Data in the circular buffer will not be accessible after this VI executes. error in describes error conditions th

### Release (SGL) VI

Releases the circular buffer by deleting all data value references and requesting memory deallocation.

Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Release_(SGL).gif']

|  | circular buffer specifies the data value reference to the circular buffer. Data in the circular buffer will not be accessible after this VI executes. |
| --- | --- |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Release (SGL) VI.

- Traction Inverter Acquisition and Waveform Logging (DAQmx) VI: LabVIEW\examples\Electrical Power\EV Power Test\Traction Inverter

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=resampling-for-power-measurement-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00113: Resampling for Power Measurement VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `resampling-for-power-measurement-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/resampling-for-power-measurement-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals for harmonic analysis, according to the trigger times that you specify. If you set the minimum fundamental frequency too low or set the transition ba

### Resampling for Power Measurement VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals for harmonic analysis, according to the trigger times that you specify.

If you set the minimum fundamental frequency too low or set the transition band too narrow in the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI, this VI requires a large amount of memory to process the voltage and current signals.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Resampling_for_Power_Measurement.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the resampling process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the resampling process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the voltage or current signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | trigger information specifies the time and number of the triggers that this VI uses to resample the voltage and current signals. trigger times specifies the time, in seconds, when triggers occur. trigger times must be synchronous with data in. You can use zero crossing times that you calculate with the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI as trigger times for this VI. You also can use the pulse times that a tachometer generates as trigger times for this VI. triggers per cycle specifies the number of triggers in one cycle of the voltage and current signals. The default is 2. |
|  | trigger times specifies the time, in seconds, when triggers occur. trigger times must be synchronous with data in. You can use zero crossing times that you calculate with the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI as trigger times for this VI. You also can use the pulse times that a tachometer generates as trigger times for this VI. |
|  | triggers per cycle specifies the number of triggers in one cycle of the voltage and current signals. The default is 2. |
|  | samples per cycle specifies the number of samples per cycle for the resampling process. The default is 192. You must set samples per cycle to be greater than or equal to (2 * maximum harmonic order) + 1. If you set samples per cycle too low, the measurement result might not be accurate enough. A greater samples per cycle value requires more real-time computation resources to process the resampled signal. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | resampled data returns the resampled signal for harmonic analysis. resampled data is synchronous with the fundamental frequency with the number of samples per cycle that you specify in samples per cycle. times returns the time instants of the resampled signals in seconds. resampled signal returns the resampled voltage and current signals. |
|  | times returns the time instants of the resampled signals in seconds. |
|  | resampled signal returns the resampled voltage and current signals. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Advanced Power Measurement - Harmonics (DAQmx) VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement directory for an example of using the Resampling for Power Measurement VI.

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=resampling-for-power-quality-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00114: Resampling for Power Quality VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `resampling-for-power-quality-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/resampling-for-power-quality-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Preprocessing VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals. The output signals are synchronous with the line frequency with a constant number of samples per cycle. Use the FPGA Resampling VI if you want t

### Resampling for Power Quality VI

**Owning Palette:** Preprocessing VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals. The output signals are synchronous with the line frequency with a constant number of samples per cycle. Use the FPGA Resampling VI if you want to perform resampling on FPGA targets.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Resampling_for_Power_Quality.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the resampling process. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the resampling process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the even-time-spaced voltage and current signals. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | reference channel index specifies the channel of the input data that this VI uses as a reference to detect the resampling time and frequency. The default is 0. reference channel index must be less than the array size of data in. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | resampled data returns the resampled signal and its information. resampled signal returns the even-angle-spaced voltage and current signals. sample rate returns the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of the resampled signal. timestamp returns the timestamp of the end of the resampled signal. |
|  | resampled signal returns the even-angle-spaced voltage and current signals. |
|  | sample rate returns the instantaneous sample rate, in hertz, of the resampled signal. |
|  | timestamp returns the timestamp of the end of the resampled signal. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Resampling for Power Quality Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Resampling for Power Quality VI:

- labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)\Power Quality Applications (DAQmx).lvproj
- labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Calibration (DAQmx)\Calibration for Power Quality Measurement (DAQmx).lvproj
- Advanced Motor Monitoring VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Motor Monitoring

Parent topic:

Preprocessing VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=rms-10-or-12-cycles-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00115: RMS (10 or 12 Cycles) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `rms-10-or-12-cycles-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/rms-10-or-12-cycles-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: U, I & Freq VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the RMS voltage value or RMS current value of a complex spectrum for a 10/12-cycle block, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. Details Examples complex spectra

### RMS (10 or 12 Cycles) VI

**Owning Palette:** U, I & Freq VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the RMS voltage value or RMS current value of a complex spectrum for a 10/12-cycle block, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/RMS__10_or_12_Cycles.gif']

|  | complex spectra specifies 10/12-cycle complex spectra up to the specified harmonic. The real part of the first vector represents DC component. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the single-sided spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the single-sided amplitude spectrum phase in radians. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the single-sided spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the single-sided amplitude spectrum phase in radians. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | RMS values returns RMS value of the magnitude over the measurement time interval. Each element of this array corresponds to one element of spectra array. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

RMS (10 or 12 Cycles) Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the RMS (10 or 12 Cycles) VI:

- Events Detection (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Events Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Events Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

U, I & Freq VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=rms-half-cycle-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00116: RMS (Half Cycle) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `rms-half-cycle-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/rms-half-cycle-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: U, I & Freq VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the RMS voltage or RMS current for a half/single-cycle block, by using the zero crossings. You can use the Zero Crossing VI to calculate zero crossing. This VI refreshes the RMS values every half-cycle. You use the half/si

### RMS (Half Cycle) VI

**Owning Palette:** U, I & Freq VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the RMS voltage or RMS current for a half/single-cycle block, by using the zero crossings. You can use the Zero Crossing VI to calculate **zero crossing**. This VI refreshes the RMS values every half-cycle. You use the half/single-cycle RMS values only for detection of voltage dips, swells, and interruption events, detection of rapid voltage changes, and measurement of flickers.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/RMS__Half_Cycle.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | waveforms specifies voltage or current waveforms. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | zero crossing specifies the zero crossing index. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | one-cycle RMS returns RMS values measured over one cycle. The measurement starts at fundamental zero crossing and refreshes each half-cycle. Each element of this array corresponds to one element of input waveforms array. timestamps returns timestamps of the end of each signal half-cycle. RMS values returns the RMS values measured over one cycle. |
|  | timestamps returns timestamps of the end of each signal half-cycle. |
|  | RMS values returns the RMS values measured over one cycle. |
|  | half-cycle RMS returns RMS values measured over half cycle. The measurement starts at fundamental zero crossing and refreshes each half-cycle. Each element of this array corresponds to one element of input waveforms array. timestamps returns timestamps of the end of each signal half-cycle. RMS values returns the RMS values measured over half cycle. |
|  | timestamps returns timestamps of the end of each signal half-cycle. |
|  | RMS values returns the RMS values measured over half cycle. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

RMS (Half Cycle) Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the RMS (Half Cycle) VI:

- Events Detection (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Events Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Events Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

U, I & Freq VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=rms-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00117: RMS VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `rms-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/rms-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Measurement VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the RMS level of a voltage or current signal. Wire data to the waveform input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. Examples Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of thi

### RMS VI

**Owning Palette:** Power Measurement VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the RMS level of a voltage or current signal. Wire data to the **waveform** input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance.

Examples

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

RMS (1 Channel)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/RMS__1_Channel.gif']

|  | waveform specifies the voltage or current signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | RMS value returns the RMS level of the voltage or current signal. The RMS level has the same engineering units as waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

RMS (N Channels)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/RMS__N_Channels.gif']

|  | waveforms specifies the voltage or current signal for each channel. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | RMS values returns the RMS level of the voltage or current signal for each channel. The RMS levels have the same engineering units as waveforms. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the RMS VI:

- Basic Power Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement
- Advanced Power Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement

Parent topic:

Power Measurement VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=scaling-calibration-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00118: Scaling & Calibration VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `scaling-calibration-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/scaling-calibration-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Quality VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Scaling & Calibration VIs to scale a signal to the appropriate engineering units if the s

### Scaling & Calibration VIs

**Owning Palette:** Power Quality VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Scaling & Calibration VIs to scale a signal to the appropriate engineering units if the signal is from a transducer or to scale a secondary-side measured signal to primary-side signal if the signal is from a transformer. You also use these VIs to apply system calibration correction coefficients to the input signal before performing any power quality measurement.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Frequency Domain Calibration | Calibrates a phase spectrum by applying gains and phase delays at all frequency bins, based on actual ranges. You can use the Get Range Utilization VI to calculate range utilization. You must specify frequencies to calibrate and ranges to calibrate in ascending order. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Fundamental Phase Calibration | Calibrates the phase of a fundamental vector based on actual ranges. You can use the Get Range Utilization VI to calculate actual ranges. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Get Range Utilization | Calculates the range utilization, in percentage, of voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Range utilization is the ratio of the actual RMS value to the nominal RMS value of the voltage or current. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Scaling | Scales waveforms based on the sensitivity of the voltage/current transformer or transducer. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |
| Time Domain Calibration | Calibrates voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current by applying gains and offsets based on actual ranges. You can use the Get Range Utilization VI to calculate the range utilization. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |

Parent topic:

Power Quality VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=scaling-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00119: Scaling VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `scaling-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/scaling-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Scaling & Calibration VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Scales waveforms based on the sensitivity of the voltage/current transformer or transducer. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems

### Scaling VI

**Owning Palette:** Scaling & Calibration VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Scales waveforms based on the sensitivity of the voltage/current transformer or transducer. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Scaling (Sensitivity)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Scaling__DBL.gif']

|  | waveforms in specifies the voltage waveforms, the current waveforms, or the waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | sensitivity specifies the sensitivity of the voltage or current transformer. For example, use 200 for a transformer ratio 200:1. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | waveforms out returns voltage or current waveforms after scaling. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Scaling (Ratio)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Scaling__ratio.gif']

|  | waveforms in specifies the voltage waveforms, the current waveforms, or the waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | CT/VT ratio specifies the ratio of the current transformer sensitivity to voltage transformer sensitivity. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | waveforms out returns voltage or current waveforms after scaling. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Scaling Details

In the single-bus single-line measurement, when you specify the **wiring** input of the Initialize System VI as **3xUph + 2xI** or **3xUline + 2xI** and specify the **waveforms in** input of this VI as a current waveform, the Scaling VI calculates the waveform of Current Phase B (L2) using the scaled waveforms of Current Phase A (L1) and Current Phase C (L3).

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations

Parent topic:

Scaling & Calibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=short-term-flicker-severity-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00120: Short-Term Flicker Severity VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `short-term-flicker-severity-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/short-term-flicker-severity-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Flicker VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Aggregates instantaneous flicker values and returns the short-term flicker of a voltage waveform for a 10-minute period, according to IEC 61000-4-15:2010. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line syst

### Short-Term Flicker Severity VI

**Owning Palette:** Flicker VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Aggregates instantaneous flicker values and returns the short-term flicker of a voltage waveform for a 10-minute period, according to IEC 61000-4-15:2010.

This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/short_term_flicker_severity.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to initialize the flicker algorithm. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the VI initializes automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Pinst specifies instantaneous flicker values according to IEC 61000-4-15:2010. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the instantaneous flicker values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the instantaneous flicker values of the waveform. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | Pst returns short-term flicker values based on an observation of 10-minute period and derived from the time-at-level statistics obtained from the level classifier in Block 5 of the flickermeter specified in IEC 61000-4-15:2010. |
|  | Pst timestamp returns the end time of the 10-minute period for the short-term flicker. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | output valid? returns TRUE if the output data is valid. |

Short-Term Flicker Severity Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Short-Term Flicker Severity VI:

- Flicker Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Flicker Measurement VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Flicker Measurement VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)
- Multiple Bus Power Quality Applications VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Multiple Line Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)

Parent topic:

Flicker VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=signaling-events-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00121: Signaling Events VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `signaling-events-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/signaling-events-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Signaling VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Detects signaling voltage events in a voltage spectrum. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. You must use the D to Y Conversion VI to convert line voltages to phase voltages before perf

### Signaling Events VI

**Owning Palette:** Signaling VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Detects signaling voltage events in a voltage spectrum.

Note

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Signaling_Events.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | signaling frequency specifies the signaling frequency at which mains signaling voltage is applied. The default is 216.66. The valid minimum value for signaling frequency is 100 Hz for 50 Hz nominal frequency and 120 Hz for 60 Hz nominal frequency. The valid maximum value must be less than or equal to the maximum frequency of phase voltage spectra. Set the maximum frequency of phase voltage spectra by specifying maximum harmonic order in the Spectrum VI. |
|  | phase voltage spectra specifies 10/12-cycle voltage complex spectra in volts. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | current spectra specifies 10/12-cycle current complex spectra in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that you specify the values for Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that you specify the values for Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | signaling voltage event settings specifies parameters of the signaling voltage. detection threshold specifies threshold level for signaling voltage in percent of nominal voltage level. detection threshold must be less than 15% and greater than 0.3%. The default is 0.3. start logical 1 length specifies the length of logical 1 on the beginning of the signal in seconds. start logical 1 length must be less than 30 seconds and greater than 0.3 seconds. The default is 2.99. start logical 0 length specifies the length of logical 0 on the beginning of the signal in seconds. start logical 0 length must be less than 30 seconds and greater than 0.3 seconds. The default is 2.33. maximum event length specifies the maximum length of signaling message in seconds. maximum event length must be less than 120 seconds and greater than start logical 1 length + start logical 0 length + 1 second. The default is 64. |
|  | detection threshold specifies threshold level for signaling voltage in percent of nominal voltage level. detection threshold must be less than 15% and greater than 0.3%. The default is 0.3. |
|  | start logical 1 length specifies the length of logical 1 on the beginning of the signal in seconds. start logical 1 length must be less than 30 seconds and greater than 0.3 seconds. The default is 2.99. |
|  | start logical 0 length specifies the length of logical 0 on the beginning of the signal in seconds. start logical 0 length must be less than 30 seconds and greater than 0.3 seconds. The default is 2.33. |
|  | maximum event length specifies the maximum length of signaling message in seconds. maximum event length must be less than 120 seconds and greater than start logical 1 length + start logical 0 length + 1 second. The default is 64. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | timestamp specifies the timestamp of the end of the actual 10/12-cycle time interval. |
|  | interval length specifies a duration of 10/12-cycle time interval in seconds. |
|  | signaling voltage events returns information about the signaling voltage events. event timestamp returns the start time of the event. phase returns the phase of the event. signaling frequency returns the value of the signaling frequency. maximum voltage returns the maximum RMS voltage of the modulation signal in volts. logical 1 averaged voltage RMS returns the averaged RMS value of voltage, in volts, when the detected logical state is high. logical 0 averaged voltage RMS returns the averaged RMS value of voltage, in volts, when the detected logical state is low. maximum current returns the maximum RMS current of the modulation signal in amperes. logical 1 averaged current RMS returns the averaged RMS value of current when the detected logical state is high. logical 0 averaged current RMS returns the averaged RMS value of current when the detected logical state is low. event digital waveform returns the signaling event represented as a digital waveform. |
|  | event timestamp returns the start time of the event. |
|  | phase returns the phase of the event. |
|  | signaling frequency returns the value of the signaling frequency. |
|  | maximum voltage returns the maximum RMS voltage of the modulation signal in volts. |
|  | logical 1 averaged voltage RMS returns the averaged RMS value of voltage, in volts, when the detected logical state is high. |
|  | logical 0 averaged voltage RMS returns the averaged RMS value of voltage, in volts, when the detected logical state is low. |
|  | maximum current returns the maximum RMS current of the modulation signal in amperes. |
|  | logical 1 averaged current RMS returns the averaged RMS value of current when the detected logical state is high. |
|  | logical 0 averaged current RMS returns the averaged RMS value of current when the detected logical state is low. |
|  | event digital waveform returns the signaling event represented as a digital waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Signaling Events Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Signaling Events VI:

- Signaling Voltage Detection (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Signaling Voltage Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Signaling Voltage Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)
