Signaling VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=signaling-rms-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00122: Signaling RMS VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `signaling-rms-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/signaling-rms-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Signaling VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the RMS voltage at a specified frequency of a complex voltage spectrum, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. The specified frequency corresponds to the signaling voltage. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line

### Signaling RMS VI

**Owning Palette:** Signaling VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the RMS voltage at a specified frequency of a complex voltage spectrum, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. The specified frequency corresponds to the signaling voltage.

Note

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Signaling_RMS.gif']

|  | phase voltage spectra specifies 10/12-cycle complex voltage spectra up to a specified harmonic. The real part of the first vector represents the DC component of the input waveforms. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel. Voltage spectra are in volts. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | signaling frequency specifies the signaling frequency at which mains signaling voltage is applied. The default is 216.66. The valid minimum value for signaling frequency is 100 Hz for 50 Hz nominal frequency and 120 Hz for 60 Hz nominal frequency. The valid maximum value must be less than or equal to the maximum frequency of phase voltage spectra. Set the maximum frequency of phase voltage spectra by specifying maximum harmonic order in the Spectrum VI. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | signaling voltage RMS values returns mains signalling voltage on the supply voltage according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. Each element of this array corresponds to one element of input spectra array. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Signaling RMS Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Signaling RMS VI:

- Signaling Voltage Detection (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Signaling Voltage Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Signaling Voltage Detection VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

Signaling VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=signaling-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00123: Signaling VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `signaling-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/signaling-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Quality VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Signaling VIs to measure mains signaling voltage on the supply voltage as defined in IEC

### Signaling VIs

**Owning Palette:** Power Quality VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Signaling VIs to measure mains signaling voltage on the supply voltage as defined in IEC 61000-4-30:2008.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Signaling Events | Detects signaling voltage events in a voltage spectrum. This VI supports measuring single-bus single-line or multiple-bus single-line systems. |
| Signaling RMS | Calculates the RMS voltage at a specified frequency of a complex voltage spectrum, according to IEC 61000-4-30:2008. The specified frequency corresponds to the signaling voltage. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. |

Parent topic:

Power Quality VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=spectrum-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00124: Spectrum VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `spectrum-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/spectrum-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the fast Fourier transform (FFT) spectra from voltage signals, current signals, or signals that contain both voltage and current. This VI returns FFT spectra as complex spectra up to maximum harmonic order with a frequency r

### Spectrum VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the fast Fourier transform (FFT) spectra from voltage signals, current signals, or signals that contain both voltage and current. This VI returns FFT spectra as complex spectra up to **maximum harmonic order** with a frequency resolution around 5 Hz. The frequency resolution is the fundamental frequency divided by 10 or 12.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Spectrum.gif']

|  | waveforms specifies the voltage signals, the current signals, or the signals that contain both voltage and current. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | maximum harmonic order specifies the maximum harmonic order of the output spectra. The default is 50. If you perform further harmonic measurement for EN 50160:2007 evaluation, maximum harmonic order must be at least 40. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | spectra returns 10/12-cycle complex spectra up to the specified maximum harmonic order. Each element of this array corresponds to one element of input waveforms array. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes returns the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases returns the phase value of the spectrum. channel returns the channel of the spectrum. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes returns the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases returns the phase value of the spectrum. |
|  | channel returns the channel of the spectrum. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Spectrum Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Spectrum VI:

- Harmonics Analysis (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=speed-estimation-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00125: Speed Estimation VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `speed-estimation-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/speed-estimation-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Motor VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the motor speed. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. Details Example Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI. N Motors reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The

### Speed Estimation VI

**Owning Palette:** Motor VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the motor speed. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

Details Example

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

N Motors

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Speed_Estimation_N_Motors.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | fundamental frequency specifies the instantaneous fundamental frequency, in hertz, of waveforms. Use the corresponding upstream Electrical Power VIs, such as the Frequency VI, to measure the fundamental frequency. The value must be greater than zero. |
|  | waveforms specifies the motor waveforms that the VI uses to calculate the speed. If algorithm is 1-phase signal, specify waveforms from one phase of a motor. If algorithm is 3-phase signals, specify waveforms from three phases one motor after another. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | speed estimation settings specifies the algorithm settings that the VI uses to calculate the speed. If you change speed estimation settings while the VI runs, the new settings take effect only when reset? is TRUE. % overlap specifies the percentage of data reused by the consecutive fast Fourier transform (FFT) computations. The valid range is between 0 and 100. The default is 0. Increase the value of % overlap to make the computations more responsive when window length requires a longer time to produce new valid data. The VI typically does not have a speed value to calculate each time LabVIEW calls the VI. You can use % overlap to increase the rate at which a new speed value is computed by reusing acquired data. For example, setting % overlap to 50 causes this VI to compute the new data twice as often as a setting of 0 because each computation reuses half the data. algorithm specifies the input signal type. The default is 3-phase signals. 01-phase signalSpecifies to use a one-phase signal from a motor for speed calculation.13-phase signals (default)Specifies to use three-phase signals from a motor for speed calculation. The array size of waveforms must be a multiple of three. |
|  | % overlap specifies the percentage of data reused by the consecutive fast Fourier transform (FFT) computations. The valid range is between 0 and 100. The default is 0. Increase the value of % overlap to make the computations more responsive when window length requires a longer time to produce new valid data. The VI typically does not have a speed value to calculate each time LabVIEW calls the VI. You can use % overlap to increase the rate at which a new speed value is computed by reusing acquired data. For example, setting % overlap to 50 causes this VI to compute the new data twice as often as a setting of 0 because each computation reuses half the data. |
|  | algorithm specifies the input signal type. The default is 3-phase signals. 01-phase signalSpecifies to use a one-phase signal from a motor for speed calculation.13-phase signals (default)Specifies to use three-phase signals from a motor for speed calculation. The array size of waveforms must be a multiple of three. |
| 0 | 1-phase signalSpecifies to use a one-phase signal from a motor for speed calculation. |
| 1 | 3-phase signals (default)Specifies to use three-phase signals from a motor for speed calculation. The array size of waveforms must be a multiple of three. |
|  | nameplate information specifies the nameplate information that the VI uses to calculate the speed. If you change nameplate information while the VI runs, the changes take effect only when reset? is TRUE. nominal frequency specifies the nominal frequency of waveforms. The default is 50 Hz. 050 Hz (default)160 Hz speed specifies the speed information for each motor based on the motor nameplate. The speed array size must be equal to the motor number. synchronous speed specifies the speed, in revolutions per minute (RPM), under no load. full load speed specifies the speed, in RPM, under full load on the nameplate. |
|  | nominal frequency specifies the nominal frequency of waveforms. The default is 50 Hz. 050 Hz (default)160 Hz |
| 0 | 50 Hz (default) |
| 1 | 60 Hz |
|  | speed specifies the speed information for each motor based on the motor nameplate. The speed array size must be equal to the motor number. synchronous speed specifies the speed, in revolutions per minute (RPM), under no load. full load speed specifies the speed, in RPM, under full load on the nameplate. |
|  | synchronous speed specifies the speed, in revolutions per minute (RPM), under no load. |
|  | full load speed specifies the speed, in RPM, under full load on the nameplate. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | window length specifies the time period for speed calculation. The default is 60 seconds. Increase window length to produce the speed calculation results in higher precision according to your needs. If you change window length while the VI runs, the new length takes effect only when reset? is TRUE. |
|  | new data available? returns TRUE when this VI finishes updating data. |
|  | speed returns the speed, in RPM, for multiple motors. |
|  | slip returns the slip value for multiple motors. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

1 Motor with Initial Conditions

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Speed_Estimation_1_Motor.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | fundamental frequency specifies the instantaneous fundamental frequency, in hertz, of waveforms. Use the corresponding upstream Electrical Power VIs, such as the Frequency VI, to measure the fundamental frequency. The value must be greater than zero. |
|  | waveforms specifies the motor waveforms that the VI uses to calculate the speed. If algorithm is 1-phase signal, specify waveforms from one phase of a motor. If algorithm is 3-phase signals, specify waveforms from three phases one motor after another. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | speed estimation settings specifies the algorithm settings that the VI uses to calculate the speed. If you change speed estimation settings while the VI runs, the new settings take effect only when reset? is TRUE. % overlap specifies the percentage of data reused by the consecutive fast Fourier transform (FFT) computations. The valid range is between 0 and 100. The default is 0. Increase the value of % overlap to make the computations more responsive when window length requires a longer time to produce new valid data. The VI typically does not have a speed value to calculate each time LabVIEW calls the VI. You can use % overlap to increase the rate at which a new speed value is computed by reusing acquired data. For example, setting % overlap to 50 causes this VI to compute the new data twice as often as a setting of 0 because each computation reuses half the data. algorithm specifies the input signal type. This VI uses different speed estimation algorithms for different signal types. The default is 3-phase signals. 01-phase signalSpecifies to use a one-phase signal from a motor for speed estimation.13-phase signals(default) Specifies to use three-phase signals from a motor for speed calculation. The array size of waveforms must be a multiple of three. |
|  | % overlap specifies the percentage of data reused by the consecutive fast Fourier transform (FFT) computations. The valid range is between 0 and 100. The default is 0. Increase the value of % overlap to make the computations more responsive when window length requires a longer time to produce new valid data. The VI typically does not have a speed value to calculate each time LabVIEW calls the VI. You can use % overlap to increase the rate at which a new speed value is computed by reusing acquired data. For example, setting % overlap to 50 causes this VI to compute the new data twice as often as a setting of 0 because each computation reuses half the data. |
|  | algorithm specifies the input signal type. This VI uses different speed estimation algorithms for different signal types. The default is 3-phase signals. 01-phase signalSpecifies to use a one-phase signal from a motor for speed estimation.13-phase signals(default) Specifies to use three-phase signals from a motor for speed calculation. The array size of waveforms must be a multiple of three. |
| 0 | 1-phase signalSpecifies to use a one-phase signal from a motor for speed estimation. |
| 1 | 3-phase signals(default) Specifies to use three-phase signals from a motor for speed calculation. The array size of waveforms must be a multiple of three. |
|  | initial conditions specifies initial internal VI conditions to guarantee consecutive calculation correctness. If you use this VI for the first time or reset? is TRUE, this VI automatically creates the initial internal conditions. Otherwise, wire the most recent final conditions to the subsequent initial conditions to inherit the conditions. |
|  | nameplate information specifies the nameplate information that the VI uses to calculate the speed. If you change nameplate information while the VI runs, the changes take effect only when reset? is TRUE. nominal frequency specifies the nominal frequency of waveforms. The default is 50 Hz. 050 Hz (default)160 Hz speed specifies the speed information for the motor based on the motor nameplate. synchronous speed specifies the speed, in revolutions per minute (RPM), under no load. full load speed specifies the speed, in RPM, under full load on the nameplate. |
|  | nominal frequency specifies the nominal frequency of waveforms. The default is 50 Hz. 050 Hz (default)160 Hz |
| 0 | 50 Hz (default) |
| 1 | 60 Hz |
|  | speed specifies the speed information for the motor based on the motor nameplate. synchronous speed specifies the speed, in revolutions per minute (RPM), under no load. full load speed specifies the speed, in RPM, under full load on the nameplate. |
|  | synchronous speed specifies the speed, in revolutions per minute (RPM), under no load. |
|  | full load speed specifies the speed, in RPM, under full load on the nameplate. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | window length specifies the time period for speed calculation. The default is 60 seconds. Increase window length to produce the speed calculation results in higher precision according to your needs. If you change window length while the VI runs, the new length takes effect only when reset? is TRUE. |
|  | new data available? returns TRUE when this VI finishes updating data. |
|  | speed returns the speed, in RPM, for the motor. |
|  | slip returns the slip value for the motor. |
|  | final conditions returns the final internal VI conditions after the internal calculation of this VI. Use final conditions as initial conditions of the subsequent iteration except that you want to customize the conditions according to your needs. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Speed Estimation Details

Because the time interval to produce the speed calculation results depends on the values of **%overlap** and **window length**, to specify an appropriate time interval for your applications, use the following formula to specify **%overlap** and **window length**.

Time Interval = Window Length · (1 - Overlap / 100)

| where Time Interval is the interval, in seconds, to produce the speed calculation results; |
| --- |
| Window Length is the value of window length; |
| Overlap is the value of % overlap. |

**Related Information**
Power Transmission


Example

Refer to the Motor Monitoring.lvproj in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Motor Monitoring directory for an example of using the Speed Estimation VI.

Parent topic:

Motor VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=subgroup-spectrum-lines-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00126: Subgroup Spectrum Lines VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `subgroup-spectrum-lines-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/subgroup-spectrum-lines-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Performs subgrouping of 10/12-cycle step spectra to harmonic and interharmonic spectra according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Use the Complex To Polar function to convert the results of this VI to magnitude and phase spectra. Use the Complex

### Subgroup Spectrum Lines VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Performs subgrouping of 10/12-cycle step spectra to harmonic and interharmonic spectra according to IEC 61000-4-7:2009. Use the Complex To Polar function to convert the results of this VI to magnitude and phase spectra. Use the Complex To Re/Im function to convert the results of this VI to real and imaginary spectra.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Subgroup_Spectrum_Lines.gif']

|  | spectra specifies 10/12-cycle complex spectra. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | harmonic subgroups complex spectra returns complex spectra of harmonic subgroups with fundamental frequency step. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. vectors returns harmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | vectors returns harmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
|  | interharmonic subgroups complex spectra returns complex spectra of interharmonic subgroups with fundamental frequency step. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. vectors returns interharmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | vectors returns interharmonic subgroup represented as a vector. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Subgroup Spectrum Lines Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Subgroup Spectrum Lines VI:

- Harmonics Analysis (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

## TOPIC 00127: Symmetrical Components (1 Cycle) VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `symmetrical-components-1-cycle-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/symmetrical-components-1-cycle-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Unbalance VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the fundamental voltage, current, and power symmetrical components for a single-cycle block. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. Details Examples one-cycle fundamental voltage vectors specifies

### Symmetrical Components (1 Cycle) VI

**Owning Palette:** Unbalance VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the fundamental voltage, current, and power symmetrical components for a single-cycle block.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Symmetrical_Components__1_cycle.gif']

|  | one-cycle fundamental voltage vectors specifies one-cycle fundamental vectors of voltage channels. Each array element represents one measurement channel and contains one-cycle fundamental vectors from 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. In the single-bus single-line measurement, this input contains three array elements, which represent three channels of phase voltage spectra. In the single-bus multiple-line or the multiple-bus measurement, this input contains a multiple of three array elements one three-phase power system after another. timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. vectors specifies the one-cycle fundamental vector value. channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
| --- | --- |
|  | timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. |
|  | vectors specifies the one-cycle fundamental vector value. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
|  | one-cycle fundamental current vectors specifies one-cycle fundamental vectors of current channels. Each array element represents one measurement channel and contains one-cycle fundamental vectors from 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. The requirements for different line and bus configurations are the same as those for one-cycle fundamental voltage vectors. timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. vectors specifies the one-cycle fundamental vector value. channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
|  | timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. |
|  | vectors specifies the one-cycle fundamental vector value. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | one-cycle fundamental symmetrical components returns the fundamental voltage, current, and power symmetrical components for the signal cycle one three-phase power system after another. timestamps returns the timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. voltage returns the fundamental voltage symmetrical component. Array elements have the following order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component current returns the fundamental current symmetrical component. Array elements have the following order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component active power returns the fundamental active power symmetrical component. Array elements have the following order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component |
|  | timestamps returns the timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. |
|  | voltage returns the fundamental voltage symmetrical component. Array elements have the following order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component |
| 0 | Zero sequence symmetrical component |
| 1 | Positive sequence symmetrical component |
| 2 | Negative sequence symmetrical component |
|  | current returns the fundamental current symmetrical component. Array elements have the following order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component |
| 0 | Zero sequence symmetrical component |
| 1 | Positive sequence symmetrical component |
| 2 | Negative sequence symmetrical component |
|  | active power returns the fundamental active power symmetrical component. Array elements have the following order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component |
| 0 | Zero sequence symmetrical component |
| 1 | Positive sequence symmetrical component |
| 2 | Negative sequence symmetrical component |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Symmetrical Components (1 Cycle) Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations
Power Transmission


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Symmetrical Components (1 Cycle) VI:

- Unbalance Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Unbalance Measurement VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Unbalance Measurement VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=symmetrical-components-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00128: Symmetrical Components VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `symmetrical-components-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/symmetrical-components-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Power Measurement VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the zero, positive, and negative sequence components of a three-phase electrical power system. You can use the zero, positive, and negative sequence components to calculate voltage or current unbalance of the three-p

### Symmetrical Components VI

**Owning Palette:** Power Measurement VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the zero, positive, and negative sequence components of a three-phase electrical power system.

You can use the zero, positive, and negative sequence components to calculate voltage or current unbalance of the three-phase electrical power system. Use the Complex To Polar function to convert the results of this VI to magnitude and phase components. Use the Complex To Re/Im function to convert the results of this VI to real and imaginary components.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Basic_Symmetrical_Components.gif']

|  | 3-phase phasors specifies the vector of voltage or current signals from a three-phase electrical power system. The three elements of 3-phase phasors have a phase separation of one-third cycle, or 120 degrees. If you wire the output data of the Fundamental Vector VI to 3-phase phasors, the phase of the first channel rotates to zero. Ideal phasors are graphically represented as magnitudes at angles of 0, -120, and 120 degrees in the clockwise direction. |
| --- | --- |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | zero sequence returns the zero sequence components of the three-phase electrical power system. |
|  | positive sequence returns the positive sequence components of the three-phase electrical power system. |
|  | negative sequence returns the negative sequence components of the three-phase electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Basic Power Measurement (DAQmx) VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement directory for an example of using the Symmetrical Components VI.

Parent topic:

Power Measurement VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=symmetrical-components-vi2.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00129: Symmetrical Components VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `symmetrical-components-vi2.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/symmetrical-components-vi2.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Unbalance VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates voltage, current, and power symmetrical components for a 10-cycle or 12-cycle block. Use the results of this VI to analyze the voltage or current unbalance of a three-phase electrical power system. This VI supports measuring

### Symmetrical Components VI

**Owning Palette:** Unbalance VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates voltage, current, and power symmetrical components for a 10-cycle or 12-cycle block. Use the results of this VI to analyze the voltage or current unbalance of a three-phase electrical power system.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Symmetrical_Components.gif']

|  | voltage spectra specifies 10/12-cycle voltage complex spectra in volts. The fundamental vectors of the three channels of phase voltage spectra must be in the clockwise direction. For example, in a balanced three-phase electrical power system, the fundamental vectors are graphically represented as magnitudes at angles of 0, -120, and 120 degrees in the clockwise direction. In the single-bus single-line measurement, this input contains three array elements, which represent three channels of phase voltage spectra. In the single-bus multiple-line or the multiple-bus measurement, this input contains a multiple of three array elements one three-phase power system after another. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that you specify the values for Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that you specify the values for Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | current spectra specifies 10/12-cycle current complex spectra in amperes. The requirements for different line and bus configurations are the same as those for voltage spectra. The fundamental vectors of the three channels of current spectra must be in the clockwise direction. For example, in a balanced three-phase electrical power system, the fundamental vectors are graphically represented as magnitudes at angles of 0, -120, and 120 degrees in the clockwise direction. The requirements for different line and bus configurations are the same as those for voltage spectra. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that you specify the values for Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that you specify the values for Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | symmetrical components returns voltage and current symmetrical components one three-phase power system after another. All the symmetrical components have the following array order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component voltage returns the voltage symmetrical components. fundamental voltage returns the fundamental voltage symmetrical components. current returns the current symmetrical components. fundamental current returns the fundamental current symmetrical components. |
| 0 | Zero sequence symmetrical component |
| 1 | Positive sequence symmetrical component |
| 2 | Negative sequence symmetrical component |
|  | voltage returns the voltage symmetrical components. |
|  | fundamental voltage returns the fundamental voltage symmetrical components. |
|  | current returns the current symmetrical components. |
|  | fundamental current returns the fundamental current symmetrical components. |
|  | power symmetrical components returns the power symmetrical components one three-phase power system after another. All the power symmetrical components have the following array order. 0Zero sequence symmetrical component1Positive sequence symmetrical component2Negative sequence symmetrical component active power returns the active power symmetrical components. fundamental active power returns the fundamental active power symmetrical components. fundamental reactive power returns the fundamental reactive power symmetrical components. |
| 0 | Zero sequence symmetrical component |
| 1 | Positive sequence symmetrical component |
| 2 | Negative sequence symmetrical component |
|  | active power returns the active power symmetrical components. |
|  | fundamental active power returns the fundamental active power symmetrical components. |
|  | fundamental reactive power returns the fundamental reactive power symmetrical components. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Symmetrical Components Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations
Power Transmission


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Symmetrical Components VI:

- Unbalance Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Unbalance Measurement VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Unbalance Measurement VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)
- Single Bus Multiple Line Power Quality Applications VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Multiple Line Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)

Parent topic:

Unbalance VIs

## TOPIC 00130: Synchrophasor VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `synchrophasor-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/synchrophasor-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Electrical Power VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Synchrophasor VIs to perform synchrophasor measurement and to build and parse synchrop

### Synchrophasor VIs

**Owning Palette:** Electrical Power VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Synchrophasor VIs to perform synchrophasor measurement and to build and parse synchrophasor message frames. The VIs for measuring synchrophasors are in compliance with IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. The C37.118 Frame VIs build and parse synchrophasor message frames for real-time communication of synchrophasor measurement data between phasor measurement units (PMUs) and phasor data concentrators (PDCs) as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011.

IEEE Std C37.118.1a-2014 is an amendment to IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. The VIs for measuring synchrophasors are also in compliance with IEEE Std C37.118.1a-2014.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Close System | Deallocates the resources that system info in reserves. |
| Initialize System (Std 2011) | Sets the configuration for a phasor measurement unit (PMU) system as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011 and IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |
| Phasor Correction | Scales the magnitudes of phasors based on the sensitivity of the voltage/current transformer or transducer and compensates the timestamps based on the delays that the data acquisition module, the voltage/current transformer, or the voltage/current transducer introduces. |
| Phasor Measurement (Std 2011) | Calculates the phasors, the frequency, and the rate of change of frequency at the reporting timestamp as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. |
| Trigger Detector | Detects triggers and returns the bitmapped flags of trigger reason in the STAT field of the data frame as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| C37.118 Frame VIs | Use the C37.118 Frame VIs to build and parse synchrophasor message frames for real-time communication of synchrophasor measurement data between phasor measurement units (PMUs) and phasor data concentrators (PDCs) as defined in IEEE Std C37.118.2-2011. |

Parent topic:

Electrical Power VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=synchrophasor-vis2.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00131: Synchrophasor VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `synchrophasor-vis2.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/synchrophasor-vis2.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: FPGA VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Synchrophasor VIs to perform synchrophasor measurement on FPGA targets. The Synchrophasor VIs are

### Synchrophasor VIs

**Owning Palette:** FPGA VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Synchrophasor VIs to perform synchrophasor measurement on FPGA targets. The Synchrophasor VIs are in compliance with IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011.

IEEE Std C37.118.1a-2014 is an amendment to IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. The Synchrophasor VIs are also in compliance with IEEE Std C37.118.1a-2014.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| FPGA Phasor Measurement for M Class | Calculates the instantaneous magnitude, phase, and frequency values of the fundamental frequency component as defined in the M class of performance in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. This VI resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals. The input signals must have a sample rate of 50 kHz. The output rate is 24 samples per cycle. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use. |
| FPGA Phasor Measurement for P Class | Calculates the instantaneous magnitude, phase, and frequency values of the fundamental frequency component as defined in the P class of performance in IEEE Std C37.118.1-2011. This VI resamples even-time-spaced voltage and current signals into even-angle-spaced signals. The input signals must have a sample rate of 50 kHz. The output rate is 25 samples per cycle. You must manually select the polymorphic instance to use. |
| FPGA Sampling Rate Ratio | Calculates the ratio of the nominal sample rate to the actual sample rate of the data acquisition module as the correction parameters for frequency calculation. |

Parent topic:

FPGA VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=thd-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00132: THD VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `thd-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/thd-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the total harmonic distortion (THD), harmonic distortion factors, and total values from voltage harmonic spectra, current harmonic spectra, or harmonic spectra that contain both voltage and current. Details Example harmonic

### THD VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the total harmonic distortion (THD), harmonic distortion factors, and total values from voltage harmonic spectra, current harmonic spectra, or harmonic spectra that contain both voltage and current.

Details Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/PM_THD.gif']

|  | harmonic spectra specifies the complex harmonic spectra. order 0 specifies the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. delta order specifies the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum specifies the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
| --- | --- |
|  | order 0 specifies the starting order of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | delta order specifies the order interval of the harmonic spectrum. |
|  | complex spectrum specifies the complex harmonic spectrum. complex spectrum uses the RMS level for the magnitude of the complex harmonic spectrum. |
|  | distortion factor denominator specifies the denominator of the equation this VI uses to calculate the harmonic distortion factor. 0Fundamental value (default)Uses the magnitude value of the fundamental component of the spectrum as the denominator.1Total valueUses the total value of the spectrum as the denominator. |
| 0 | Fundamental value (default)Uses the magnitude value of the fundamental component of the spectrum as the denominator. |
| 1 | Total valueUses the total value of the spectrum as the denominator. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | THD returns the THD values, in percentages, that this VI calculates from the harmonic spectra. |
|  | harmonic distortion factors returns the harmonic distortion factor, in percentages, of each harmonic. The number of rows corresponds to the number of channels in the harmonic spectra. The number of columns corresponds to maximum harmonic order in the Harmonic Spectrum VI. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
|  | total values returns the total values, in volts or amperes, that this VI calculates from the harmonic spectra. |

THD Details

This VI uses the following equation to calculate THD:

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_harmonic_THD.gif']

| where | k is the harmonic order of the complex spectrum |
| --- | --- |
|  | U(k) is the magnitude value of the kth order harmonic of the complex spectrum |
|  | max is maximum harmonic order in the Harmonic Spectrum VI |
|  | distortion factor denominator is distortion factor denominator in this VI |

This VI uses the following equation to calculate the harmonic distortion factor:

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_distortion_factor.gif']

| where | k is the harmonic order of the complex spectrum |
| --- | --- |
|  | U(k) is the magnitude value of the kth order harmonic of the complex spectrum |
|  | distortion factor denominator is distortion factor denominator in this VI |

This VI uses the following equation to calculate the total value:

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/eq_total_value.gif']

| where | k is the harmonic order of the complex spectrum |
| --- | --- |
|  | U(k) is the magnitude value of the kth order harmonic of the complex spectrum |
|  | max is maximum harmonic order in the Harmonic Spectrum VI |

Example

Refer to the Advanced Power Measurement - Harmonics (DAQmx) VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement directory for an example of using the THD VI.

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=thd-vi2.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00133: THD VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `thd-vi2.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/thd-vi2.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Harmonics VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the total harmonic distortion (THD) of voltage spectra, harmonic spectra, or spectra that contain both voltage and current as defined in IEC 61000-4-7:2009. This VI calculates the THD for all harmonics up to the 40th order a

### THD VI

**Owning Palette:** Harmonics VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the total harmonic distortion (THD) of voltage spectra, harmonic spectra, or spectra that contain both voltage and current as defined in IEC 61000-4-7:2009. This VI calculates the THD for all harmonics up to the 40th order as defined in EN 50160:2007.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/THD.gif']

|  | spectra specifies 10/12-cycle complex spectra up to the specified harmonic. The real part of the first vector represents DC component. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel. Voltage spectra are in volts and current spectra are in amperes. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the voltage or current spectra. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | THD returns the THD values, in percentages, calculated from the spectra. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

THD Details

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the THD VI:

- Harmonics Analysis (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (DAQmx)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (Delta-Sigma)
- Harmonics Analysis VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Applications (cRIO) (SAR)
- Advanced Motor Monitoring VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Motor Monitoring

Parent topic:

Harmonics VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=three-phase-power-measurement-vis.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00134: Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `three-phase-power-measurement-vis.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/three-phase-power-measurement-vis.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: EV Power Test VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target. Use the Three Phase Power Measurement VIs to perform power measurements on three-phase, nonsinuso

### Three Phase Power Measurement VIs

**Owning Palette:** EV Power Test VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit. This topic might not match its corresponding palette in LabVIEW depending on your operating system, licensed product(s), and target.

Use the Three Phase Power Measurement VIs to perform power measurements on three-phase, nonsinusoidal signals as defined in IEEE Std 1459-2010, IEEE Standard Definitions for the Measurement of Electric Power Quantities Under Sinusoidal, Nonsinusoidal, Balanced, or Unbalanced Conditions.

| Palette Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Cross Power Spectrum | Measures the cross power spectrum of a time-domain voltage and current signal. Wire data to the voltage waveform input or the current waveform input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| Power | Calculates the power values from a nonsinusoidal voltage and current signal as defined in IEEE Std 1459-2010. Wire data to the voltage waveform input or the current waveform input to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| Power Components | Calculates the power components for nonsinusoidal signals as defined in IEEE Std 1459-2010. Wire data to the voltage waveform input or the current waveform inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| Harmonic Power | Measures the harmonic real powers and harmonic reactive powers from cross power spectrum or cross power spectra inputs. For accurate measurements of harmonic power, this VI requires that all harmonics be resolvable within the spectrum. This requires at least four cycles of the electrical signal in the original waveform segment. Harmonic components may indicate design and production characteristics that lead to decreased power conversion efficiency. Wire data to cross power spectrum or cross power spectra to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| Harmonic Analysis | Measures the DC, fundamental, and harmonic components of an input waveform that contains an integer number of cycles of the fundamental frequency component. The fundamental frequency is used to calculate harmonic component levels and phases. Harmonic components may indicate design and production characteristics that lead to decreased power conversion efficiency. Wire data to the waveform and electrical frequency or electrical angle inputs to determine the polymorphic instance to use or manually select the instance. |
| Real and Reactive Power in Band | Measures the power in the specified frequency band. Real power is calculated from the sum of in-phase power, and reactive power is calculated from the sum of quadrature (imaginary) power. Use this VI to calculate the power in band from cross power spectrum and cross power spectra. This VI measures power accurately when processing the integer and noninteger cycles of the fundamental frequency component. |

| Subpalette | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Efficiency VIs | Use the Efficiency VIs to calculate the power efficiency of motors and inverters. |
| Transform VIs | Use the Transform VIs to convert nonsinusoidal three-phase signals from one reference frame to another reference frame and to convert between line voltages and phase voltages and between line currents and phase-to-phase currents. |
| Utility VIs | Use these Utility VIs to share waveform data between sections of your application, to parse signals into cycle-based segments, and to build data clusters for three-phase power measurements. |

Parent topic:

EV Power Test VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=time-domain-calibration-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00135: Time Domain Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `time-domain-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/time-domain-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Scaling & Calibration VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calibrates voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current by applying gains and offsets based on actual ranges. You can use the Get Range Utilization VI to calculate the range utili

### Time Domain Calibration VI

**Owning Palette:** Scaling & Calibration VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calibrates voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current by applying **gains** and **offsets** based on **actual ranges**. You can use the Get Range Utilization VI to calculate the range utilization.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details Examples

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Time_Domain_Calibration.gif']

|  | ranges to calibrate specifies piecewise calibration points in range percentage. You must specify calibration points in ascending order, for example, 25, 50, 75, and 100. |
| --- | --- |
|  | waveforms in specifies the voltage waveforms, the current waveforms, or the waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | gains specifies gain characteristics for ranges that you specify. The first dimension represents channels and the second dimension matches range percentages. |
|  | offsets specifies the DC offsets. offsets is in volts for voltage channels and in amperes for current channels. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | actual ranges specifies actual range utilization in percentages, which is the waveform amplitude relative to RMS full range. Each element of actual ranges corresponds to one element of waveforms in. This VI interpolates the gains and offsets of a specific actual ranges using the closest two ranges to calibrate. |
|  | waveforms out returns voltage or current waveforms after calibration. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Time Domain Calibration Details

In the single-bus single-line measurement, when you specify the **wiring** input of the Initialize System VI as **3xUph + 2xI** or **3xUline + 2xI** and specify the **waveforms in** input of this VI as a current waveform, the Time Domain Calibration VI calculates the waveform of Current Phase B (L2) using the scaled waveforms of Current Phase A (L1) and Current Phase C (L3).

**Related Information**
Line and Bus Configurations


Examples

Refer to the following VIs for examples of using the Time Domain Calibration VI:

- Calibration for Power Quality Measurement (Host) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Calibration
- Calibration for Power Quality Measurement (DAQmx) VI: labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Quality\Calibration (DAQmx)

Parent topic:

Scaling & Calibration VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=to-integral-cycles-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00136: To Integral Cycles VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `to-integral-cycles-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/to-integral-cycles-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Advanced VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Divides the voltage and current signals into integral-cycle data for power measurement, according to the trigger times that you specify. If you set the minimum fundamental frequency too low or set the transition band too narrow in the R

### To Integral Cycles VI

**Owning Palette:** Advanced VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Divides the voltage and current signals into integral-cycle data for power measurement, according to the trigger times that you specify.

If you set the minimum fundamental frequency too low or set the transition band too narrow in the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI, this VI requires a large amount of memory to process the voltage and current signals.

Example

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/To_Integral_Cycles.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset building integral-cycle data. The default is FALSE. When you call this VI for the first time, the process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | data in specifies the voltage or current signal. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | trigger information specifies the trigger time and the number of triggers in each cycle that this VI uses to divide the voltage or current signal into integral cycles. trigger times specifies the time, in seconds, when triggers occur. trigger times must be synchronous with data in. You can use zero crossing times that you calculate with the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI as trigger times for this VI. You also can use the pulse times that a tachometer generates as trigger times for this VI. triggers per cycle specifies the number of triggers in one cycle of the voltage and current signals. The default is 2. |
|  | trigger times specifies the time, in seconds, when triggers occur. trigger times must be synchronous with data in. You can use zero crossing times that you calculate with the Reference Signal Processing (Zero Crossing) VI as trigger times for this VI. You also can use the pulse times that a tachometer generates as trigger times for this VI. |
|  | triggers per cycle specifies the number of triggers in one cycle of the voltage and current signals. The default is 2. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | integral-cycle data returns the integral-cycle data for electrical power measurement. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Example

Refer to the Advanced Power Measurement (DAQmx) VI in the labview\examples\Electrical Power\Power Measurement directory for an example of using the To Integral Cycles VI.

Parent topic:

Advanced VIs
