<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=y-to-d-conversion-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00149: Y to D Conversion VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `y-to-d-conversion-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/y-to-d-conversion-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Utility VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Converts phase voltages to line voltages. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems. Details Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this V

### Y to D Conversion VI

**Owning Palette:** Utility VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Converts phase voltages to line voltages. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details

Use the pull-down menu to select an instance of this VI.

Y to D Conversion (Waveform)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Y_to_D__DBL_WFM__Conversion.gif']

|  | phase voltage waveforms specifies voltage waveforms, in volts, one three-phase system after another. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Ensure that you specify the waveforms of Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C for each three-phase system. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
| --- | --- |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | line voltage waveforms returns double-precision line voltage waveforms, in volts, one three-phase system after another. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Y to D Conversion (Spectra)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Y_to_D__Spectra__Conversion.gif']

|  | phase voltage spectra specifies 10/12-cycle complex voltage spectra, in volts, one three-phase system after another. The spectrum value is up to a harmonic order that you specify in maximum harmonic order in the Spectrum VI. The real part of the first vector represents the DC component of the input waveforms. Each element of this array corresponds to one channel. Ensure that you specify the vectors of Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C for each three-phase system. f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
| --- | --- |
|  | f0 specifies the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df specifies the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes specifies the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases specifies the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | line voltage spectra returns the line voltage spectra after conversion one three-phase system after another. f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. magnitudes returns the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. phases returns the phase of the spectrum. channel returns the channel of the line voltage vector. |
|  | f0 returns the start frequency of the spectrum. |
|  | df returns the frequency resolution of the spectrum. |
|  | magnitudes returns the magnitude of the spectrum in RMS level. |
|  | phases returns the phase of the spectrum. |
|  | channel returns the channel of the line voltage vector. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Y to D Conversion (Vectors)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Y_to_D__Vectors__Conversion.gif']

|  | one-cycle fundamental phase voltage vectors specifies one-cycle fundamental phase voltage vectors of current channels one three-phase system after another. Each array element represents one measurement channel and contains one-cycle fundamental vectors from 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Ensure that you specify the vectors of Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C for each three-phase system. timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. vectors specifies the one-cycle fundamental vector value. channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
| --- | --- |
|  | timestamps specifies timestamp of the end of each signal cycle. |
|  | vectors specifies the one-cycle fundamental vector value. |
|  | channel specifies the channel of the fundamental vector. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. |
|  | one-cycle fundamental line voltage vectors returns fundamental line voltage vectors for each cycle one three-phase system after another. Each array element represents one measurement channel and contains one-cycle fundamental vectors from 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. timestamps returns timestamps of the end of each signal cycle. vectors returns values of fundamental vectors. channel returns the channel information of the fundamental vector. |
|  | timestamps returns timestamps of the end of each signal cycle. |
|  | vectors returns values of fundamental vectors. |
|  | channel returns the channel information of the fundamental vector. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |

Y to D Conversion Details

In the multiple-line measurement, ensure that the input signals of this VI are from the phase voltage channels based on the physical connection type. If the input signals are from line voltage channels, this VI returns incorrect values.

**Related Information**Conversion
Line and Bus Configurations
Power TransmissionWye Connections

Utility VIs

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref path=zero-crossing-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00150: Zero Crossing VI

- bundle_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- source_path: `zero-crossing-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/zero-crossing-vi.html
- document_id: `labview-electrical-power-toolkit-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Owning Palette: Utility VIs Requires: Electrical Power Toolkit Calculates the zero crossing indexes for voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Zero crossing is the instantaneous point at which the voltage or current turns from positive to negative o

### Zero Crossing VI

**Owning Palette:** Utility VIs

**Requires:** Electrical Power Toolkit

Calculates the zero crossing indexes for voltage waveforms, current waveforms, or waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Zero crossing is the instantaneous point at which the voltage or current turns from positive to negative or from negative to positive.

This VI supports measuring single-line and multiple-line systems.

Details

[IMAGE alt='image' src='images/Zero_Crossing.gif']

|  | reset? specifies whether to reset the calculation process. The default is FALSE. When you run this VI for the first time, the calculation process resets automatically. |
| --- | --- |
|  | waveforms in specifies the voltage waveforms, the current waveforms, or the waveforms that contain both voltage and current. Each array element represents one phase and contains samples representing 10-cycle time interval for a 50 Hz power system or 12-cycle time interval for a 60 Hz power system. Voltage waveforms are in volts and current waveforms are in amperes. t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 specifies the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt specifies the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y specifies the data values of the waveform. |
|  | system info in specifies the basic configuration of the electrical power system. Use the Initialize System VI to generate the basic configuration information. |
|  | waveforms out returns voltage or current waveforms after zero crossing calculation. t0 returns the start time of the waveform. dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | t0 returns the start time of the waveform. |
|  | dt returns the time interval, in seconds, between data points in the waveform. |
|  | Y returns the data values of the waveform. |
|  | zero crossings returns zero crossing indexes. |
|  | samples since the last cross returns the number of samples since the last cross. |
|  | system info out returns information about the basic configuration of the electrical power system. |
|  | error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
